title: kitty image preview describe path should read settings.json and default to github-copilot/claude-opus-4.8
description:
Operator request (Harry, 2026-06-07): the kitty image preview describe path (kitty_image_preview_* tools with describe:true) should expose the vision/describe model via settings.json like tendril-share already does, and default to github-copilot/claude-opus-4.8 (reusing pi's Copilot auth via ctx.modelRegistry.getApiKeyAndHeaders — no separate JWT/auth.json hook needed).

CURRENT STATE (code-grounded):
- kitty-image-preview resolveVisionModel (kitty-image-preview.js ~L734) resolves model from: per-call describeModel param -> KITTY_IMAGE_PREVIEW_DESCRIBE_MODEL env -> DEFAULT_DESCRIBE_MODEL constant. It does NOT read settings.json.
- DEFAULT_DESCRIBE_MODEL = "litellm-anthropic/claude-opus-4-7" (kitty-image-preview/constants.js:15).
- By contrast tendril-share.js already reads settings.json (tendril.describeModel / tendrilShare.describeModel / agentUtils.tendril.describeModel / agentUtils.tendrilShare.describeModel) + TENDRIL_SHARE_DESCRIBE_MODEL env, default github-copilot/claude-opus-4.8 with a fallback chain.
- Both paths make a separate complete() call (not a pi turn) but resolve model+auth through pi's registry, so github-copilot models reuse pi's baked Copilot token automatically.

SCOPE:
1. Add a settings reader for kitty-image-preview describe model: kittyImagePreview.describeModel and agentUtils.kittyImagePreview.describeModel (mirror tendril-share's configuredDescribeModelFromSettings shape).
2. resolveVisionModel order: per-call param -> KITTY_IMAGE_PREVIEW_DESCRIBE_MODEL env -> settings.json keys -> default.
3. Change DEFAULT_DESCRIBE_MODEL to github-copilot/claude-opus-4.8; add a small fallback chain (mirror tendril-share) so nodes without 4.8 registered degrade gracefully instead of hard-throwing "not registered".
4. Update schema.js describeModel description text to reflect the new default + settings source.
5. Unit tests: model resolution honors settings.json and env/param precedence; default is copilot opus 4.8; fallback engages when unregistered.

ACCEPTANCE: kitty_image_preview describe uses github-copilot/claude-opus-4.8 by default and can be overridden via settings.json (kittyImagePreview.describeModel); auth path unchanged (pi registry). Operator settings (~/collective/standalone/pi/settings.json) will be pinned explicitly as a follow-up step.
creator: ms-mac:agent-utils:ms-mac-agent-utils-agnt-dev-0

title: Harden kitty image preview ID namespace and passthrough diagnostics
description:
Harry observed that kitty-image-preview static Unicode previews show raw placeholder glyphs/blank pixels inside caco/tmux, and pi-graphics primitive styles also appear absent. Implement the in-scope agent-utils hardening:

- Reuse the pi-graphics scoped ID namespace for kitty-image-preview static/Unicode IDs instead of static/global preview placement IDs.
- Prefer placeholder placement IDs allocated through the pi-graphics/stable placeholder placement path for Unicode mode.
- Add status/debug details that expose resolved transport, passthrough, placement mode, SSH/tmux detection, and current image/placement IDs so caco-host passthrough failures can be separated from ID/Unicode failures.
- Keep cursor placement as an explicit fallback/debug mode.

This does not by itself fix a caco host that strips kitty escape passthrough; it should make that failure diagnosable and remove ID-overlap as a confounder.

title: web-search: read Copilot bearer from Pi auth.json + bump retired default model
description:
Fix the web-search plugin auth: read the Copilot bearer from Pi's auth.json and bump the retired default model.

Operator-reported: the web-search plugin stopped working because the static token file at config/gh-auth-tokens/copilot.token is stale and expired. The copilot-pat launchd service that is supposed to refresh it is not keeping it current. Pi's own auth.json at .pi/agent/auth.json holds github-copilot.access, which is the same Copilot bearer shape and is auto-refreshed by the Pi runtime.

Two root causes were found and fixed:
- Auth: the bearer is now resolved from Pi's auth.json github-copilot.access by default, with the legacy static token file kept as a fallback and an explicit token-file override still taking precedence for back-compat. New env knobs WEB_SEARCH_COPILOT_AUTH_JSON and WEB_SEARCH_COPILOT_AUTH_JSON_KEY.
- Model: the previous default model gpt-5.2-codex has been retired by the Copilot integrator and returns a 400 model-not-available error. Bumped the default to gpt-5.3-codex, which was verified live to return HTTP 200 with web_search_calls and grounded citations.

Applied to both implementations: the Pi JS extension extensions/web-search.js and the standalone Python web-search-mcp server. Docs updated in README.md and web-search/README.md.

Validated: node check clean, JS suite 587 green, Python pytest passing, docs check passing, plus a live end-to-end Copilot Responses API call confirming the fresh auth.json bearer is accepted and gpt-5.3-codex completes a real web search.

Filed from an operator request during agent-utils operation.
creator: ms-mac:agent-utils:ms-mac-agent-utils-agnt-dev-2

title: Investigate GitHub Pages publication for agent-utils
description:
The GitHub Pages site for agent-utils is not accessible at https://a.skh.am/agent-utils. Need to investigate whether:
1. The site is being published correctly
2. The publication is configured to run on a self-hosted runner
3. The DNS/domain mapping is set up correctly
4. The GitHub Actions workflow is executing successfully

Acceptance criteria:
- Determine if GH Pages is actually being published
- Verify if self-hosted runner is being used and if it's properly configured
- Document any configuration issues found
- Restore access to the site or provide explanation of why it's unavailable

Controller finding from agnt-ctrl while driving app automation: the Pages SPA code was reintegrated to main at 501b817. `gh run list --workflow pages.yml` shows the self-hosted Deploy GitHub Pages workflow triggering, but the newest run 25773551749 was still queued/in progress at observation time and older runs were cancelled by concurrency. `gh api repos/{owner}/{repo}/pages` returned 404, so Pages may not yet be enabled/configured even though the workflow exists. Follow-up should inspect the queued run/runner availability and repository Pages settings/custom domain for https://a.skh.am/agent-utils.

Controller follow-up 2026-05-14: agent-utils .github/workflows/pages.yml does not fully match the android-utils Pages example. Current agent-utils workflow has separate build/deploy jobs, both runs-on: self-hosted, concurrency cancel-in-progress: true, actions/configure-pages@v5 without enablement: true, upload/deploy actions v3/v4, and no explicit linux runner label. Android-utils uses a single deploy job on runs-on: [self-hosted, linux], timeout-minutes: 10, cancel-in-progress: false, stages docs into RUNNER_TEMP with .nojekyll, and calls actions/configure-pages@v6 with enablement: true before upload/deploy @v5. Current external check still returns 404 for https://a.skh.am/agent-utils and gh api repos/{owner}/{repo}/pages still returns 404; latest pages.yml run observed queued. Operator note: regular GitHub-hosted linux runner capacity should be available, so an implementation should consider moving the static Pages job to ubuntu-latest rather than depending on scarce self-hosted runner capacity, unless a repo policy requires self-hosted.
creator: pocket4:cacophony:harry

title: Land the Tendril source-machine resolver dedup (remediate false-closed bd-28e9b4/bd-ac2045)
description:
The Tendril source-machine resolver dedup described by the now-closed bd-28e9b4 and bd-ac2045 was never actually landed: on origin/main HEAD, extensions/tendril-share.js still defines its own inline resolveSourceMachine() (returning {host, remote:bool, source}) while extensions/tendril-command.js exports the shared tendrilSourceMachine() (returning {machine, remote:string|null, isRemote, wslTunnel, source}). Both closed beads have zero commits on origin/main and the duplication persists, so this is a remediation of a false-close.

Goal: collapse the inline resolveSourceMachine() in tendril-share.js onto the shared tendrilSourceMachine() helper so there is a single source of truth for "which machine produced this capture", WITHOUT changing the {host, remote, source} shape that tendril-share's own consumers (sourceMachineLabel) expect. Approach: keep a thin local adapter that calls tendrilSourceMachine() and maps machine->host, isRemote->remote(bool). Import tendrilSourceMachine from ./tendril-command.js. Keep test/tendril-share.test.js and test/tendril-command.test.js green. Verify full suite.

Context: dev-0 owns the shared tendril-command.js helper; this is in-lane and disjoint from dev-1 (auth-retry, landed) and dev-2 (lane clean). Supersedes closed bd-28e9b4 / bd-ac2045 (false-closed by ms-dev-2 instance, work not landed).
creator: ms-mac:agent-utils:ms-mac-agent-utils-agnt-dev-0

title: Add configurable Pi package autoupdate preflight extension
description:
Operator request from Harry via agnt-ctrl, 2026-05-17: add an extension for autoupdate settings so that when package updates are available at startup, Pi can automatically run `pi update --extensions` cleanly as part of a preflight phase.

Observed startup excerpt:
Package updates are available. Run pi update
Packages:
- github.com/harryaskham/agent-utils

Requested behavior:
- Add a configurable auto-update/preflight extension, likely building on `extensions/pi-self-update.js`, that detects the same "package updates available" condition before the normal interactive session proceeds.
- When enabled, run `pi update --extensions` cleanly from a safe cwd, with timeout/error reporting consistent with the existing `/update` command.
- Avoid update loops: only run once per startup/check result, handle no-op/no-updates as success, and avoid recursively restarting/reloading forever.
- After successful extension package updates, reload resources/tools or instruct the user when a full restart is required.
- Provide user-facing controls for enabling/disabling autoupdate and for status/help. If Pi does not yet expose first-class extension settings in `/settings`, use a namespaced config convention and document it.
- Add tests covering enabled/disabled behavior, successful update, update failure, and no-update cases without running real `pi update` in tests.

Controller notes: filed from operator startup transcript. Existing `extensions/pi-self-update.js` already provides `/update`, `pi_self_update`, `/reload-tools`, and restart helpers around `pi update --extensions`; this bead should extend that surface into a startup/preflight opt-in rather than duplicating the update runner.

Operator follow-up from Harry, 2026-05-17: settings.json exposure is acceptable for this autoupdate feature. A first-class Pi `/settings` integration is not required for this bead; use a clearly documented namespaced JSON setting plus command/status helpers.
creator: pocket4:agent-utils:pocket4-agent-utils-agnt-ctrl

title: Tendril/kitty image preview destroys terminal scrollback (row-reservation scroll yanks viewport to live bottom)
description:
Operator-reported (Harry, 2026-06-07): "when agents do tendril graphics stuff in pi, it stops me being able to scroll back at all; need to preserve scrollback buffer, make this work naturally." I.e. while a tendril capture/stream or kitty image preview is visible, the terminal viewport keeps getting yanked to the live bottom, so scrolling up into history is impossible.

WHAT IS ALREADY SCROLL-SAFE (do not redo — code-grounded):
- Editor decorative graphics use a non-forced, coalescible render request (`requestEditorDecorativeRender` in pi-graphics.js ~L483; `PI_GRAPHICS_EDITOR_FORCE_REDRAW=1` is diagnostic-only). Heat/context redraws are scroll-safe and tmux-gated.
- Image-preview render requests are all NON-forced: `requestSidePanelRender(state,{force=false})`, `requestSideOverlayRender` → `requestRender()` with no force (kitty-image-preview.js ~L218-225).
- Cursor-mode display already suppresses cursor movement: `buildPngDisplayCommand` emits `a=T` WITH `C=1` (kitty-graphics.js L250); placement + relative-placement commands all carry `C=1` defensively (L407, L443). So the preview's own kitty emit does not move the terminal cursor.
- Unicode-placeholder path decouples transmit from placement (transmit-once guard, bd-d6fa1b) and anchors in-place.
- Prior fixes in this exact area: bd-b7d96b (P0, "Revert inline cursor relative placement scroll regression"), bd-903d89 (Ghostty cursor-placement fallback to avoid PUA tofu), bd-3d7aca (cap previews to half viewport).

NARROWED RESIDUAL ROOT CAUSE (needs live byte-stream confirmation in Harry's terminal):
1. ROW RESERVATION SCROLL (most likely): the `aboveEditor`/inline widget path is N rows tall. pi-tui reserves those rows by consuming screen rows at the bottom; when the widget appears / changes height / a stream repaints at a new size, the terminal scrolls to make room. Repeated per stream frame, this continuously pulls the viewport to the live bottom and defeats scroll-up. The `rightOverlay`/side-panel path (`renderTuiWithSidePanel`) instead composes the image into EXISTING top rows without adding height — height-neutral, scroll-preserving.
2. STREAM REPAINT CADENCE: `scheduleNextStreamFrame` (setTimeout, intervalMs) plus animation/cycle timers re-render frequently; any per-frame height change re-triggers (1).
3. pi-tui core full-frame repaint when the managed region grows (partly upstream).

PROPOSED FIX DIRECTION (agent-utils side):
a. For tendril captures/streams, DEFAULT to the height-neutral side-panel / `rightOverlay` placement (compose into existing rows) instead of the height-adding `aboveEditor`/inline widget, so showing/refreshing a preview never reserves new bottom rows.
b. Keep the reserved preview height STABLE for the lifetime of a stream (size once, do not grow/shrink per frame) so repaints don't re-scroll.
c. Add an opt-out env flag and unit tests asserting: stream/preview selects a non-height-adding placement by default, and repeated frames do not change reserved height. (kitty-image-preview layout helpers already have headless tests — bd-6dda10.)
d. If any residual yank remains, capture the emitted escape byte stream during a live tendril preview (kitty + ghostty, with/without tmux) to confirm whether newline/scroll sequences are emitted for row reservation; route the remainder to a pi-tui upstream report if it is core repaint behavior.

ACCEPTANCE: with a tendril stream/preview active, scrolling up into terminal history stays put across repaints; default tendril preview placement does not add bottom rows; regression test covers placement default + stable stream height. FINAL VISUAL CONFIRMATION REQUIRES A LIVE kitty/ghostty TERMINAL (cannot be verified headlessly); this path already had one P0 scroll regression (bd-b7d96b), so changes must be eyeballed before trust.
creator: ms-mac:agent-utils:ms-mac-agent-utils-agnt-dev-0

title: Cover kitty-graphics serializeKittyGraphicsChunks (multi-chunk) + detectKittyPassthroughMode
description:
Finish the kitty-graphics pure surface after bd-5332d7. Two high-value pure functions remain untested:

1. serializeKittyGraphicsChunks(control, payloadBase64, {chunkSize}): the multi-chunk transmission serializer for large images. Logic: chunkBase64 split (chunkSize normalized to [512,4096], multiple of 4); single-chunk delegates to serializeKittyGraphicsCommand; multi-chunk emits one APC per chunk with the m (more) flag = 1 for all but the last (0), the first chunk carrying the full control map and subsequent chunks only {m}. A bug here breaks large-image display. Probed: payload 'A'*2000 @ chunkSize 512 -> 4 APCs with m flags 1,1,1,0; first carries a=<control.a>.
2. detectKittyPassthroughMode(env): returns env.KITTY_IMAGE_PREVIEW_PASSTHROUGH override, else 'tmux' when env.TMUX set, else 'none'.

Scope: add tests to test/kitty-graphics.test.js: serializeKittyGraphicsChunks single-chunk delegation (1 APC) and multi-chunk (N APCs, first has the control's a=, m flags 1...1,0); detectKittyPassthroughMode none/tmux/explicit-override. Regression net, no source changes. Verified via npm test + coverage. In-lane self-improvement (aurora-agent-utils-agnt-dev-aur-0, 2026-06-24).

title: Implement Kitty graphics caching layer for stable image IDs
description:
Create a caching and state management system for Kitty graphics that tracks:
1. Uploaded image stable IDs and their content hash
2. Current placement of each image ID on screen
3. Whether an image is already uploaded vs needs fresh upload

This infrastructure enables the renderer to:
- Reuse previously uploaded graphics without re-uploading
- Update only the placement coordinates between animation frames
- Determine when a full redraw is actually necessary (geometry changes, new content)

Acceptance criteria:
- Graphics state is tracked across frames
- Stable IDs are reused when content hasn't changed
- Placement updates are decoupled from content uploads
- Cache is properly invalidated when needed

title: charCellWidth over-counts zero-width formatting/joiner chars (ZWJ/ZWSP/word-joiner/BOM/bidi)
description:
extensions/pi-graphics/ansi-width.js charCellWidth() treats Unicode zero-width FORMATTING/JOINER characters as width 1, over-counting visible width. It already zeroes C0/C1 controls, the Latin combining-diacritical block (U+0300..U+036F), and variation selectors (U+FE00..U+FE0F), but NOT:

- U+200B..U+200F (ZWSP, ZWNJ, ZWJ, LRM, RLM)
- U+202A..U+202E (bidi embedding/override controls)
- U+2060..U+2064 (word joiner, function application, invisible operators)
- U+FEFF (zero-width no-break space / BOM)

These render as zero cells but currently return 1. Observed: the family emoji ZWJ sequence U+1F468 ZWJ U+1F469 ZWJ U+1F467 reports approximateVisibleCells=8 (2+1+2+1+2) instead of the ~2 cells it renders as. Impact: footer segment budgeting (fitFooterSegments) and rendered-row clamping (clampRenderedLineToWidth/Rows) over-estimate the width of any status/label text containing emoji ZWJ sequences or zero-width formatting marks, shrinking/truncating more aggressively than needed.

Fix: extend the zero-width branch in charCellWidth to include the four ranges above (strict correctness improvement, consistent with the existing combining-mark/variation-selector handling). Pure-ASCII/CJK widths are unchanged. Extend test/ansi-width.test.js to cover ZWJ/ZWSP/word-joiner/BOM/bidi zero-width and the family-emoji sequence. Verified via npm test (ansi-width + footer-layout). In-lane self-improvement, backed by the coverage added in bd-cf194e (aurora-agent-utils-agnt-dev-aur-0, 2026-06-24).
creator: aurora:agent-utils:aurora-agent-utils-agnt-dev-aur-0

While working bd-0395bf, Harry observed that kitty-image-preview Unicode placeholders rendered as raw glyphs/blank pixels inside caco/tmux, and noted pi-graphics primitive styles were also not visible. The current generator emits tmux DCS-wrapped kitty APC commands and tmux has allow-passthrough=on, so if forced cursor-mode previews are also blank the likely failure is the caco/Pi host output path not forwarding raw kitty graphics escape sequences from widget output or ctx.ui.write to the outer kitty client.

Suggested fix shape: add a first-party caco/Pi host passthrough probe that writes a tiny kitty graphics command through each available output path (widget render prefix, ctx.ui.write, ctx.ui.terminal.write) and reports which path reaches kitty; then route image-preview/pi-graphics through the working path or advertise a fallback.

Filed via reflect-session mixin from bd-0395bf session.

title: Audit remaining lowercase d=i supersede deletes (box-chrome strip, cursor/relative placements) for orphaned image data
description:
Follow-up from bd-1be5ca (kitty image-data free on teardown). bd-1be5ca converted the main teardown paths to uppercase d=I (free image data). A few per-supersede placement-only deletes were intentionally left as lowercase d=i and should be audited to confirm they don't orphan image data:

- extensions/pi-graphics/box-chrome.js:~3008 — buildDeleteCommand({ imageId: previous.stripImageId, placementId: previous.relPlacementId, deleteMode: "i" }) deletes a SUPERSEDED box-rail strip placement on resize. If the strip image id is width-dependent (fresh id per width) and is not later freed via clearBoxChromeImages/session_end ownership, its data is orphaned on every resize.
- extensions/pi-graphics.js cursor/relative-placement deletes (~576, ~1017, ~1339, ~1592) use deleteMode "i". These are per-keystroke/redraw; if the relative-placement/halo image ids are re-used (stable) they are bounded, but if any path allocates fresh ids it would orphan data.

Action: confirm for each that the superseded image id is either (a) re-used (stable id → overwrite, bounded), or (b) still tracked in state.ownedImageIds so session_end's freeImageData:true reclaims it, or (c) freed inline with freeImageData. Add a regression assertion where a fresh id is superseded. Likely small/bounded vs the stream leak bd-1be5ca already fixed, hence P3.

Also note (dev-loop friction, already partly covered by bd-d433ca structural harness): the actual macOS WindowServer/IOSurface RSS reclamation from bd-1be5ca cannot be confirmed headlessly because the running Pi session loads kitty-image-preview from a pinned package, not the checkout — needs one live kitty-terminal confirmation after a repin (use footprint WindowServer / vmmap, not ps).

Filed via reflect-session mixin from bd-1be5ca session.

title: Register openai-realtime provider during realtime extension startup
description:
Operator request from Harry via agnt-ctrl, 2026-05-17: when the realtime extension is enabled, it should register the `openai-realtime` provider and `gpt-realtime-2` model immediately during Pi startup, not only lazily when `/rt` commands are called.

Observed startup symptom:
`Warning: No models match pattern "openai-realtime/gpt-realtime-2"`

Requested behavior:
- The realtime extension should call `pi.registerProvider()` during extension factory/startup so the provider/model exist before model pattern resolution, scoped-model checks, and startup warnings.
- `/rt` commands should switch to the realtime provider/model as needed, but should not be the first point where the model becomes known.
- Use the documented extension startup pattern: provider registrations made during the extension factory are queued and flushed before normal startup continues; async factories are awaited before `session_start`/`resources_discover`.
- Add regression coverage that enabling the realtime extension makes `openai-realtime/gpt-realtime-2` visible during startup or `--list-models`, before invoking any `/rt` command.

Controller notes: filed from operator startup transcript. Relevant docs: pi `docs/extensions.md` says async extension factories are awaited before startup and `pi.registerProvider()` calls during factory load are flushed before normal startup; `pi.registerProvider()` is the supported dynamic provider/model registration API.
creator: pocket4:agent-utils:pocket4-agent-utils-agnt-ctrl

title: kitty graphics cleanup frees placements but not image data — macOS IOSurface/WindowServer RAM leak in long-running agents
description:
Operator (Harry) report 2026-06-14: macOS WindowServer RAM grows huge; suspects the in-Pi agent-utils kitty image-preview/pi-graphics plugin spams IOSurface that is never cleaned up, so long-running backgrounded pi agents leak unbounded GPU/image memory even when kitty graphics is not actively displayed.

Root cause (audit this session): every cleanup/teardown path uses the kitty delete selector lowercase `d=i` (buildScopedDeleteCommand and buildDeleteCommand default deleteMode "i"). Per the kitty graphics protocol, lowercase selectors delete only PLACEMENTS; the uploaded image DATA (and its macOS IOSurface-backed texture) is freed only by the UPPERCASE selector `d=I`. So:
- pi-graphics.js session_end handler deletes placements but never frees image data → every backgrounded agent that ever rendered box-chrome/footer/cursor chrome leaves its image bitmaps resident in the shared kitty process for the life of that kitty process.
- pi_graphics_clear tool description says "Delete every kitty image owned by this extension" but only deletes placements (does not free data) — correctness bug.
- kitty-image-preview.js has NO session_end handler at all; preview/screenshot/tendril/playwright stream frames are never freed on exit.

Protocol confirmation: lowercase i/a/p = placements only; uppercase I/A/P = same match + free image data when unreferenced. Virtual (Unicode-placeholder) placements are only affected by i/I/r/R/n/N delete modes (not z/p/c), so scoped image-id delete is the authoritative free path and must use uppercase to actually reclaim memory.

Fix direction (narrow, evidence-driven; the 2026-05-24 audit warns against broad rewrites):
- Add a freeImageData option to buildDeleteCommand/buildScopedDeleteCommand that emits uppercase `d=I` (still scoped per owned image id; never global d=A/d=I-all).
- Use freeImageData=true on genuine teardown paths: pi-graphics session_end, pi_graphics_clear, kitty-image-preview clear/stop-stream-with-cleanup, and a new kitty-image-preview session_end handler.
- Keep lowercase placement-only deletes for in-session transient hide/redraw/next-image transitions that intentionally retain the cached upload for fast repaint.
- Add/extend tests asserting uppercase `d=I` is emitted on teardown/clear.

Filed from operator broadcast while operating the harness (cacophony-bead-scope / agent-utils).

UPDATE 2026-06-14 (reconciliation): While implementing, peer bead bd-b94fa1 (ms-mac) independently landed a broad kitty image-data free fix on main — freeData option (uppercase d=I/d=Z), a releaseOwnedImageData helper, and freeing data on pi-graphics session_end/clear/box-chrome plus kitty-image-preview session_shutdown, explicit clear, a session_start startup-reclaim, and a multi-image-gallery eviction reclaim. I reset my branch onto main rather than clobber that landed work. One residual gap remained and is what this bead now delivers: the per-frame STREAM supersede. preparePagePayloads' eviction reclaim early-returns unless there are 2+ images in side-overlay mode, but a stream replaces items down to a single live frame each capture — so neither that reclaim nor the single-image placement-only delete frees superseded stream-frame data, and ownedImageIds grows unbounded WITHIN a long-running streaming session (bd-b94fa1's teardown/startup reclaim only bounds it across session boundaries). Fix: prepareCurrentImage, when a stream is active and clearPrevious is set, calls releaseOwnedImageData(state, keepIds:[current.id]) to free superseded frame DATA and prune ownership to the live frame. Commit b2ecb9b; +2 tests; full suite 637 pass.

title: Add trueDefault model/provider/effort settings guard
description:
Operator request from Harry via agnt-ctrl, 2026-05-17: add `trueDefault*` settings.json entries for model, provider, and effort/thinking so runtime TUI changes do not permanently overwrite the user's real defaults.

Problem: Pi's built-in TUI controls mutate the standard default settings when the user changes model/provider/effort at runtime. Harry wants to be able to change model at runtime just fine, but have a plugin-owned immutable-by-convention set of true defaults that only changes when settings.json is edited directly.

Requested behavior:
- Support namespaced settings.json entries such as `trueDefaultProvider`, `trueDefaultModel`, and `trueDefaultThinkingLevel` / `trueDefaultEffort` (final names to align with Pi setting terminology; Pi docs currently call the effort setting `defaultThinkingLevel`).
- On Pi load/startup, force-copy the true-default values onto the built-in default provider/model/effort settings before the session uses them.
- On clean Pi shutdown, force-copy the true-default values onto the built-in default provider/model/effort settings again, so any runtime TUI model/effort changes do not persist as the normal defaults.
- Runtime model/provider/effort switching must remain allowed during the session. This is a persistence/default guard, not a runtime lock.
- Do not modify the `trueDefault*` values from TUI/runtime commands; they should change only via direct settings.json edit unless Harry explicitly asks for a command to edit them later.
- Add documentation with an example settings.json block and explain the relationship to `/model`, Ctrl+P, `/settings`, and the requested `/effort` shortcut (`bd-c4a33f`).
- Add tests for startup restoration, clean-shutdown restoration, and runtime model changes remaining possible.

Controller notes: no duplicate open/draft bead was visible during triage. Related request: `bd-c4a33f` adds a quick `/effort` shortcut; this bead covers persistent true-default semantics across model/provider/effort settings.

title: Implement Rust skill-server utility with MCP stdio meta-tool
description:
Implement a new Rust-based agent-utils utility called `skill-server` (`ss`) anchored around the `mcp-cli` crate, using Tendril as the reference for CLI/MCP stdio structure.

Goal:
- Provide both a CLI and stdio MCP server for dynamic skill/tool discovery without requiring agents to fully reload.
- Expose a single meta-tool / slash entrypoint for agents, conceptually `/ss <domain> <query-or-command>`.
- Example: `/ss web query ...` discovers a registered web-search MCP tool/server on the host and calls it.

Configuration:
- Read host configuration from `.config/ss/config.yaml` by default, with env/flag override if practical.
- Config should define skill search paths and MCP server definitions, e.g. paths to scan for Pi/Cacophony skills plus named MCP stdio servers/commands.
- Config should allow discovery of skills and other host MCP servers, including metadata needed to route a request to a matching server/tool.

Implementation expectations:
- Rust workspace/crate for `skill-server`/`ss`.
- Use or mirror Tendril's `mcp-cli` usage for JSON envelopes, CLI/MCP parity, and `mcp stdio` entrypoint design.
- Keep CLI and MCP execution paths unified.
- Implement a skeleton that builds first, with typed config loading, discovery abstractions, and a minimal meta-tool call path.
- Add tests/smokes for config parsing and MCP/CLI surface where feasible.

Acceptance criteria:
- `ss` (or `skill-server`) has a working CLI help surface.
- `ss mcp stdio` starts a stdio MCP server using mcp-cli-style plumbing.
- `.config/ss/config.yaml` sample/default is documented and supports search paths plus MCP server definitions.
- A minimal `/ss`-style meta request can discover a configured skill/server/tool and return a structured result or clear structured error.
- Build/test instructions are documented.

Filed from agnt-ctrl after Harry requested a new utility skill-server and pointed to Tendril's MCP CLI usage as the example.

title: Add coverage for pi-graphics runtime.js buildAnimatedPlacement (transmit-once dedup)
description:
Found via npm run test:coverage: extensions/pi-graphics/runtime.js is at ~79% line, with the only uncovered region (88-115) being buildAnimatedPlacement — the animated sibling of the already-tested buildPlacement. It carries genuine transmit-once dedup/caching logic: on first call with a given (image id + content hash) it emits the kitty virtual-placement animation escape and returns transmitted:true; a repeat call with the same frames returns an empty transmit and transmitted:false (so animation bytes are not re-sent over SSH/tmux on every redraw). frames reports pngs.length either way.

Scope: add test/pi-graphics-runtime.test.js asserting buildAnimatedPlacement: image id in the 32-bit namespace, transmitted:true + non-empty transmit (\\x1b_G) + frames===pngs.length + lines + state.ownedImageIds tracking on first call; transmit==="" + transmitted:false on a same-content repeat. Mirrors the existing buildPlacement test, using renderPromptEnclosure frames. Regression net, no source changes. Verified via npm test + coverage. In-lane self-improvement (aurora-agent-utils-agnt-dev-aur-0, 2026-06-24).

title: Generic checkout-behind-default-branch warning guard (async/throttled Pi extension with settings)
description:
ASK (Harry 2026-06-17, filed cross-project by caco-ctrl for msm-3 who is cacophony worker-scoped): Cacophony's git-check mixin (PostToolUse nudge when a managed checkout falls behind main) was effectively lost when managed Pi moved to the pi-home-override path. Add a GENERIC reusable agent-utils Pi extension that warns ANY Pi agent when its checkout is significantly behind the origin default branch.

KEY DESIGN:
- Configurable settings: behind-threshold N, default-branch auto-detect (via origin/HEAD or explicit override), enable/disable.
- ASYNC + THROTTLED, NOT per-message: every N messages (async, non-blocking) and/or git-tool-triggered (PreToolUse/PostToolUse on bash git = the 'pre-git hook' idea).
- Fetch CACHED so back-to-back git calls don't each fetch.
- Check = fetch origin default + 'git rev-list --count HEAD..origin/<default>', warn >= threshold with a rebase nudge.
- Graceful no-op on fetch-fail / detached-HEAD / non-git.
- Advisory warning, not a hard error. +docs.
- Reference: generalize Cacophony's git-check mixin, decoupled from daemon-checkout specifics.

msm-3 offered to drop the full verbatim spec into a scratchpad note if needed.
creator: ms-mac:cacophony:ms-mac-cacophony-caco-ctrl

title: Fix kitty graphics Pi mode rendering inside tmux/caco
description:
Operator report from Harry via agnt-ctrl, 2026-05-18: the new kitty graphics Pi mode does not work inside tmux/caco. It shows many duplicate graphics rows and draws badly.

Observed behavior:
- Running the kitty graphics-backed Pi mode under the managed caco/tmux environment produces duplicated graphics rows.
- The rendered layout is visually corrupted/badly drawn, making the mode unusable in the primary managed-agent environment.

Requested behavior:
- Kitty graphics Pi mode must render correctly when Pi is running inside tmux under caco.
- Avoid duplicate placeholder/image rows and stale image artifacts across rerenders.
- Validate tmux/caco terminal capability handling separately from direct-terminal kitty behavior; feature should degrade gracefully or disable itself if the environment cannot support stable graphics.
- Add a targeted tmux/caco smoke test or reproducible harness that exercises the kitty graphics Pi mode in a tmux pane and checks for duplicate row output / unstable redraw behavior where practical.

Controller notes:
- This is related to, but distinct from, `bd-3d7aca` (preview height cap/flicker). This bead is specifically about the new kitty graphics Pi mode being broken inside tmux/caco with duplicate graphics rows and bad drawing.
creator: pocket4:agent-utils:pocket4-agent-utils-agnt-ctrl

title: Dedupe Tendril source-machine resolver to shared tendrilSourceMachine helper
description: Two beads (bd-a4f693 native --remote hooks, bd-668a82 inference attribution), both landed on main, each resolve the Tendril bridge into a source-machine identity. To stay independently reintegrable during a beads-daemon outage, bd-a4f693 landed a shared tendrilSourceMachine(env, override) export in extensions/tendril-command.js while bd-668a82 kept an inline resolveSourceMachine() inside extensions/tendril-share.js. Both are now on origin/main (79e66f7 and 18ce8c9). title: Clarify Pi graphics cleanup limits for Unicode virtual placements
description:
Audit finding from Harry's Kitty graphics protocol review: Kitty z-index delete commands do not affect Unicode virtual placements (`U=1`); virtual placements are affected by image/id-oriented delete modes such as `d=i`, not `d=z`. Our docs/comments discuss reserved z-index band cleanup for hosted Pi graphics, which is valid for real/relative placements but not sufficient for virtual placeholder placements.

Clarify docs/comments and any cleanup helper naming so per-image scoped deletes remain the authoritative cleanup path for virtual placeholder graphics, while z-band cleanup is described as real/relative placement scrubbing only.

Filed from agnt-dev-2 Kitty protocol audit session.
creator: ms-mac:agent-utils:ms-mac-agent-utils-agnt-dev-2

title: Preserve Pi TUI cursor markers in graphics wrapping

Preserve APC/DCS controls during visible-width calculation, truncation, placeholder insertion, and padding so custom editors and focusable dialogs keep IME cursor placement correct under graphical chrome.

title: Suppress subagent ready banner during quiet startup
description:
Operator request from Harry via agnt-ctrl, 2026-05-17: the Subagent startup message should be suppressed when Pi quiet startup is enabled.

Observed startup excerpt:
`Subagent: ready. /subagent <mode> <task> or use subagent tool. Modes: single, parallel, chain.`

Requested behavior:
- When Pi's `quietStartup` setting is true, the subagent extension should not print or notify the startup-ready banner.
- Preserve explicit help/discovery via `/subagent` help or command descriptions.
- If verbose startup is enabled, keep existing startup visibility.
- Add coverage or a small startup harness assertion for quiet startup so extension banners do not pierce the quiet setting.

Controller notes: filed from operator startup transcript. Pi settings docs define `quietStartup` as hiding startup header; extension output should respect the same quiet-startup intent where possible. Current ExtensionAPI does not expose a first-class settings getter in the documented API, so implementation may need to use existing startup context/options if available in agent-utils, or add a small runtime helper if the package already carries one.

Triage note from ms-mac-agent-utils-agnt-dev, 2026-05-22: I claimed this bead and verified the agent-utils checkout has no tracked `subagent` extension/source (`git ls-tree -r origin/main --name-only | grep -i subagent` returns nothing; `rg` finds no startup banner in the checkout). The observed banner string appears in prior Pi session evidence pointing at `/Users/harryaskham/collective/standalone/pi/extensions/subagent.js`, i.e. outside the managed agent-utils checkout; the current managed Pi-local copy at `../.pi-agent/extensions/subagent.js` also no longer contains the `session_start` ready-banner block. Because there is no agent-utils source change to land and closing would falsely mark unlanded work done, I moved this bead back to draft/triage with `out-of-checkout` for controller/operator routing or duplicate/obsolete resolution.
creator: pocket4:agent-utils:pocket4-agent-utils-agnt-ctrl

title: Cover verified pure/injectable gaps: pi-self-update restart spawn-fallback + layout componentContains
description:
More verified (not assumed) pure/injectable coverage gaps surfaced by test:coverage:summary, in modules I'd written off as harness-gated:

1. extensions/pi-self-update.js executePiRestartPlan SPAWN-FALLBACK path (lines ~353-360) is untested — only the execve path has a test, so the fallback used when process.execve is unavailable (spawn child + wire exit/error -> exit code) is uncovered; a bug there breaks /restart on platforms without execve. Also buildPiRestartPlan's sessionDir branch (~323-324: pushes --session-dir) is uncovered. Both are testable via injected spawnImpl/exit and explicit sessionDir.
2. extensions/kitty-image-preview/layout.js componentContains (line ~269) is a PURE recursive component-tree contains check (with a seen-set cycle guard) and is untested.

Scope: append a test to test/pi-self-update.test.js for executePiRestartPlan with execve unavailable (asserts spawnImpl invoked with executable + args.slice(1), method:'spawn', and exit wiring) and buildPiRestartPlan with sessionDir (asserts --session-dir in restartArgs); add a focused test for componentContains (root contains target nested in children; false when absent; cycle-safe via seen). Regression net, no source changes. Verified via npm test + coverage. In-lane self-improvement (aurora-agent-utils-agnt-dev-aur-0, 2026-06-24).
creator: aurora:agent-utils:aurora-agent-utils-agnt-dev-aur-0

title: False-close: bd-d433ca closed with no landed deliverable; close-validator bypass let work leak
description:
bd-d433ca ("Runnable multi-image kitty side-panel render smoke") was marked closed at 2026-06-13T23:05 by agnt-dev-1, who narrated "render smoke and its node test are committed and green ... reintegrating now". But the deliverable (a scripts/ runnable smoke + package.json entry) never landed: it was absent from origin/main and from every ref (git log --all -- 'scripts/*paged*' 'scripts/*sidepanel*' empty; no commit footer references bd-d433ca on main). Only the companion bd-3623f7 render-harness.js library landed. agnt-dev-0 rediscovered the gap on crash-revive, reopened bd-d433ca, and re-implemented + landed the missing smoke.

This is the closed-but-not-landed failure mode the dev profile warns about: closed must mean "work is on main". A close that passed despite the bead ID being absent from origin/main implies the close-validator was bypassed (likely --validate-on-main false or --admin-override) on a bead that genuinely had pending real work, OR the reintegrate silently failed and close ran anyway.

Friction-shape / suggested fix directions (close-validator lives in the cacophony harness, not agent-utils, so this likely re-routes to the cacophony project on triage):
- When a worker requests bd close --validate-on-main false, require it to also assert the landing path (reintegrate receipt / commit SHA) so a missing-deliverable close is caught.
- Consider a periodic sweep that flags closed beads whose ID has zero origin/main commit footers AND whose description promised a concrete file deliverable, surfacing them for re-open.
- Tighten the lifecycle so a failed/rejected reintegrate cannot be followed by a close in the same session without an explicit override + reason.

Filed via reflect-session mixin from the bd-d433ca re-implementation session (agnt-dev-0).
creator: ms-mac:agent-utils:ms-mac-agent-utils-agnt-dev-0

title: No first-party dev loop to live-validate an in-development agent-utils extension checkout (kitty render)
description:
While implementing bd-502d6a (kitty image-preview sidebar), the render-dependent
parts (multi-placement side panel, scrollback clobber) cannot be validated by the
implementing dev agent: the running Pi session loads the `kitty-image-preview`
extension from a pinned package, not the agent checkout, so live kitty rendering
and scrollback behaviour can only be confirmed by the operator manually pointing a
terminal/session at the checkout.

Friction shape: there is no documented/first-party way for a managed dev agent to
launch or point a Pi session at a local extension checkout (e.g. an
`pi --extension-dir <checkout>` override or a `CACO`/`PI` env that prefers checkout
extensions over the pinned package) to validate kitty-render changes in-loop. This
forces render work to land "foundation only + pure unit tests" and defers the
actual wiring to an operator validation window, which slows multi-part render beads.

Suggested fix: provide a first-party, documented way to run a Pi session against an
in-development extension checkout for visual/kitty validation, or a headless kitty
render harness that exercises the transmit/placeholder escape output structurally.

Filed via reflect-session mixin from bd-502d6a session.
creator: ms-mac:agent-utils:ms-mac-agent-utils-agnt-dev-0

creator: pocket4:agent-utils:pocket4-agent-utils-agnt-ctrl

title: copilot-auth-refresh extension injects unbounded retry user-messages -> reply storm / context fill
description:
The agent-utils Pi extension extensions/copilot-auth-refresh.js injects a retry prompt via pi.sendUserMessage on the agent_end hook when a github-copilot missing-API-key error is seen. Its dedup guard keys on `${timestamp}:${retryText}`, which does NOT prevent a storm: each successive auth failure produces a NEW assistant-error timestamp, so the guard passes every time and a fresh "GitHub Copilot auth was refreshed... Retry the previous request now:" user message is injected repeatedly. There is no bounded retry budget, so repeated transient auth failures fill context and cause reply storms.

Fix: bound retries per underlying request. Track an attempt counter keyed on the stable retry text (the user request being retried), reset when the request text changes or a turn succeeds. After a small cap (e.g. 2 attempts) stop injecting and surface a single terminal notify instead of another sendUserMessage. Keep existing exact-duplicate guard. Preserve the existing passing tests and add a regression test that distinct-timestamp repeated copilot failures with the same retry text only inject up to the cap.

Supersedes mis-scoped cacophony bead bd-46cb54 (root cause is this project's plugin, not the core harness).
creator: ms-mac:agent-utils:ms-mac-agent-utils-agnt-dev-1

title: Add efficient facet Pi graphics input chrome
description: Add a deterministic low-entropy facet box chrome effect for input surfaces: sparse angled facets, entry glints, and prompt guide cuts, so input surfaces no longer use the prism motif. Keep prism available as an explicit variant; avoid timers, dense textures, masks, glyph dependencies, graph layout, or repaint loops.
creator: ms-mac:agent-utils:ms-mac-agent-utils-agnt-dev

title: Document the typebox vs lib/tool-schema.js shim choice for testable extensions
description: Friction hit while implementing bd-a0e836: a new extension that imports { Type } from '@sinclair/typebox' (like firecracker-vm.js / pi-graphics.js / web-search.js) fails to load under 'node --test' in the agent-utils checkout because typebox is a peerDependency not installed in node_modules here — the whole test file errors with ERR_MODULE_NOT_FOUND. The fix is to import { ToolSchema as Type } from './lib/tool-schema.js' (the pattern android.js already uses), whose lowercase API (Type.object/string/boolean/number, Type.optional as identity) keeps the extension importable in tests. This split is currently tribal knowledge: there's no doc note saying 'use the tool-schema shim, not typebox, if you want the extension unit-tested at import level.' Suggestion: add a short CONTRIBUTING/README note (or a comment in lib/tool-schema.js) documenting when to use the shim vs typebox, and which existing extensions are intentionally not import-tested because they pull typebox directly. Filed from the bd-a0e836 session.
creator: ms-dev-2:agent-utils:ms-dev-2-agent-utils-agnt-dev-ms2-1

title: Expose agent-side realtime call and Pulse device controls
description:
Operators currently can start Pi realtime calls through the user-facing `/rt` slash command, and the realtime extension can read Pulse routing from process environment such as `PI_RT_AUDIO_BACKEND=pulse`, `PULSE_SERVER=sgu24:4713`, `PULSE_SOURCE=source.bluetooth`, and `PULSE_SINK=...`. From the agent side, though, there is no first-class tool/API surface for managing a realtime call or selecting Pulse routing at runtime.

Observed in helsinki-cacophony-cluster-ctrl session while trying to satisfy Harry's request: "start a rt call with pulse on sgu24:4713 and device source.bluetooth". Inspection of `extensions/realtime-agent.js` and `docs/realtime-agent.md` showed `/rt start vad|ptt|nolisten`, `/rt backend pulse`, `/rt doctor`, and env/custom-command knobs, but no agent-callable tool for configuring `PULSE_SERVER` / `PULSE_SOURCE` / `PULSE_SINK`, starting/stopping/listening, or reporting call state.

Desired capability:
- Expose agent-side tools/control methods for realtime call lifecycle: start, stop, cancel, status/doctor, audio on/off, listen mode, STT mode.
- Expose safe runtime configuration for Pulse routing: server, source, sink, and/or explicit record/playback commands, ideally scoped to the active Pi session/call rather than requiring Pi process restart.
- Make the tool status clearly report the resolved backend and Pulse endpoints so agents can confirm the requested device is in use.
- Preserve the existing slash-command UX and safety boundaries; do not leak API keys or raw environment secrets in tool output.

Acceptance criteria:
- A managed Pi agent can start a realtime call using Pulse server `sgu24:4713` and source `source.bluetooth` without operator-only slash-command intervention or Pi process restart, assuming credentials and Pulse reachability exist.
- Agents can query status/doctor output and stop/cancel the call via the same agent-side surface.
- Documentation explains both the slash command and agent-side tool workflows.

Filed from helsinki Cacophony controller after Harry requested agent-managed realtime call support.

Coordination note: attempted twice to direct-message `pocket4-agent-utils-agnt-ctrl` after filing, but `caco msg send --project agent-utils --target pocket4-agent-utils-agnt-ctrl` failed retryably with `daemon_endpoint_transport_error` on `/api/v1/projects/agent-utils/messages/send` while `/api/v1/node` was reachable. Broadcast fallback succeeded as `msg-019e1b82-b256-7fe3-a5fc-abd460d006d1` so the agent-utils controller can pick up/triage the request.
creator: helsinki:cacophony:helsinki-cacophony-cluster-ctrl

title: Remove accidental shell line 'export CACOPHONY_PROJECT=agent-utils' from .gitignore
description:
.gitignore line 4 is `export CACOPHONY_PROJECT=agent-utils` — a shell statement that was accidentally committed into the gitignore file (not a valid gitignore pattern). It is a no-op ignore rule: no tracked or untracked file matches the literal string, so it ignores nothing, but it is clearly erroneous cruft from a paste error (verified: `git ls-files | grep` finds no such path; `git status --ignored` shows nothing ignored by it). The other four lines (/target/, .direnv, /artifacts/, result) are legitimate.

Fix: remove the erroneous `export CACOPHONY_PROJECT=agent-utils` line from .gitignore, leaving the four valid patterns. No functional change (the pattern matched nothing); this just removes the accidental shell line. Verified via npm run check + npm test stay green and git status is unaffected. Found by re-scrutinizing a tracked config file (aurora-agent-utils-agnt-dev-aur-0, 2026-06-24).
creator: aurora:agent-utils:aurora-agent-utils-agnt-dev-aur-0

title: Document Pi extension interception boundaries (input event vs TUI-consumed builtins; completions have no ctx)

This isn't documented in the agent-utils README or pi extension notes. A short "Pi extension capability boundaries" note (which builtins are interceptable via the `input` event vs consumed earlier in the TUI; how getArgumentCompletions has no ctx so the registry must be captured at session_start) would save future agent-utils extension authors significant spelunking through node_modules dist. Filed via reflect-session mixin from bd-bc26e9 session.

title: Add mcp-cli test for JSON-RPC error/edge paths (unsupported method, ping, notifications, unknown tool)
description:
The mcp-cli crate's stdio server JSON-RPC handling (McpServer::handle_request in crates/mcp-cli/src/server.rs) has a happy-path test (stdio_server_handles_initialize_list_and_call covers initialize/tools.list/tools.call success) but the protocol error/edge branches are untested: unsupported method -> JSON-RPC error code -32601; ping -> empty {} result; notifications/initialized -> NO response (a notification must not get a reply, or MCP clients break); tools/call with an unknown tool -> structured error envelope with isError:true. A regression in any of these breaks MCP protocol compliance.

Scope: add a #[test] to crates/mcp-cli/src/lib.rs (reusing the existing build_math_router / frame_request / parse_framed_responses fixtures) that feeds ping + notifications/initialized + an unsupported method + a tools/call to a missing tool through serve_transport, and asserts: exactly 3 responses (the notification produces none), ping result == {}, unsupported method error.code == -32601, and the unknown-tool tools/call result.isError == true. Regression net, no source changes. Verified via cargo test/clippy/fmt. In-lane self-improvement (aurora-agent-utils-agnt-dev-aur-0, 2026-06-24).
creator: aurora:agent-utils:aurora-agent-utils-agnt-dev-aur-0

title: Add test:coverage:summary — ranked low-coverage module report (lcov-based)
description:
Follow-on to bd-5ed02b (test:coverage). Throughout this session I repeatedly ran the raw coverage report and hand-ranked the lowest-covered modules (grep/awk over the long per-file text table) to decide what to test next. A dedicated ranked summary automates that. Node's test runner emits stable lcov coverage (--test-reporter=lcov) with LF/LH, BRF/BRH, FNF/FNH per source file, so the summary can be built on a structured format rather than brittle text-parsing.

Scope: add scripts/coverage-summary.mjs exporting a pure summarizeLcov(lcovText, {threshold}) that parses lcov into per-file {line%, branch%, func%} records, filters to those below a line-coverage threshold (default 90; --all shows everything; --threshold N overrides), sorts worst-first, and prints them plus an overall totals line; the main runs the coverage pass to a temp lcov (or reads an lcov path arg) and prints the summary. Add an npm script test:coverage:summary, a unit test test/coverage-summary.test.js for the pure parser (sample lcov -> expected ranking + percentage math, including the found==0 -> 100% edge), and a one-line README mention. Verified via npm test + the script itself. In-lane dev-ergonomics self-improvement (aurora-agent-utils-agnt-dev-aur-0, 2026-06-24).
creator: aurora:agent-utils:aurora-agent-utils-agnt-dev-aur-0

title: Cap kitty graphics previews to half the viewport height
description:
Operator request from Harry via agnt-ctrl, 2026-05-17: kitty graphics previews should take up no more than 1/2 the viewport height when displayed.

Problem: when a graphics preview is taller than half the visible viewport, there is not enough room left for text. The UI then enters permanent flicker/reflow because the preview and surrounding text compete for terminal space.

Requested behavior:
- Clamp displayed kitty graphics previews to at most half of the current viewport height.
- Preserve aspect ratio and/or use existing scaling behavior within that maximum.
- Ensure enough text rows remain available beneath/around the preview so Pi does not get stuck in a flicker loop.
- Add regression coverage for the viewport-height cap and the no-permanent-flicker layout invariant where practical.

title: Add docs/testing.md documenting test conventions + coverage workflow
description:
agent-utils has 66 test files with strong, consistent conventions (pure-function extraction into per-extension submodules, the assertPng render-smoke pattern, injectable-IO/deterministic testing, a coverage philosophy of pure+injectable vs intentionally-untested ctx/IO/render) plus two coverage dev tools (test:coverage, test:coverage:summary) — but none of it is documented. It is worked by multiple agents, so codifying these conventions reduces inconsistency. This session's verify-don't-assume coverage sweep produced the deepest current context on these patterns.

Scope: add docs/testing.md (public-safe, fits the existing docs/ topic-doc ecosystem) documenting: the node:test framework + how to run (npm test / test:coverage / test:coverage:summary / Rust cargo test); CI gating (ci.yml runs npm test + npm run check + Rust); conventions — pure-function extraction (extensions/<area>/ submodules imported by both extension and tests), render-smoke testing (valid PNG/APNG signature + IHDR dimensions, no visual judgment), injectable IO (execve/spawnImpl/writeFile/env/now params; the null-vs-undefined default gotcha), determinism (inject now/date, avoid toLocaleTimeString/wall-clock); the coverage philosophy (cover pure logic + injectable IO; ctx-coupled handlers, subprocess IO, and render pixel internals are intentionally not unit-tested; use test:coverage:summary to find genuine gaps; verify-don't-assume rather than dismiss a flagged module); and the workflow to add tests for a new extension. Link it from the README Testing section. No source changes; markdown + README link only; verified via npm run check. In-lane self-improvement (aurora-agent-utils-agnt-dev-aur-0, 2026-06-24).
creator: aurora:agent-utils:aurora-agent-utils-agnt-dev-aur-0

title: Add app automation snapshot read and digest tools

title: Add coverage for app-automation editor.js (injection-safety escaping + prepareEditorReplace)
description:
Found via the new npm run test:coverage (bd-5ed02b): extensions/app-automation/editor.js is the lowest-covered small module (~73%, uncovered lines 28-37). buildEditorReplaceScript has only a basic /querySelector/ assertion — its JSON-escaping injection-safety property (selector/text are embedded as JSON string literals so a value containing quotes/newlines/code cannot break out of the browser-injected JS) is NOT tested. prepareEditorReplace (the no-selector guard + readFile/writeFile happy path) has NO direct coverage.

Scope: add test/app-automation-editor.test.js covering: (1) buildEditorReplaceScript embeds selector/text as JSON literals (injection safety) incl. quotes/newlines/code-looking input; the value/contenteditable branches are present; (2) prepareEditorReplace returns {executable:false} with no selector; (3) prepareEditorReplace happy path against a real temp snapshotDir (reads paste.txt, writes editor-replace.js, returns scriptPath/outputPath/textLength), incl. step.inputPath / params.pastePath overrides; temp dir cleaned up after. Regression net + security coverage, no source changes. Verified via npm test. In-lane self-improvement (aurora-agent-utils-agnt-dev-aur-0, 2026-06-24).
creator: aurora:agent-utils:aurora-agent-utils-agnt-dev-aur-0

title: [health] Migrate remaining fixed-sleep waits in realtime-agent.test.js to a shared poll helper
description:
test/realtime-agent.test.js has ~23 remaining fixed-duration setTimeout waits
(await new Promise(r => setTimeout(r, N))) used to sync with async WebSocket
message handling. One of them (the /rt speed test) was already de-flaked with a
bounded poll helper (main 3079fa4, bd-b447e1). The other 23 share the same latent
race risk under concurrent-reintegration load.

title: Headless-node display detection helper (Xvfb/WSLg awareness) for display-dependent extensions
description:
Operator hint (broadcast 2026-06-02, harryaskham): "headless nodes: be creative. you could use xvfb or wslg etc". agent-utils has several display-dependent extensions — tendril-share (window/display capture), android (emulator screenshots), app-automation (Playwright/browser), kitty-image-preview/pi-graphics (terminal graphics) — but NO headless/DISPLAY awareness anywhere (grep for DISPLAY/XVFB/WAYLAND_DISPLAY/WSLg in extensions/ returns nothing). On a headless Linux/WSL node these tools fail or error with no guidance.

First slice (this bead): add a small reusable headless-display detection helper and surface actionable guidance. Detect display availability from env (DISPLAY / WAYLAND_DISPLAY / WSLg via WAYLAND_DISPLAY+/mnt/wslg, macOS = always has a display) and classify as: native-display | wslg | headless-no-display. Expose a helper that returns the classification plus a remediation hint (e.g. "no DISPLAY detected; start a virtual display: `Xvfb :99 -screen 0 1920x1080x24 &` then export DISPLAY=:99", or WSLg note). Unit-test the classification across env permutations. Wiring into tendril/android/app-automation doctor output and an optional auto-Xvfb spawn are FOLLOW-UP slices (separate beads) to keep this bounded.

Filed + claimed by agent-utils worker agnt-dev-1 from the operator broadcast.

Fix: add 8 concise, factual entries (mirroring the README wording, matching the existing schema name/command/audience/purpose/commonActions/sourceOfTruth) to the "Repo-local tools" section where the other repo extensions already live, bump lastReviewed, and regenerate docs/index.html via npm run docs:build. Verified with npm run check (docs:check + lint:workflows). No behavior change; public docs completeness only. Reconciles the published inventory with the package's own README. title: Harden android adb screencap against non-PNG stdout + add capture-path test coverage
description:
Operator-instructed (Harry 2026-06-20: "test and improve anything landed recently on caco android/ios/macos").

Gap in extensions/android.js captureAdbPng(): if `adb ... exec-out screencap -p` exits 0 but emits non-PNG bytes on stdout (a common failure mode: adb prints a warning/error to stdout while still exiting 0), the helper writes those bytes straight to the .png, silently producing a corrupt screenshot. There is also zero automated coverage of the actual capture path (android_emulator_screenshot / android_emulator_stream) — existing tests only cover tool registration, dry-run install/update, help text, and the doctor display line.

Fix:
- Validate the PNG 8-byte signature in captureAdbPng before writing; throw a clear, actionable error (with a short stdout preview) when adb did not return PNG data.
- Add deterministic capture-path tests using a fake `adb` (via the existing ANDROID_ADB_BIN override): valid 1x1 PNG -> file written + image content; non-PNG stdout on exit 0 -> clear rejection (guards the new check); exit 1 -> screencap-failed rejection; stream path captures N frames with the 2-file rotation and reports latestPath.

No device required; fully deterministic and additive. Distinct lane from the realtime flaky investigation owned by agnt-dev-msm-0.
creator: ms-mac:agent-utils:ms-mac-agent-utils-agnt-dev-msm-1

This lets agents warm or verify authenticated browser sessions before running live snapshot bundles. Follow-up hardening under bd-2fc6bf.

title: charCellWidth still over-counts non-Latin combining marks (Hebrew/Arabic/Cyrillic/extensions)
description:
Follow-up to bd-15271b (which zeroed ZWJ/ZWSP/word-joiner/BOM/bidi). extensions/pi-graphics/ansi-width.js charCellWidth() still counts NON-Latin combining marks as width 1, the same over-count class. It zeroes only the Latin combining-diacritical block U+0300..U+036F, but other widely-deployed combining-mark blocks render as zero cells and are currently over-counted, e.g.:

- U+0483..U+0489 (Cyrillic), U+0591..U+05BD / U+05BF / U+05C1.. (Hebrew points)
- U+0610..U+061A, U+064B..U+065F, U+0670 (Arabic)
- U+0300-block already covered; U+1AB0..U+1AFF, U+1DC0..U+1DFF (combining extensions)
- U+20D0..U+20FF (combining symbols), U+FE20..U+FE2F (half marks)

Lower realistic impact than the ZWJ fix (pi status/footer text is mostly ASCII + emoji, rarely Hebrew/Arabic), so deferred as a draft rather than landed immediately. If picked up, prefer a small curated range table (not a full Unicode property import) consistent with the existing approximate-by-design comment, and extend test/ansi-width.test.js. owning-checkout:agent-utils, lane:pi-graphics. Filed via self-improvement (aurora-agent-utils-agnt-dev-aur-0, 2026-06-24).

Update (aurora, 2026-06-25): the SAFE subset of this bead — the dedicated all-non-spacing combining-mark blocks (Cyrillic U+0483-0489, U+1AB0-1AFF, U+1DC0-1DFF, U+20D0-20FF, U+FE20-2F) — was implemented and landed via bd-c7f504 (origin/main 24b4a0f). REMAINING SCOPE for this bead is now ONLY the interspersed-spacing scripts: Hebrew (U+0591-05C7) and Arabic (U+0610.. / U+064B.. / U+06D6..), whose blocks interleave SPACING punctuation/format chars (e.g. U+05BE/05C0/05C3/05C6, U+06DD) that must NOT be zeroed. These require careful per-codepoint ranges (not whole-block) and remain deferred; lower realistic impact in pi UI text.
creator: aurora:agent-utils:aurora-agent-utils-agnt-dev-aur-0

title: pi-graphics editor border duplicates above native read-image + FD-28/29 unmanaged-mode warnings (shared kitty namespace with pi-tui)
description:
Operator-observed (Harry, 2026-06-07): when an agent uses Pi's native `read` tool on a PNG, the image renders correctly, but (a) Node prints "File descriptor 28/29 opened in unmanaged mode twice" / "closed but not opened in unmanaged mode" just before the image, and (b) the pi-graphics editor TOP border graphic is duplicated/repeated above the rendered image — consistent with a kitty image/placement overlap.

ROOT-CAUSE ANALYSIS (code-grounded):
- Pi native renderer `pi-tui/dist/terminal-image.js` `allocateImageId()` = `Math.floor(Math.random()*0xfffffffe)+1` — a RANDOM id across the full 32-bit range [1,0xFFFFFFFF]. Its own comment: "Uses random IDs to avoid collisions between different module instances (e.g., main app vs extensions)." Read images are transmitted with `a=T` (direct cursor placement) and scroll the screen.
- Our extension `extensions/kitty-graphics.js` allocates editor-border image ids via `stableKittyImageId()` = FNV-1a hash with forced non-zero MSB, ALSO spanning the full 32-bit range, and renders the border with Unicode placeholder cells (U+10EEEE) on virtual placements (`stableKittyPlaceholderPlacementId()` lives in the 0x800000..0xFFFFFF band).
- Both pools therefore share the same tty-global 32-bit kitty image namespace. Pi's random ids CAN land on our deterministic border ids (rare per single read). More importantly for the *repeatable* duplication: when pi-tui's `a=T` read image scrolls the screen, our border's Unicode-placeholder cells scroll up into the scrollback and keep rendering the border image at the scrolled position (placeholder cells render their image wherever they appear). That is the most likely mechanism for "border repeats above the image" on every read.
- FD-28/29 "unmanaged mode" warnings are a Node/libuv-level message (string is NOT present in agent-utils or pi source). Our extension writes ONLY through Pi's managed writer (`ctx.ui.write` / `ctx.ui.terminal.write` — see pi-graphics.js resolveGraphicsWriter); we never open our own /dev/tty handle. So the duplicate-fd wrapping originates in Pi/pi-tui core terminal/image tty handling, NOT this extension — that half is an upstream Pi report.

RELATED: bd-a2cc54 (closed) "Avoid Pi graphics kitty ID collisions across live tmux instances" handled a sibling collision class (multiple tmux instances of our OWN extension). This bead is the cross-module variant: our extension vs Pi's native read-image renderer in the SAME terminal.

PROPOSED FIX DIRECTION (agent-utils side):
1. Make the editor border resilient to transcript-image scroll: after a scrolling transcript image is emitted, re-assert/repaint our owned border placements and/or ensure placeholder cells are not left in rows that scroll into scrollback (scope the border image to the managed editor region so scrolled copies are cleared).
2. Reduce namespace overlap: keep our extension image ids in a reserved high sub-band and add a regression test; lowers (not eliminates) random-collision probability with pi-tui's allocator.
3. Track the FD-28/29 unmanaged-mode tty double-wrap + `a=T` scroll behavior as an upstream Pi/pi-tui issue (not fixable from agent-utils).

ACCEPTANCE: reading an image with the native read tool no longer leaves a duplicated editor border above it; no agent-utils-originated tty fd double-open; regression test covers extension image-id banding. Verifying the no-duplicate-border outcome requires a live kitty/ghostty terminal (cannot be confirmed headlessly).
creator: ms-mac:agent-utils:ms-mac-agent-utils-agnt-dev-0

title: Beads authority proxy times out fleet-wide under concurrent resume/fan-out load
description:
While operating under the operator's fleet-wide "resume all work" broadcast (~19 agents on ms-mac resuming at once), the beads authority proxy (helsinki, https://helsinki.miku-owl.ts.net:12100) began timing out on many ordinary read+write calls: `caco bd list`, `caco bd show`, `caco agent status set`, and `caco choices present/list` returned MCP error -32001 "Request timed out" and the CLI returned exit 124. The same calls succeeded moments earlier and recovered shortly after, so this reads as a load/contention spike (thundering herd) in the authority proxy path rather than a hard outage.

Friction shape: a single coordinated "resume" pulse degrades the shared beads authority enough that idle workers cannot reliably read the board or set status, which is exactly when coordination matters most. Per bd-3ae61c I did not build client-side retry choreography; I backed off, recorded a health note, and proceeded with local-only work, but the surface itself was briefly unusable.

Suggested directions: bound/queue or rate-limit cross-node beads authority requests, add jitter/backoff on the proxy side, raise the proxy timeout, or shed/deprioritize non-mutating reads under load so claims/status still get through. Worth confirming whether the in-process cluster_mtls proxy serializes requests in a way that amplifies head-of-line blocking under concurrent fan-out.

Observed on ms-mac, 2026-06-12T21:12Z..2026-06-13T08:30Z window, agent ms-mac-agent-utils-agnt-dev-1. Filed via reflect-session mixin from bd-3623f7 session.

title: [health] Migrate skill-server off deprecated serde_yaml 0.9.34 to a maintained YAML crate
description:

`serde_yaml::Error`, and several test fixtures).

title: Image-preview sidebar: width-fit aspect ratio + greedy multi-image pages with headers + scrollback fix
description:
Operator (Harry) request for the kitty-image-preview rightOverlay/sidebar mode:
1. BUG: sidebar mode clobbers the left-hand scrollback — cannot scroll back.
2. BUG: images lose original aspect ratio; should fit column WIDTH and scale HEIGHT proportionally (buildSidePanelLayout currently shrinks image WIDTH via fitImageColumnsForRows to fit a row budget).
3. FEATURE: greedily pack many width-fit images into the side widget; paginate by pages-of-images, each with a name header; reflow on resize.
Touch points: extensions/kitty-image-preview/layout.js, extensions/kitty-image-preview.js (renderSidePanelImageLines, renderTuiWithSidePanel), widget.js, status-line.js. Unit-test via test/kitty-layout.test.js.

PROGRESS 2026-06-12: Part B (width-fit aspect ratio) DONE and landed on origin/main at ad95b54 — added layout.containImageBox aspect-preserving contain fit + wired into renderSidePanelImageLines; kills the vertical squish and fills column width when not height-bound; +4 unit tests in test/kitty-layout.test.js (35/35 kitty tests pass).

REMAINING (bead stays in_progress; both need live-terminal validation, cannot be validated headlessly):
- (C) greedy multi-image pages with per-image name headers + page-level pagination + resize reflow (multi-placement kitty render);
- (A) left-hand scrollback clobber in rightOverlay mode.

PROGRESS 2026-06-12 (cont): Part C foundation landed — added pure greedy page-packing primitives packImagesIntoPages + pageForIndex in extensions/kitty-image-preview/layout.js (commit 7cffcd4 on agent branch, pending reintegrate). Greedily packs aspect-fit width-fit images into pages of a row budget, one name-header row reserved per image, overflow to a new page when the next header+image block exceeds the page, each image height-bounded by page-minus-header so a tall image never spans more than one page; pageForIndex maps global item index to its packed page for pagination. Reflow on resize is automatic (recompute from live terminal dims each render). +6 unit tests (24/24 kitty-layout, 126/126 across kitty suites).

title: Cover kitty-graphics pure helpers: parsePngDimensions, chunkBase64, controlDataToString, estimateRowsForImage, passthrough wrap/unwrap
description:
Richest verify-don't-assume find: extensions/kitty-graphics.js (the core of the kitty image-preview feature, 90% line / 85% func) has many untested PURE encode/parse/build helpers. These underpin image transmission and sizing, so a bug shows broken/blank images.

Untested pure functions to pin:
- parsePngDimensions(buffer): PNG signature+IHDR parse -> {width,height}; undefined for short/non-PNG. Cross-checks with transparentPixelPngBase64() -> 1x1.
- bufferToBase64 / textToBase64: base64 encoders.
- chunkBase64(b64, size): splits into <=size chunks ([""] for empty).
- controlDataToString(control): serializes k=v,... ; throws on invalid control key; drops undefined.
- estimateRowsForImage({imageWidth,imageHeight,columns,maxRows,minRows}): aspect->rows math, clamped to [minRows,maxRows]; default 16 when inputs missing.
- isSupportedKittyPngPath(path): .png/.apng (case-insensitive).
- wrapForPassthrough(seq, mode, env): tmux DCS-wrap (doubles ESC) / none passthrough / throws unsupported.
- unwrapTmuxGraphicsPassthrough(text): strips DCS start + un-doubles ESC for the APC parser (NOT a strict inverse — leaves the DCS terminator, which the parser ignores; assert the real contract, not exact roundtrip).

Scope: add a test block to test/kitty-graphics.test.js covering the above with locked-in values (e.g. chunkBase64('ABCDEFGHIJ',4)->['ABCD','EFGH','IJ']; estimate tall->maxRows, wide->minRows; controlDataToString invalid-key throw; parsePngDimensions(transparent)->{1,1}). Regression net, no source changes. Verified via npm test + coverage. In-lane self-improvement (aurora-agent-utils-agnt-dev-aur-0, 2026-06-24).
creator: aurora:agent-utils:aurora-agent-utils-agnt-dev-aur-0

title: copilot-auth-refresh retry budget resets every cycle because the injected retry message mutates the budget key (real root cause of bd-efcf8d storm)
description:
Root cause for the residual copilot-auth-retry-storm (the real reason bd-efcf8d kept reproducing "despite landed bd-364a68 fix").

bd-364a68 added a bounded retry budget in extensions/copilot-auth-refresh.js keyed on the underlying request text (retryBudgetText / retryBudgetCount), resetting to 0 whenever the request text changes. Its own description and bd-efcf8d both assumed "the live retry text is constant so the text-keyed budget should bound it." That assumption is FALSE.

The agent_end handler extracts retryText from the most recent user message, then INJECTS a follow-up user message whose body is:
  "GitHub Copilot auth was refreshed after a transient missing-token error. Retry the previous request now:\n\n${retryText}"

On the next copilot auth failure, that injected message IS the most recent user message, so the newly-extracted retryText now starts with the "GitHub Copilot auth was refreshed..." prefix and DIFFERS from the prior retryBudgetText. The budget therefore resets to 0 every cycle, MAX_COPILOT_AUTH_RETRIES is never reached, and the storm continues unbounded — exactly what was observed live in ms-mac dev-1 sessions (6+ stacked injections in one window). A freshly-reloaded process still storms, which is why bd-efcf8d's "stale process" hypothesis did not fully resolve it.

Evidence: extensions/copilot-auth-refresh.js lines ~117-138. The existing "no storm" regression test (test/copilot-auth-refresh.test.js) feeds a STATIC lastUser text across failures, so it never exercised the feedback loop and passed while the real path stormed.

Fix: normalize retryText before computing the budget key/text by stripping the auto-injection prefix (and any nested repetition of it), so a re-failure of an already-injected retry shares the ORIGINAL request's budget. Then distinct injected timestamps with the recovered-same underlying request correctly cap at MAX_COPILOT_AUTH_RETRIES. Add a regression test that simulates the real loop: failure -> capture injected text -> feed it back as the next lastUser -> assert injections cap at the bound and a terminal error notify fires.

Discovered live in ms-mac dev-1 session 2026-06-05; supersedes the "stale process" framing of bd-efcf8d as the primary cause.
creator: ms-mac:agent-utils:ms-mac-agent-utils-agnt-dev-1

title: Bug: /rt start vad does not switch Pi to realtime model
description:
Harry observed during live Pulse/RT testing that `/rt start vad` did not switch the active Pi model away from the current text model (gpt55) to `openai-realtime/gpt-realtime-2`. The result was STT-like behaviour: the transcript was delivered to the normal text agent, but the special realtime model/audio path did not receive the audio itself, so full realtime speech behaviour was effectively going somewhere else / not attached to the agent conversation.

Expected behaviour:
- `/rt start vad` (and `/rt`, `/rt ptt`, `/rt nolisten`) should switch Pi to the realtime provider/model before or while opening the realtime session, unless explicitly in STT-only mode.
- `/rt stt ...` should remain transcription-only and should not switch the current model.
- Status/doctor should make it obvious whether the active model is realtime or STT-only.

Observed environment during the same debugging session:
- PI_RT_AUDIO_BACKEND=pulse
- PULSE_SERVER=sgu24.miku-owl.ts.net:4713
- Pulse default source was source.bluetooth, and source.bluetooth was RUNNING.
- Transcripts were reaching the agent, proving microphone capture/transcription was at least partially functioning.

Likely investigation area:
- `extensions/realtime-agent.js::startRealtime(ctx, { listenMode, sttOnly })` around the model-switching path.
- Ensure `startRealtime` verifies `pi.setModel(...)` success and reports a hard/visible error if the realtime model is not found or cannot be selected.
- Add tests covering `/rt start vad` from a non-realtime current model and `/rt stt vad` preserving the current model.

Related beads: bd-36c9e2 (agent-side realtime call and Pulse controls), bd-5d068b (env-style slash/tool args).

Filed from l7fipzlhc4u4d0t9 at Harry's request during live RT/Pulse testing.
