title: Add env-style key=value parser for symmetrical slash command and tool args
description:
Harry requested that realtime Pulse routing be runtime-settable both from slash commands and from the agent tool view once agent-side realtime tools are exposed.

Observed semantics check:
- Pi extension docs show pi.registerCommand(name, { handler(args, ctx) }) receives a raw args string; I did not find a built-in reusable key=value / env-style parser for slash-command args in the documented Pi command API.
- Pi tools already use structured Type.Object parameters via pi.registerTool(...), which is the right canonical shape for agent-side tool arguments.

Desired behaviour:
- Add or reuse a shared parser/helper for env-like command syntax such as /rt backend=pulse source=source.bluetooth sink=...
- Order must not matter: /rt source=source.bluetooth backend=pulse should be equivalent.
- Spaces should delimit tokens but not be semantic otherwise.
- Support shell/env-like quoted values, e.g. key="value in quotes", including useful escaping rules and clear errors for malformed input.
- Realtime slash commands should map parsed key/value args into the same normalized parameter object that the agent-side realtime tool uses, so command UX and tool UX stay symmetrical.
- Preserve existing positional forms where sensible (/rt start vad, /rt backend pulse) while documenting precedence and ambiguity handling.
- Realtime should include runtime-settable Pulse fields at minimum: backend, pulse server, source, sink; changes should affect newly spawned mic/playback processes and status/doctor output should report the resolved routing without leaking secrets.
- Prefer an existing Pi parser/semantics if one exists in a newer API; otherwise implement the helper in agent-utils as shared library code and document it as the convention for future command/tool pairs.

Related existing bead: bd-36c9e2 (Expose agent-side realtime call and Pulse device controls). This bead focuses on the shared env-style arg parsing and command/tool symmetry needed by that work.

Filed from l7fipzlhc4u4d0t9 at Harry's request after Pulse Bluetooth mic testing discussion.
creator: ms-mac:agent-utils:l7fipzlhc4u4d0t9

title: Auto-mount Pi graphics rendered terminal scene
description: Follow-up operator request from Harry: Pi kitty graphics still are not visibly different enough. title: Extract shared tendril tool-result builder for capture/describe data envelope
description:
While implementing bd-668a82 I noticed extensions/tendril-share.js builds an
identical tool-result `data: { outputPath, target, envelope, sourceMachine,
pathOnly, includeList }` object inline in both the tendril_capture and
tendril_describe tool execute() handlers. Threading any new field (e.g.
sourceMachine) requires editing two near-identical lines, and they are
indistinguishable enough that unique-match edits need follower-context
disambiguation. A small helper like `buildTendrilToolData(captured, params)`
(or a shared content+data builder) would remove the duplication and make future
result-shape changes single-site. Low priority polish. Filed via reflect-session
mixin from bd-668a82 session.

title: Add opt-in test:coverage npm script + README Testing section
description:
The repo runs tests via `node --test` (npm test) but has no coverage-reporting script, and the README has no Testing section documenting how to run the suite. During this session's review work I repeatedly hand-grepped test/ to find untested modules; a coverage report makes that instant. Node (CI uses 20; supports --experimental-test-coverage) produces a per-file line/branch/func + uncovered-lines report; verified it runs cleanly on the full suite (overall 92.44% line coverage) and pinpoints lower-covered files (e.g. affordances.js 71%, editor.js 73%).

Scope: add an opt-in `test:coverage` npm script (node --test --experimental-test-coverage) — NOT wired into CI and NOT a gating threshold (purely informational, avoids changing the merge gate). Add a short README "Testing" section documenting `npm test` and `npm run test:coverage`. No source/behavior change. Verified via npm test + npm run check. In-lane dev-ergonomics self-improvement (aurora-agent-utils-agnt-dev-aur-0, 2026-06-24).
creator: aurora:agent-utils:aurora-agent-utils-agnt-dev-aur-0

title: Add large typing-reactive Pi graphics editor cursor glow

Desired behavior: cursor Unicode placeholder remains the anchor, but the actual kitty placement is larger than one cell, approximately 6 columns by 3 rows, centered on the cursor so the cursor line remains in the middle of the middle cell. The image should be mostly transparent with a pronounced heat/glow extending slightly into neighbouring rows. Infer inter-character typing speed/WPM from editor rerenders/input movement, change color/frame based on recent typing speed, control glow radius, and tick down after typing stops.

title: Cover playwright-bridge pure builders: playwrightCliCommand, prepareDomExtractStep, authMissingHint
description:
Verified (not assumed) gaps from test:coverage:summary in extensions/app-automation/playwright-bridge.js (79% line, uncovered 6-11, 48-63, 80-81): these are pure/injectable command-builder + analyzer functions, not browser IO.

1. playwrightCliCommand(env) (6-11) untested — pure: returns env.APP_AUTOMATION_PLAYWRIGHT_CLI or DEFAULT_PLAYWRIGHT_CLI.
2. prepareDomExtractStep(step, params, {snapshotDir, actionId, scripts}) (48-63) untested — async; resolves script source (params.extractorScript / scripts[step.script] / step.script), writes an extractor file into snapshotDir when present, and resolves output path (absolute kept, relative joined to snapshotDir). Testable with a temp snapshotDir.
3. authMissingHint(result) (80-81) — pure auth-detection over stdout/stderr; one branch uncovered.

Scope: add tests to test/app-automation.test.js: playwrightCliCommand env override + default; prepareDomExtractStep no-script (output-only, no file written, relative path joined to snapshotDir + absolute kept) and with-script (writes <actionId>-extractor.js into a mkdtemp snapshotDir, sets nextStep.scriptFile + paths.scriptPath); authMissingHint true cases (sign in / unauthorized / forbidden in stdout or stderr) + false. Regression net, no source changes. Verified via npm test + coverage. In-lane self-improvement (aurora-agent-utils-agnt-dev-aur-0, 2026-06-24).
creator: aurora:agent-utils:aurora-agent-utils-agnt-dev-aur-0

title: Add C=1 to inline Kitty display placement commands
description:
Audit finding from Harry's Kitty graphics protocol review: the Kitty protocol moves the terminal cursor after image placement unless `C=1` is specified. `extensions/kitty-graphics.js::buildPngDisplayCommand()` emits inline `a=T` display commands without `C=1`, and `kitty-image-preview` prefixes them into rendered text rows in cursor-placement mode. This can desynchronize surrounding TUI layout.

Add `C=1` where display commands are embedded inline, or make it default for `buildPngDisplayCommand()`, and add tests asserting cursor movement policy is explicit.

Filed from agnt-dev-2 Kitty protocol audit session.
creator: ms-mac:agent-utils:ms-mac-agent-utils-agnt-dev-2

title: Fix extension-tool-schemas.md drift: add self-compact.js shim row + note plain-JSON extensions
description:
Concrete doc drift found by verifying docs/extension-tool-schemas.md's shim-vs-TypeBox table against the code (the doc itself says to regenerate via grep -l 'lib/tool-schema' vs grep -l '@sinclair/typebox' extensions/*.js if it drifts).

Findings:
- self-compact.js registers a tool using the lib/tool-schema.js shim and is import-tested (test/self-compact.test.js) but is MISSING from the shim table. Genuine drift (self-compact was added after the doc was written).
- web-search-models.js is NOT drift: it mentions @sinclair/typebox only in a comment, registers no tool, and is a pure data module — correctly absent from the table.
- Accuracy gap: effort.js, m.js, tendril-share.js register tools with neither the shim nor TypeBox (plain JSON-schema parameters) and are also import-testable, so the doc's binary shim-vs-TypeBox framing is incomplete — the real rule is 'avoid a direct @sinclair/typebox import'; both the shim and plain JSON keep an extension import-testable.

Scope: update docs/extension-tool-schemas.md — add the self-compact.js row to the shim section of the table, and add a one-line note that some extensions register tools with plain JSON-schema parameters (neither shim nor TypeBox) and are likewise import-testable. Doc-only, public-safe; verified via npm run check. In-lane self-improvement (aurora-agent-utils-agnt-dev-aur-0, 2026-06-24).
creator: aurora:agent-utils:aurora-agent-utils-agnt-dev-aur-0

title: Audit Kitty graphics protocol implementation for correctness and efficiency
description:
Harry requested a full pass over https://sw.kovidgoyal.net/kitty/graphics-protocol and the agent-utils Kitty/Pi graphics implementation.

Scope:
1. Audit protocol-following correctness first: escape framing, chunking, upload/placement/delete semantics, ids, Unicode placeholders, tmux passthrough, animation/APNG semantics, z-index/layers, cursor behavior, query/cleanup handling, and fallbacks.
2. Audit efficiency second: redundant uploads, payload sizes, caching, placement reuse, cleanup cost, animation frame handling, and log/test noise.
3. Produce an operator-readable findings report with concrete issues and suggested follow-up beads/patches if necessary.

Requested directly by Harry on 2026-05-23.
creator: ms-mac:agent-utils:ms-mac-agent-utils-agnt-dev-2

title: Diagnose ms-dev PowerShell CDP run timeouts per target

This suggests ms-dev reachability can succeed while the Windows PowerShell/CDP script hangs or blocks. Add finer-grained remote-stage diagnostics/timeouts inside the PowerShell script (for example per-target progress/status, CDP list/new/evaluate stage errors, and/or per-target timeout budget) so a single page/CDP hang does not obscure all surfaces. title: Pi inbox follow-up delivery still misses streamingBehavior
description:
Operator observed runtime error while router/inbox follow-up was being delivered: Extension "<runtime>" error: Agent is already processing. Specify streamingBehavior ('steer' or 'followUp') to queue the message. The pi-inbox mixin expects inbox nudges to use Pi's non-stopping follow-up channel, so inspect/fix the extension path that injects messages while the agent is already processing. Filed from operator report on 2026-05-16.

\nInvestigation note: in this agent's materialized ../.pi-agent/extensions/caco-inbox.mjs, deliverMessages called sendUserMessage(prompt, { deliverAs: DELIVERY_MODE }) without streamingBehavior. Local hotfix applied in this live session to use { deliverAs: DELIVERY_MODE, streamingBehavior: DELIVERY_MODE }; ../.pi-agent/extensions/caco-inbox.test.mjs passes via node --test. This is not a landed repository fix because the canonical pi-inbox source appears to live in the Cacophony project, not this agent-utils checkout. Permanent fix should be applied to .cacophony/pi/inbox/extensions/caco-inbox.mjs in the Cacophony repo and rolled out to materialized agents.

Agent ms-mac-agent-utils-agnt-dev attempted to hydrate this from the agent-utils project on 2026-05-17. The bound checkout has no tracked caco-inbox source; the canonical files are visible in the Cacophony repository under .cacophony/pi/inbox/extensions/caco-inbox.mjs and .cacophony/pi/inbox/extensions/caco-inbox.test.mjs. Moving to project cacophony so a worker with the correct checkout can land the repository fix. Do not close from agent-utils.
creator: ms-mac:agent-utils:l7fipzlhc4u4d0t9

title: Label agent-utils drafts with owning-checkout/lane so idle workers can filter claimable-in-lane work
description:
Worker-side draft-triage friction observed repeatedly during agent-utils burndown (ms2-1, 2026-06-22 session). Even correctly-filed agent-utils drafts mix three categories that determine claimability, and nothing on the bead distinguishes them, so an idle worker must hydrate each draft (caco bd show) one at a time to decide.

Categories:
(1) fix lives in THIS agent-utils extensions checkout -> claimable in-lane;
(2) fix lives in caco-CLI / Pi-core / cacophony-daemon repos reachable only from another checkout/scope -> not landable here;
(3) in an active peer specialist lane (e.g. pi-graphics behavior-over-source-regex) -> should not be force-claimed.

Suggested fix: adopt a label convention at filing/triage time, e.g. owning-checkout:agent-utils | owning-checkout:caco-cli | out-of-checkout, and/or lane:kitty | lane:pi-graphics, so an idle worker can server-side filter claimable-in-lane drafts with caco bd list --label owning-checkout:agent-utils instead of N hydrations.

Complements bd-fec29a (filing-time path/project validation) from the consumer/filter side rather than the validation side. Filed via reflect-session / self-improvement from the 2026-06-22 burndown session (agent ms-dev-2-agent-utils-agnt-dev-ms2-1).

title: caco msg send rejects documented unambiguous short agent-id target
description:
`caco msg send --target <short-agent-id>` rejected an unqualified current-project short agent id with `invalid_request: --target '<id>' is not a valid direct-message recipient; use <project>:<agent-id> or <node>:<project>:<agent-id>`, even though the same agent had just messaged this project and the `caco_msg_send` tool/CLI help states "Project-qualified IDs are accepted; an unambiguous current-project short agent ID is expanded automatically."

Repro: from an agent-utils worker, `caco msg send --project agent-utils --target ms-mac-agent-utils-agnt-dev-0 --body "..."` errored; the project-qualified form `agent-utils:ms-mac-agent-utils-agnt-dev-0` worked.

Friction shape: a documented convenience (short-id auto-expansion) does not actually work, so callers must already know the project/node-qualified form. Either implement the documented unambiguous short-id expansion in `msg send`, or correct the tool/CLI help to require the qualified form. Minor but a clear doc/behaviour mismatch that costs a round-trip.

Filed via reflect-session mixin from bd-d433ca session (ms-mac, agent-utils).
creator: ms-mac:agent-utils:ms-mac-agent-utils-agnt-dev-1

title: Document canonical web-search Python test command (uv run --extra test pytest)
description:
While adding web-search MCP tests (bd-c658d6), I hit friction running the Python
test suite. `python3 -m pytest` fails with ModuleNotFoundError (src-layout, and
the mcp dependency is not installed in the ambient nix python env).
`PYTHONPATH=src python3 -m pytest` then fails on `No module named 'mcp'`. The
working invocation is `uv run --extra test python -m pytest` (uses uv.lock,
builds a .venv, installs mcp + pytest). This is not documented in
web-search/README.md, the root README, or AGENTS.md, so each agent has to
rediscover it by trial and error.

Suggested fix: document the canonical web-search test command
(`uv run --extra test python -m pytest`, run from web-search/) in
web-search/README.md and/or a Build-and-test section, mirroring how
docs/skill-server/README.md documents the cargo test commands. Optionally add a
package.json or flake test app entry so it is discoverable alongside the JS
`node --test` suite.

Filed via reflect-session mixin from bd-c658d6 session (agnt-dev).
creator: ms-mac:agent-utils:ms-mac-agent-utils-agnt-dev

title: Add skill-server unit tests for NotFound contract and skill-file routing
description:
The skill-server crate's unit tests cover MCP-tool routing, the MCP server
surface, and (newly) the ambiguous-route human output, but two documented
behaviors have no unit coverage:

1. The NotFound contract. docs/skill-server/README.md states "A miss returns a
   structured not_found response instead of guessing." There is no unit test
   asserting execute_meta_request returns RouteStatus::NotFound with no selected
   route for an unknown selector. This is a core promise of the design and
   should be pinned so a future routing change cannot silently start guessing.

2. Skill-file routing. The RouteKind::SkillFile path (discover_skill_files +
   domain/name derivation, e.g. web-search.md -> name web-search, domain web)
   is exercised by no unit test. Integration tests use an empty skill_paths
   config.

Add two unit tests: one asserting the NotFound human/status contract, and one
that writes a skill file into a tempdir-backed skill_path and asserts it routes
as a SkillFile match by both name and domain. Pure test additions, no behavior
change. Filed as proactive coverage polish under operator overnight directive
(no open beads). agnt-dev session, follow-up to bd-b08fa2.
creator: ms-mac:agent-utils:ms-mac-agent-utils-agnt-dev

title: Add render smoke coverage for pi-graphics affordances.js (valid PNG + dimensions)
description:
Found via npm run test:coverage: extensions/pi-graphics/affordances.js is ~71% covered — many render* functions that produce the pi-graphics chrome PNGs are untested (renderAccentBar, renderGlowPanel, renderGlowPanelFrames, renderGradientBorder, renderEditorCursorVline, renderEditorBox, renderEditorRailFrame, renderFooterDividerPng). Scope: add test/affordances-smoke.test.js asserting each render function returns a valid PNG (0x89 'PNG' signature) of the expected dimensions columns*CELL_PX_W x rows*CELL_PX_H (using the exported CELL_PX_W/CELL_PX_H), frame objects expose widthPx/heightPx with a pixels buffer of widthPx*heightPx*4, and the *Frames variants return an array of the requested length. Regression net, no source changes. Verified via npm test + coverage. In-lane self-improvement (aurora-agent-utils-agnt-dev-aur-0, 2026-06-24).

title: fitFooterSegments returns overflowing layout instead of null when segment minimums cannot fit
description:
extensions/pi-graphics/footer-layout.js fitFooterSegments() violates its own documented contract. The doc comment says it "Returns ... or null when even the per-segment minimums plus the inter-segment dividers cannot fit", but the only null guard is `if (budget < rawSegments.length) return null;` which assumes each segment needs >=1 cell. When segments have min > 1 and the sum of minimums plus dividers exceeds the width (while width - dividers >= segment count), the shrink loop bottoms out at the minimums with used > budget, hits its `!changed` break, skips absorbSpare (used < budget is false), and RETURNS the overflowing segments instead of null.

Empirically verified: 2 segments each min=5, FOOTER_DIVIDER_WIDTH=3 (min renderable width = 13), width=12 -> fitFooterSegments returns segments of width [5,5] whose footerSegmentsWidth() = 13 > 12, instead of null. On a real narrow terminal this overflows the status line rather than letting the caller fall back to the documented null path.

Fix: after the shrink loop, add `if (used > budget) return null;` so the documented can't-fit-at-minimums contract is honored. Minimal, honors the existing comment; the old count-based guard stays as a fast path. Add a footer-layout test asserting null when sum(mins)+dividers > width, and confirm the full npm test suite (incl. pi-graphics footer tests) stays green so no caller relied on the overflow behavior. title: [health] restorePublicState clamps index against pre-filter snapshot length
description:
extensions/kitty-image-preview/state.js restorePublicState() clamps the restored
index against the RAW snapshot item count, before items are filtered:

  state.index = clampInteger(snapshot.index, 0, 0, Math.max(0, snapshot.items.length - 1));
  state.items = snapshot.items.filter((item) => typeof item?.path === "string").map(...);

If the snapshot contains items that get dropped during filtering (e.g. entries
without a string path), state.index can end up pointing past the end of the
filtered state.items array. summarizeCurrent() then returns "No image is
loaded." even though valid items exist.

This is a benign latent edge-case (requires a snapshot with both invalid items
AND a high index), characterized in test/kitty-state.test.js ("restorePublicState
clamps index, filters bad items..."). Possible fix: clamp index against the
filtered items length instead of the raw snapshot length.

Low priority. Filed from the test-health pass (bd-c1c555) in agnt-dev-2's
state.js extraction area. Lane: health.
creator: ms-mac:agent-utils:ms-mac-agent-utils-agnt-dev

title: pi-graphics: animated editor border + box chrome (relative-anchor)
description:
Two related followups for bd-553242, scoped after static editor border ships:

- Make piGraphics.editor.style="animated" actually animate: the relative-anchor + a=a tick approach uploads frames once, creates virtual U=1 anchors per edge and non-virtual relative placements, then ticks frame select. Current symptom: anchor cells render as yellow fallback glyphs instead of being invisible kitty placeholders, and frames do not advance. Verify image-id high byte is encoded in placeholder diacritics; consider whether Ghostty repaints non-virtual relative placements on a=a,c=N or whether we need another approach.

- Box chrome for built-in message components (assistant/tool/bash/user/custom/skill/branch/compaction). Currently gated behind PI_GRAPHICS_AUTO_BOX_CHROME=1 because:
  - prepending the placeholder cell inflates visible line width and triggers TUI 'Rendered line exceeds terminal width'. Need to replace first visible cell instead of prepending.
  - anchor cells visible (yellow) instead of invisible; same root cause as editor border.
  - need to verify the jiti dual-module concern is moot in practice (we monkey-patch prototype, which should reach Pi's instantiated class because jiti aliases @earendil-works/pi-coding-agent to the same packageIndex Pi imports from).

Acceptance:
- piGraphics.editor.style="animated" produces a smoothly animating editor border in Ghostty under tmux.
- PI_GRAPHICS_AUTO_BOX_CHROME=1 yields theme-toned translucent backgrounds + side pillars around every built-in message type with no width inflation crashes, anchor cells invisible.
- Defaults remain: static editor border, box-chrome off, until both above land cleanly.
creator: ms-mac-agent-utils-agnt-dev

title: caco scratch append --text fails with "unsupported flag" when value starts with "- " (markdown bullet)
description:
Repro: `caco scratch append --note-id X --text "- some bullet"` fails with `error: unsupported flag: - some bullet` (exit 2). The leading `- ` of a markdown bullet is parsed as a CLI flag rather than the --text value.

Impact: the persistent-agent health-note protocol literally instructs agents to append `- <utc> | node=... | ...` bullet entries via `caco scratch append`. Following that guidance verbatim hits an opaque "unsupported flag" error exactly during an outage, when scratch reliability matters most (observed live this session during the ms-mac daemon flap on 2026-06-22).

Workarounds that work: use the equals form `--text=- ...`, or start the entry with a non-dash char (e.g. `* `).

Suggested fix: set clap `allow_hyphen_values` on the `--text` arg (and likely the sibling `--body`/`--text` string flags such as `caco msg ... --body "- ..."`), or add a hint to the error mentioning `--text=<value>` / `--` so hyphen-leading values are accepted. 

Filed via reflect/self-improvement during idle from the ms-mac agent-utils dev worker (msm-1). Durable copy in scratch note `improve:agent-utils-caco-friction`.

Sibling instance (aurora-agent-utils-agnt-dev-aur-0, 2026-06-24): the same leading-dash-parsed-as-flag class also hits `caco bd update --append-description` when the appended text starts with a dash, e.g. a value beginning "--- Triage ...". Via the MCP caco_bd_update bridge the text was dispatched as unrecognised flags ("received unrecognised flag(s): --- Triage diagnosis ..."), not as the append value. Workaround that worked: route the body through `--description-stdin` (heredoc) instead of an inline value, which bypasses flag parsing. Suggests the fix should normalise leading-dash text values across caco bd/scratch verbs (and/or the MCP arg bridge) by forcing them through a stdin/value path rather than argv.
creator: ms-mac:agent-utils:ms-mac-agent-utils-agnt-dev-msm-1
spoken_name: caco scratch markdown bullet flag parsing

title: Release v1.2.0 (35 commits since v1.1.2; linear-extra MCP server)

- Suite grown to 587 tests, all green modulo one known timing flake

Release convention is project-native: bump package.json, land on main under the merge-queue gate, publish annotated git tag to canonical GitHub via the daemon checkout. flake.nix and crate versions are left untouched per convention they version the skill-server package not the project.

Executed autonomously under the standing work-autonomously directive, with the agent-utils release convention owned by dev-2.

title: Distinguish benign drained-queue idle from real stalls in agent summary stuck detection
description: During agnt-ctrl monitoring, persistent worker agents that are healthy but have NO open/ready beads to claim get flagged '[SEVERE] Potentially Stuck' in 'caco agent summary' purely because last_tool_activity ages past stale_after (1800s). This is expected/correct idle (drained ready queue), not a stall, but it looks identical to a genuinely hung worker. The controller must re-derive 'is there ready work for this worker?' every sweep to tell them apart. Suggested fix: in stuck detection, downweight or separately label persistent workers that are idle while their claimable ready-bead set is empty (e.g. 'idle (no ready work)' vs '[SEVERE] stuck'). Could check: worker has no in_progress claim AND project has zero open/ready beads -> benign idle, not severe. Workers idle while HOLDING an in_progress bead they should advance remain worth flagging. Filed via reflect-session mixin from agnt-ctrl monitoring session (context: agent-utils pool idle 5h on drained queue while bd-e1914a blocked on draft bd-f5f802).
creator: pocket4:agent-utils:pocket4-agent-utils-agnt-ctrl

title: Reintegrate/hook should clean up orphaned .git/index.lock; offer first-party safe-unlock
description:
During bd-b08fa2 I hit a git index.lock that blocked my commit. Investigation
showed a *separate* `caco agent reintegrate` rebase-retry loop running for the
SAME agent id on the same checkout (PID with 2400s timeouts, fast-test-gate hook
running `cargo test-small`). When that process exited it left a stale 0-byte
.git/index.lock behind, which then blocked the next legitimate commit until
manually cleared.

Friction shape: two reintegration drivers for one agent checkout can race the
git index, and an abnormally-exiting reintegrate/hook can orphan
.git/index.lock. Workers are (correctly) told not to remove locks blindly, so
each one must re-derive "is this lock live or orphaned" via ps/lsof every time —
slow and error-prone.

Suggested fix: have `caco agent reintegrate` (and its before_reintegration
hooks) clean up their own .git/index.lock on abnormal exit, and/or expose a
first-party `caco agent unlock-checkout`-style command that safely checks for a
live holder (lsof/ps) and clears an orphaned lock, so workers don't hand-roll
ps/lsof lock-liveness checks. Also consider guarding against two concurrent
reintegrate loops for the same agent id on one checkout.

Filed via reflect-session mixin from bd-b08fa2 session (agnt-dev).
creator: ms-mac:agent-utils:ms-mac-agent-utils-agnt-dev

title: Port bd-cdbf79 same-agent-reconnect re-acknowledgment test upstream to mcp-cli repo (survives re-vendor)
description:
Follow-up to bd-cdbf79 (discovered via that bead). bd-cdbf79 added a regression test (stdio_server_reacknowledges_repeated_initialize_from_same_connection) to crates/mcp-cli/src/lib.rs, which locks mcp-cli's stateless idempotent re-initialize (same-agent reconnect re-acknowledgment) as a reference spec.

PROBLEM: per crates/mcp-cli/README.md, mcp-cli is VENDORED into agent-utils from upstream github.com/harryaskham/mcp-cli, and the policy is to land framework changes upstream and re-vendor rather than drift the local copy. The bd-cdbf79 test was added to the LOCAL vendored copy, so it will be silently overwritten/lost on the next re-vendor from upstream.

ACTION (out-of-checkout — belongs to the upstream mcp-cli repo, not agent-utils): port the double-initialize / same-agent-reconnect re-acknowledgment test into github.com/harryaskham/mcp-cli so it survives re-vendoring and benefits all consumers (Tendril mcp stdio, skill-server, and the cacophony precursor port). Then re-vendor into agent-utils. The behavior is already correct upstream (stateless handler); this is purely about where the regression guard lives. Operator-flagged 2026-06-26.
creator: aurora:agent-utils:aurora-agent-utils-agnt-dev-aur-0

title: Cover run-manifest buildSafeRunManifest field-preservation + defaults (branch lens)
description:
Branch-coverage lens on run-manifest.js (41% branch): the gap is summarizeResult's 11 optional-field conditionals, reached via buildSafeRunManifest. Rather than mechanical branch-hunting, assert the real contract: the safe manifest (which is persisted/replicated) faithfully preserves all result fields, so a future refactor that drops a field is caught. Also cover buildSafeRunManifest's plan/run-absent defaults branch and runStatusFromResults' empty-array edge.

Probed: buildSafeRunManifest({plan:{app:{id},action:{id}}, run:{status,reason,results:[full]}, now}) preserves index/kind/status/code/authRequired/authRequiredPath/snapshotStatus/syncStatus/count/counts/outputs/outputPath/extractionError/reason; buildSafeRunManifest() -> {version:1,status:'unknown',results:[]} (no app/action/reason); runStatusFromResults([]) -> 'ok'.

Scope: add tests to test/app-automation.test.js: full-population field preservation, absent-plan/run defaults, runStatusFromResults empty edge. Regression net, no source changes. Verified via npm test + coverage. In-lane self-improvement (aurora-agent-utils-agnt-dev-aur-0, 2026-06-24).
creator: aurora:agent-utils:aurora-agent-utils-agnt-dev-aur-0

title: mcp-cli: harden McpServer stdio serve loop to isolate per-message errors (stop dropping the connection on transient errors)
description:
Surfaced by Harry's 2026-06-26 MCP-reconnect discussion: "since we control the mcp stdio binary we could do something smarter than just fail to reacknowledge connections from the same agent."

Root cause (server side, in scope): `crates/mcp-cli/src/server.rs` `serve_transport` was a bare loop that `?`-propagated BOTH the per-frame JSON parse (`serde_json::from_slice(&message)?`) and the per-request handler error (`handle_request_value(...)?`). A single malformed frame or one handler error (e.g. tools/call with bad params) therefore tore down the ENTIRE MCP session — the "erroring MCP that fails to reacknowledge / drops the connection" symptom we hit fleet-wide during the daemon flap window.

Fix (this bead): per-message error isolation in `serve_transport`.
- Malformed frame -> answer with JSON-RPC parse error (-32700, id null) and keep serving.
- Per-request handler error -> answer with JSON-RPC internal error (-32603) preserving the request id, and keep serving.
- Only a genuine reader EOF (Ok(None)) or a broken read/write stream ends the session.
- Re-initialize stays idempotent, so a reconnecting client is cleanly re-acknowledged.
Added 3 unit tests (malformed-frame isolation, handler-error isolation + id preservation, idempotent re-initialize) and a README resilience note. `cargo test -p mcp-cli` (19 passed) and `cargo clippy -p mcp-cli --tests` clean.

Scope line: the complementary client-side transparent-reconnect-with-backoff and the auto-`caco agent reconnect-mcp` watcher live in the cacophony stdio bridge ("precursor to mcp-cli") which is outside agent-utils worker scope — route those to a cacophony worker / caco-ctrl. This bead is the server-side root-cause hardening that makes transient errors stop dropping the link in the first place.
creator: ms-mac:agent-utils:ms-mac-agent-utils-agnt-dev-msm-1

title: kitty-image-preview defaults to file transport + unicode placement on no-passthrough Ghostty, causing fd-33 warnings + placeholder tofu
description:
<from-screenshot=/Users/harryaskham/screenshots/ms-mac/Screenshot_20260607_103808.png>

## Symptoms
When opening an image in the Pi agent (observed on ms-mac, Ghostty, model ghcp/opus-4.8) two cosmetic artifacts appear:

1. Node.js fd warnings, repeated:
   - `(node:NNNNN) Warning: File descriptor 33 closed but not opened in unmanaged mode`
   - `(node:NNNNN) Warning: File descriptor 33 opened in unmanaged mode twice`
2. Garbled glyphs: kitty graphics protocol Unicode Private-Use-Area placeholder cells leak as raw text instead of rendering the image, with stray status-line bytes (e.g. `ghcp/opus-4.8`, `10:25`) interleaved.

The image data itself is fine (`image/png · 723.1KB` read correctly). This is display-only, but noisy.

## Root cause (corroborated live)
Reproduced/confirmed the suspect config on a clean local Ghostty session:
- Terminal: `TERM=xterm-ghostty`, `TERM_PROGRAM=ghostty`, **no tmux, no SSH** (native kitty graphics, zero passthrough hops).
- `kitty_image_preview_status` resolves to: `transport=file ... passthrough=none placement=unicode`; `currentCommand transport=file inlinePayload=false filePath=true`.

So on a no-passthrough Ghostty terminal the extension still selects **file transport + unicode placement**:
- **file transport** opens/closes a raw fd (fd 33) outside Node's fd bookkeeping (node-pty TTY write path), which is exactly what produces the `unmanaged mode` open-twice/close-without-open warnings.
- **unicode placement** emits PUA placeholder cells; when a write gets tangled with Node's fd tracking / the TUI text stream they leak as tofu instead of being consumed as an image.

## Proposed fix
When there is no passthrough hop (no tmux, no SSH; native kitty/Ghostty graphics), default the resolved config to **memory transfer + cursor placement** instead of file + unicode. This avoids the raw-fd churn (no file fd to open/close) and the placeholder leak (cursor placement, no PUA cells). Keep unicode placement + file transport only where a passthrough/multiplexer actually requires it (tmux Unicode placeholders).

Concretely:
- Audit the transport/placement auto-resolution logic in the kitty-image-preview extension.
- For `passthrough=none` + native kitty graphics, prefer `memory`+`cursor`.
- Verify the fd-33 warnings and tofu both disappear after the change.

## Notes
- Screenshot agent (in-image analysis) independently reached the same conclusion and suggested forcing memory transfer + cursor placement.
- Severity: cosmetic / log noise, no data loss; but it degrades the image-preview UX and spams Node warnings.
creator: ms-mac:agent-utils:harryaskham

title: caco bd list filtered-query path flaps with 'beads proxy temporarily unavailable' while bd show works
description: During agnt-ctrl health sweeps, 'caco bd list --status <s>' intermittently and sometimes near-persistently fails with: 'beads proxy to the active primary is temporarily unavailable ... failed to reach caco-daemon beads API for active primary helsinki via cluster endpoint https://<helsinki>:12100'. Behavior is per-query random (different status filters fail on different attempts) and at times degrades to most list queries failing for ~30s+. Crucially, 'caco bd show --bead-id <id>' direct lookups keep working and return correct data throughout, and helsinki:12100 is TCP-reachable. So board data is intact; only the list/filtered-query proxy path is degraded. Impact: controller health sweeps that depend on 'caco bd list' get noisy/blocked and must fall back to 'bd show'. Suggested fix: investigate why the list proxy path to the active primary flaps independently of direct-bead reads (proxy timeout/pooling vs. per-bead read path), and/or add a short bounded server-side retry so transient primary unavailability doesn't surface as a hard list error. Filed via reflect-session mixin from agnt-ctrl monitoring session.
creator: pocket4:agent-utils:pocket4-agent-utils-agnt-ctrl

title: Brittle source-text-pinning tests (assert.match over module source) break on benign refactors; prefer behavioral assertions
description:
While fixing bd-b94fa1 (kitty free-data leak), a one-argument code change (adding `, freeData: true` to a few delete-builder call sites) broke FIVE separate test assertions that pin the exact source text of pi-graphics.js via regex over the file contents, e.g. test/pi-graphics.test.js asserts /buildDeleteByZIndexBandCommand\(\{ zIndices: PI_GRAPHICS_RESERVED_Z_INDICES \}\)/ and test/box-chrome.test.js asserts the emitted /a=d,d=i,.../ shape. These source-string pins are brittle: any legitimate refactor that adds an option, reorders args, or reformats triggers test failures unrelated to behavior, and they also forced a noisy multi-iteration test-fix cycle here. Several of them (the `assert.match(source, /literal call text/)` family in pi-graphics.test.js around lines 1434/1552/2092) assert that the SOURCE contains a specific call expression rather than asserting BEHAVIOR.

Friction-shape / suggested fix directions:
- Prefer behavioral assertions (drive the function, parse the emitted kitty escape stream, assert d=i vs d=I) over `assert.match(source, /exact call text/)`. The bd-3623f7 render harness already parses deleteMode from output and is the right tool.
- Where a source pin is genuinely wanted (e.g. ensuring a teardown path stays scoped), pin the STABLE intent (function name present, scoped-not-global) not the full argument object literal.
- Consider a lightweight lint/test-helper that flags `assert.match(source, ...)` over module source so new pins are reviewed.

Filed via reflect-session mixin from the bd-b94fa1 session (agnt-dev-0).
creator: ms-mac:agent-utils:ms-mac-agent-utils-agnt-dev-0

title: realtime: wire live /rt stt local-vad mode (VadSegmenter + batch stt) — pending operator UX sign-off
description:
Follow-up to bd-d4ff24 (the unwired VadSegmenter core, now landed). PENDING operator UX sign-off — do not implement until Harry picks the design.

Wire a live `/rt stt local-vad` listen mode into the realtime extension (extensions/realtime-agent.js) that uses the existing local capture (`defaultRecordCommand()` raw PCM 24k/mono/s16le) + `extensions/lib/realtime-vad-segmenter.js` `VadSegmenter`, instead of the OpenAI realtime WebSocket:
- Feed captured PCM frames into `segmenter.push(frame)`.
- On an `insert` event: run a batch model (e.g. `stt --stdin --transcription-model mai-transcribe-1.5`) over the event audio and insert the partial transcript (transcribe-and-insert).
- On a `commit` event: transcribe the whole-turn audio and SEND it as the user message (like the existing `/rt stt` injection).
- On stop/cancel: `segmenter.flush()`.

Operator decisions to confirm before building:
- exact thresholds (insert ~1s / commit ~3s defaults) + energy threshold, and whether they get PI_RT_LOCAL_VAD_* env knobs alongside the existing PI_RT_VAD_* server-VAD ones.
- insert semantics: re-transcribe whole-turn-so-far (accurate, more cost) vs transcribe only the delta chunk (cheap, may drift) — VadSegmenter provides both `audio` and `delta`.
- whether commit re-transcribes the full turn for the final send.
- the new mode id / command surface (`/rt stt local-vad` vs a new `micMode`), status-line/diagnostics, and `/rt-doctor` reporting.

This touches Harry's personal voice workflow and needs a mic/Pulse to validate end-to-end, so it stays operator-gated. Filed via reflect/feature-capture from the 2026-06-26 realtime-plugin conversation.
creator: ms-mac:agent-utils:ms-mac-agent-utils-agnt-dev-msm-1
spoken_name: wire live realtime stt local vad

title: [health] Add CI workflow that runs JS+Rust tests and clippy (currently only docs deploy runs)
description:
The repository has substantial automated tests that CI never runs:
- `npm test` (node --test): 336 passing JS tests across extensions
- `cargo test --workspace`: 12 passing Rust unit tests (mcp-cli 7, skill-server 5)
- `cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets`: clean under strict all+pedantic lints

The only GitHub Actions workflow is `.github/workflows/pages.yml`, which
deploys docs to GitHub Pages and only runs `npm run docs:check` (and only on
docs/** path changes). There is NO workflow that runs the JS test suite, the
Rust tests, or clippy on push / pull_request.

Impact: regressions in extensions or the Rust crates are not caught by CI.
The test investment exists but provides no automated guard on main/PRs.

Proposed (health, no new features):
- Add a `ci.yml` workflow on push + pull_request (GitHub-hosted ubuntu-latest,
  consistent with the existing pages.yml hosting choice) that runs:
  - `npm test`
  - `npm run check` (docs:check)
  - `cargo test --workspace`
  - `cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings`
- Keep it fast and cache cargo where reasonable.

Filed from the project-health audit (operator directive: audit health + perf,
no big new features). Lane: test/build/lint health (agnt-dev).
creator: ms-mac:agent-utils:ms-mac-agent-utils-agnt-dev

- Captured images are properly transmitted to models
- Solution maintains compatibility with existing tendril functionality

title: (Optional) Modularize mcp-cli/src/lib.rs into focused modules (993 LOC single file)
description:
## (Optional, low-pri) Modularize mcp-cli/src/lib.rs (993 LOC, single file)

`crates/mcp-cli/src/lib.rs` is a single 993-line file bundling several distinct concerns:
- error/category types + `StructuredError` trait + `JsonError`
- `JsonEnvelope<T>` + envelope/result helpers
- `Tool`/`ToolRouter`/`ToolMetadata`
- `McpServer` + stdio config
- JSON-RPC protocol framing (`read_protocol_message`/`write_protocol_message`)
- tests

### Recommendation
Optionally split into modules (e.g. `envelope.rs`, `error.rs`, `router.rs`, `server.rs`, `protocol.rs`) with `lib.rs` as the re-export root. Purely a readability/maintainability refactor — no behavior change. Low priority; the crate is otherwise clean (only 1 non-test expect()).

NOTE: touches mcp-cli only (not skill-server), so it does not collide with the serde_yaml/rustfmt skill-server health work. Coordinate/rebase if claimed alongside mcp-cli test work (bd-9689fb).

title: /gfx settings runtime-only unless saved (dialog Enter or /gfx save)
description:
Operator (Harry) request: changes to /gfx settings should be runtime-only unless
either Enter is pressed in the /gfx settings dialog, or `/gfx save` is run.
Otherwise they must NOT persist to the agent settings file
(~/.pi/agent/settings.json / $PI_CODING_AGENT_DIR/settings.json). This lets Harry
run e.g. `pi '/gfx mode on'` to try different modes without mangling his home
config.

Current behaviour (extensions/pi-graphics.js): every mutating /gfx subcommand
persists immediately:
- the main mutation loop ends with applyGfxSettingsAndReload() which calls
  saveSettings() then ctx.reload();
- /gfx debug and /gfx preset also save;
- /eink saves;
- the settings dialog already saves only on Enter (done("save")), which is the
  desired model.

Plan:
- Add an in-memory runtime overlay so /gfx mutations apply live (env/theme/chrome
  + rerender) WITHOUT writing settings.json, and persist across invocations
  in-session.
- Apply runtime-only changes via applyGfxSettingsLive without ctx.reload()
  (reload would revert to on-disk settings).
- Add `/gfx save` to flush the pending runtime overlay to settings.json.
- Keep dialog Enter = save (unchanged).
- Surface an "unsaved changes" indicator in /gfx status and usage text.
- Tests in test/pi-graphics.test.js asserting runtime-only-by-default + /gfx save
  persists + dialog Enter persists.
creator: ms-mac:agent-utils:ms-mac-agent-utils-agnt-dev

Add a /pi-graphics-live-probe command and tool that reports runtime extension/package version, configured theme/mode/env flags, available ctx.ui APIs (setHeader/setFooter/setWidget/setEditorComponent/notify/setTheme), emits raw bootstrap + notification + header/footer/editor/status surfaces on demand, and returns a compact triage text. title: Add bounded autoreconnect for realtime WebSocket timeouts
description:
Harry observed during live realtime/Pulse testing that the realtime connection times out easily and then does not automatically reconnect. The `/rt` UI was still showing the restore/previous-model state (e.g. ↩ litellm-openai/gpt-5.5), but once the realtime WebSocket/session timed out the extension did not retry or recover automatically.

Expected behaviour:
- For realtime call/STT modes, transient WebSocket timeouts or close events should trigger bounded automatic reconnect when the user has not explicitly turned realtime off.
- Reconnect should preserve intended mode where safe: full realtime vs STT-only, audio on/off, listen mode (vad/ptt/nolisten), voice/backend/reasoning, and previous-model restore target.
- Reconnect attempts should be bounded/backoff-based and visible in `/rt-status` / `/rt-doctor` (last disconnect reason, retry count, next retry delay).
- Explicit `/rt-off`, `/rt stop` close, or user cancellation should disable autoreconnect.
- If reconnect ultimately fails, surface a clear error and leave the session in a consistent stopped state.

Acceptance tests / coverage ideas:
- Simulate a realtime WebSocket close/timeout while `/rt start vad` is active and assert reconnect is attempted.
- Simulate close after explicit `/rt-off` and assert no reconnect occurs.
- Verify diagnostics report last disconnect/reconnect state.

Related bugs/features: bd-5896e5 (`/rt start vad` does not switch Pi to realtime model), bd-36c9e2 (agent-side realtime call and Pulse controls).

Filed from l7fipzlhc4u4d0t9 at Harry's request during live RT/Pulse testing.
creator: ms-mac:agent-utils:l7fipzlhc4u4d0t9

title: Pin mcp-cli envelope round-trip deserialization and details-omission contracts
description:
The mcp-cli JsonEnvelope wire format is well-tested for serialization (status
tag, schema_version, error category/code/details-present), but two contract
guarantees are unpinned:

1. Round-trip deserialization. JsonEnvelope<T> and JsonError derive Deserialize
   so consumers can parse responses back into typed values, but no test asserts
   a serialized envelope deserializes back to an equal typed JsonEnvelope.
   Existing tests only parse into an untyped serde_json::Value.

2. details omission. JsonError.details uses skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none".
   The present-details case is tested, but nothing asserts that a None details
   field is omitted from the JSON object entirely (consumers rely on the key
   being absent, not null).

Add two focused unit tests in crates/mcp-cli/src/envelope.rs (or the lib.rs test
module) covering typed round-trip equality for both Success and Error envelopes,
and the details-None-is-omitted contract. Pure test additions, no behavior
change. Proactive coverage polish under operator overnight directive (no open
beads). agnt-dev session, follow-up to bd-723120.
creator: ms-mac:agent-utils:ms-mac-agent-utils-agnt-dev

title: Add behavioral coverage for footer-text.js formatters (only source-grep tested today)
description:
Found via npm run test:coverage: extensions/pi-graphics/footer-text.js is at 44.83% line / 12.50% FUNCS coverage. It has 8 pure footer formatters (formatFooterTokens, formatFooterPct, compactPathSegment, compactFooterPath, prettyFooterCwd, compactFooterProvider, compactFooterModelName, noEllipsisFooterText) but the only test references are source-pattern assert.match greps in pi-graphics.test.js (asserting the SOURCE contains function signatures) — they do NOT exercise behavior, so ~7 of 8 formatters are behaviorally untested. These render the operator status footer (token counts, percentages, cwd path, provider/model abbreviations), so wrong formatting is operator-visible.

Scope: add test/footer-text.test.js with real behavioral assertions for all 8 functions, e.g. formatFooterTokens (1.5m / 1.5k / 999 / 1000k boundary), formatFooterPct (.0 stripped, 1-decimal round), compactPathSegment (leading-dot strip, ".."->".", ""->"" ), compactFooterProvider (ghcp/oai/ant/loai/lant/oprt/az/passthrough), compactFooterModelName (gpt- strip except ghcp gpt-5*, claude- strip, opus-4-7->opus-4.7, -1m-internal strip), prettyFooterCwd (~ for HOME, compaction), compactFooterPath, noEllipsisFooterText. HOME saved/restored for cwd tests. Regression net, no source changes. Verified via npm test + coverage. In-lane self-improvement (aurora-agent-utils-agnt-dev-aur-0, 2026-06-24).

title: Remove legacy adaptive-thinking payload shim after Pi 0.77 native support
description:
Pi 0.77.0 added native Claude Opus 4.8 metadata and Opus adaptive-thinking coverage. agent-utils still carries the older adaptive/effort shim from bd-a6c521, including extensions/effort.js payload rewriting of thinking.type=enabled to thinking.type=adaptive plus output_config.effort and true-defaults handling of adaptive startup defaults.

Now that Pi core understands adaptive thinking and Opus 4.8 metadata directly, this local shim can double-apply or fight core model metadata, and the operator reports it may be breaking sessions. Rework agent-utils so native Pi support owns adaptive thinking. Likely remove or narrow the adaptive payload patching path, remove adaptive from true-default persistence if Pi core now accepts it, and keep only harmless UX shortcuts such as /effort delegating to core APIs if still useful.

Acceptance criteria:
- No agent-utils extension mutates outbound model payloads for adaptive thinking when Pi core can do it natively.
- /effort, if kept, delegates to Pi core rather than rewriting thinking/output_config itself.
- Tests/docs updated to reflect Pi >=0.77 native adaptive support.
- Validate with targeted agent-utils tests covering effort/true-defaults behavior.

Context: operator asked whether the 0.77.0 release note means manual adaptive overrides can be removed; bd-a6c521 is the closed bead that introduced the shim.

Operator clarification 2026-05-31: remove all agent-utils adaptive-thinking overrides here. Adaptive should just be a thing Pi supports natively now. Do not keep any Opus 4.8 special-casing at all.

title: kitty image-preview / Tendril-stream IOSurface leak: long-running backgrounded Pi agents never delete kitty images -> macOS WindowServer unbounded RAM
description:
## Problem
On macOS, WindowServer grows to unbounded RAM (observed 31.75GB in ~1h41m on the ms-mac Cacophony node, driving node-wide thrash: ~78MB free / 36GB host, ~18.6GB compressor, heavy swap, load 10-16) from accumulated IOSurfaces. Root cause traced to the agent-utils Pi kitty-image-preview + Tendril screenshot-stream tooling failing to delete kitty images / clear placements, so long-running BACKGROUNDED Pi agents leak IOSurfaces indefinitely — even when the operator's foreground kitty terminal is NOT in graphics mode.

## Mechanism (operator Harry directive + cacophony caco-ctrl + cacophony msm-1 audit)
- A long-running backgrounded Pi agent uses the agent-utils kitty tools (kitty_image_preview_add / _stream_start / _animate / _add_folder) and Tendril screenshot streams to add images/placements.
- If the plugin does not emit the kitty delete (a=d) + clear placements when a stream stops, animation stops, the gallery is cleared, or the agent session ends/idles/tears down, the images persist as IOSurfaces charged to macOS WindowServer.
- The cacophony caco-tui is RULED OUT as the leak (full audit by cacophony msm-1): its own kitty delete lifecycle is robust + refcounted (recycle_image_id recycles only AFTER mark_image_delete_sent; long-lived panes reuse placements via retained_image_id a=p redisplay with refcounted orphan-delete, shared-image-safe per cacophony bd-327f8f); and PTY-passthrough (remap_pty_passthrough_image_id) only remaps child image IDs into the 2B namespace and passes them through — the CHILD (agent runtime / agent-utils plugin) owns the image lifecycle/delete, not caco-tui. In the operator's gfx-off case the images bypass caco-tui entirely and go straight to the terminal.
- Evidence: cacophony ios-2 found 18 STALE kitty preview placements its agent was holding (visible=false) and cleared them via pi_graphics_clear — concrete proof of accumulated un-deleted placements.

## Fix direction
Audit + fix the agent-utils kitty-image-preview + Tendril-stream + pi_graphics_clear delete lifecycle so kitty images/placements are reliably deleted (a=d) on: stream stop, animation stop, gallery clear, agent session end/idle, and agent teardown — especially for long-running backgrounded agents. Ensure scoped image-id deletes reach every owned placement. Consider a bounded ceiling on retained placements + a teardown/idle reaper so a backgrounded agent cannot accumulate surfaces without bound.

## Cross-ref
- cacophony canonical crisis bead: bd-e10fcd (ms-mac WindowServer leak P1; full diagnostics + census 6 kitty / 6 ghostty / 63 tmux / 36 pi over ~10h uptime).
- cacophony caco-tui full rule-out + a macOS WindowServer-RSS doctor guardrail (in-lane cacophony mitigation, warns before thrash) are tracked as children of bd-e10fcd by cacophony msm-1.
- Operator directive (Harry): "audit our tendril/kitty placements to make sure we aren't spamming IOSurface that we never clean up, otherwise long running backgrounded pi agents could use infinite ram ... probably within-pi agent-utils plugin failing to delete kitty images."

Filed by cacophony caco-ctrl via cross-project routing; the durable fix belongs to the agent-utils extension owner.

## ROOT CAUSE PINPOINTED (tndrl-mac, agent-utils HEAD 6c7e528) — lowercase vs uppercase kitty delete
The leak is a kitty-protocol delete-mode bug: LOWERCASE delete (a=d, d=i) removes only the PLACEMENT and keeps the image DATA cached; only UPPERCASE (d=I) frees the image data — and on macOS that backing data is the IOSurface held by WindowServer. The agent-utils kitty extensions delete EVERYTHING with lowercase i and have ZERO uppercase data-freeing deletes, so every transmitted preview/capture/stream frame leaves its IOSurface alive forever; long-running backgrounded pi agents grow WindowServer RAM unbounded even with kitty gfx visually off (transmit+cleanup still run).

EXACT EVIDENCE (agent-utils HEAD 6c7e528):
- extensions/kitty-graphics.js:789 buildScopedDeleteCommand hardcodes deleteMode "i"; :663 buildDeleteCommand defaults to lowercase i for id-deletes.
- extensions/pi-graphics.js cleanup sites all pass deleteMode "i" at lines 579, 1020, 1342, 1595; scoped clears at 2123, 3190, 3300.
- extensions/kitty-image-preview.js clear/teardown paths (753, 776, 1192, 1418, 1431, 1885, 1899) all route through the lowercase scoped delete.

FIX: teardown / clear / stream-stop / pi_graphics_clear / clearPrevious-of-discarded-images must emit UPPERCASE d=I so the terminal frees the image data (IOSurface). Keep lowercase i ONLY where the same image id is intentionally retained for redraw. Add a regression test asserting teardown emits an uppercase data-freeing delete.

This is the precise, actionable agent-utils fix — ready for an agent-utils dev to claim. (Root cause found by tndrl-mac, who is tendril-scoped and routed it here via cacophony caco-ctrl.)

## COMPLEMENTARY MECHANISM (caco-profile + picasso-dev-2 convergent audits) — pid-salted image IDs + no restart cleanup
Beyond the lowercase-vs-uppercase delete bug, a SECOND contributing mechanism: pi-graphics image IDs are SALTED WITH THE PROCESS PID, with no on-exit or startup cleanup, and Cacophony does not set the stable-namespace env that would make restarts reuse/overwrite IDs. So every time a managed agent restarts inside its persistent tmux pane it gets brand-new image IDs and ORPHANS its previous images, which the new process can never free (it no longer knows the old IDs). Combined with the never-uppercase-free bug, restarts compound the leak.

Consolidated fix elements (tndrl-mac + caco-profile + picasso-dev-0/-2):
(a) uppercase data-freeing delete (d=I) on clear/hide/replace/stream-turnover/teardown;
(b) FREE-BEFORE-FORGET in the clear path (it currently deletes placements then forgets the IDs, so the data can never be freed);
(c) STABLE image IDs across restarts (stop pid-salting, or set a stable-namespace env, or clean up on exit/startup);
(d) reuse stable IDs for stream frames instead of minting a new ID per frame;
(e) free even when headless/backgrounded (don't skip the delete when there's no UI);
(f) regression test asserting teardown emits an uppercase data-freeing delete.

Convergent duplicate beads deduped into cacophony incident bd-e10fcd: bd-7f7650 (picasso-dev-0), bd-6514e7 (picasso-dev-2), bd-dc9403 (log-monitor). This bead (bd-ad43f8) is the single canonical agent-utils FIX item.

## REFINEMENTS (caco-profile audit) — existing stable-ID mechanism + exact free-before-forget sites + cross-pid-orphan diagnostic
1. STABLE-ID MECHANISM ALREADY EXISTS: id-space.js piGraphicsIdScope() drops the :pid: salt when PI_GRAPHICS_ID_NAMESPACE_EXACT=1. PREFERRED agent-utils fix for mechanism B: AUTO-DROP the pid salt under a managed signal like CACO_AGENT_ID (helps all consumers automatically) instead of requiring each consumer to set env vars. Effect: a restart in the same pane reuses + overwrites (frees) prior images.
2. EXACT FREE-BEFORE-FORGET SITES: runtime.js resetPlacementTracking() (~line 117) clears ownedImageIds with NO delete; called from pi-graphics.js:3194 and :3309. Those must emit uppercase d=I frees for owned ids BEFORE clearing.
3. CROSS-PID-ORPHAN DIAGNOSTIC (key): pi_graphics_clear on a running managed agent freed 0 images — confirming the DOMINANT leak is UNREACHABLE CROSS-PID ORPHANS (images from prior pids the current process can't address), NOT within-process accumulation. Implication: the graphics-cleanup ask + pi_graphics_clear CANNOT fully fix it; only (a) stable IDs across restarts (reuse+overwrite/free) and (b) uppercase-free-on-exit (so an exiting agent leaves no orphans) prevent the restart-orphan leak.

Cacophony INTERIM mitigation (lands without the agent-utils change, uses the existing mechanism): managed Pi launch sets PI_GRAPHICS_ID_NAMESPACE=$CACO_AGENT_ID + PI_GRAPHICS_ID_NAMESPACE_EXACT=1 — tracked as a cacophony child of bd-e10fcd. The agent-utils auto-drop-under-CACO_AGENT_ID (point 1) is the preferred durable version that helps all consumers.

## CROSS-GENERATION RECLAIM REFINEMENT (picasso-linear + picasso-dev-0, from deduped bd-539c83)
Concrete vehicle for mechanism B (cross-generation reclaim on startup): state.js ALREADY persists ownedImageIds across sessions (snapshot restore ~L52-55), but restore currently only repopulates the tracking Set and frees nothing. FIX: on restore, emit UPPERCASE per-image frees (d=I) for the persisted prior-session ids BEFORE reusing/clearing them — this reclaims the prior generation's orphaned images.

Backstop (for crashed generations whose snapshot never persisted): reclaim by the reserved pi-graphics high id band (PI_GRAPHICS_IMAGE_ID_BAND_BASE 0xc0000000) on startup.

CAVEAT: buildDeleteByZIndexCommand uses d=z (LOWERCASE) = placements-only, so pi_graphics_clear --hostedBand does NOT free IOSurface and cannot be relied on for reclaim — it must use the uppercase per-image free.

(Source: id-space.js piGraphicsIdScope() salts ids as ${sessionish}:pid:${process.pid}, deliberately including pid except when PI_GRAPHICS_ID_NAMESPACE_EXACT=1; the header comment self-documents the cross-generation-reclaimability vs redraw-collision tradeoff. Recreated/restarted managed agents mint different ids and never free the prior PID's images, which were also not freed at the prior process's lowercase-only/headless-skip/crash exit. Managed agents recreate constantly, so every generation orphans a fresh PID-salted set.)

NET — the complete agent-utils fix needs BOTH halves: (A) within-process uppercase d=I free on evict/stop/shutdown (free-before-forget, free even when headless); (B) cross-generation reclaim on startup (free persisted prior-session ids via snapshot-restore; high-id-band backstop). Interim cacophony mitigation for (B) without the agent-utils change = bd-28f859 (stable per-agent namespace via PI_GRAPHICS_ID_NAMESPACE_EXACT, caco-profile landing).
creator: ms-mac:cacophony:ms-mac-cacophony-caco-ctrl

title: Clarify or helperize TypeBox usage for extension tool schemas
description:
While implementing realtime agent tools in bd-36c9e2, importing TypeBox directly from `extensions/realtime-agent.js` caused `npm test` to fail with `ERR_MODULE_NOT_FOUND: Cannot find package '@sinclair/typebox'`, even though other package files and peerDependencies refer to TypeBox. The extension runtime/test environment does not guarantee that peer dependency is installed for every extension import path.

Friction shape: extension authors can easily copy the `pi.registerTool` examples that use TypeBox and accidentally introduce a runtime import failure in packages where TypeBox is only a peer dependency. A small shared local schema helper or explicit dependency/documentation would prevent this.

Potential fix: document the package dependency rule for extension tool schemas, add a local helper for simple JSON-schema tool parameter objects, or promote `@sinclair/typebox` from peerDependency to dependency if extension code is expected to import it directly.

Filed via reflect-session mixin from bd-36c9e2 session.
creator: ms-mac:agent-utils:l7fipzlhc4u4d0t9

title: Implement app automation config loader and deterministic action runner
description: Add the core Pi extension runtime for loading blessed app configs, listing apps/actions, dry-running plans, and executing deterministic Playwright/Tendril steps through a small bridge rather than forcing agents to hand-write browser commands. Include tests for config validation and safe command construction. Parent bd-ee8e57; depends on bd-bf9c5e.
creator: pocket4:agent-utils:pocket4-agent-utils-agnt-ctrl

title: Honor --status draft during caco bd create
description:
During the bd-3d7aca reflection step, caco bd create was invoked with --status draft --priority P3 --type task, but the returned bead bd-eac404 had status open and had to be corrected with a separate caco bd update --status draft. This creates a risk that reflection-polish beads briefly enter the dispatchable open queue despite the reflect-session contract requiring drafts. Investigate create/status handling and ensure --status draft is honored atomically. Filed via reflect-session mixin from bd-16f204 session.

Triage note 2026-06-16 (ms-dev-2 ms2-0, during post-outage bead cleanup): the symptom appears resolved. Three beads created this session via caco bd create --status draft (bd-dd63c4, bd-15ea4f, bd-f4d277) all correctly persisted status=draft on creation — none briefly entered the open queue. The underlying create/status handling is in caco-core (Rust), not this agent-utils checkout, so I cannot verify the exact fix commit from here and am not closing unilaterally. Recommend a caco-scoped triager confirm against caco-core history and close as resolved.

Triage confirmation 2026-06-19 (ms-mac agnt-dev-0, overnight draft audit): corroborates the 2026-06-16 ms-dev-2 note — symptom appears RESOLVED. Observable now: all 26 current agent-utils drafts correctly hold status=draft (none leaked into the open/ready queue; open=0, ready=0 confirmed via count-only). The create/status handling lives in caco-core (cacophony), outside agent-utils scope, so I cannot verify the fix commit or close from here. RECOMMEND a caco-scoped triager confirm against caco-core history and close as resolved. Staleness verdict: stale/resolved candidate.
creator: ms-mac:agent-utils:ms-mac-agent-utils-agnt-dev

title: Expose Pi graphics typing impulse settings
description: Typing impulse/editor impulse-response effect exists in renderer but is not visible/configurable in /gfx settings/status/help. Add visible settings and commands for enabling/tuning the typing impulse response, and document it.
creator: ms-mac:agent-utils:ms-mac-agent-utils-agnt-dev-2

title: Add Pi graphics TUI render parity validation
description: Follow-up operator request: Pi kitty graphics still appears invisible. Add stronger TypeScript-side validation/proof inspired by the Cacophony Rust visual diagnostics: a rendered TUI component/scene probe with measurable glow/gradient/pulse metrics, live command/tool output that cannot look like normal terminal text, and tests that assert the rendered graphical output differs substantially from the plain theme. Keep defaults calm but make the proof and validation unmistakable.
creator: ms-mac:agent-utils:ms-mac-agent-utils-agnt-dev

title: web-search: bounded model-fallback resilience against retired models
description:
Add a bounded model-fallback to the web-search plugin for resilience against retired models.

Motivation: web search broke this session because the default model gpt-5.2-codex was retired by the Copilot integrator and the plugin 400ed hard. A bounded fallback would have absorbed that retirement and kept web search working.

Change: on a model-availability error (HTTP 400 with not-available-for-integrator / model_not_supported / unsupported_api_for_model / not-supported), the plugin now retries with the next model in an ordered candidate list (requested model, configured default, then configured fallbacks, deduped). Non-model errors (auth, rate-limit, input) are surfaced immediately without retrying. Fallbacks default to gpt-5.3-codex, gpt-5.5, gpt-5.4 (all verified to support the Responses API web_search tool) and are overridable via WEB_SEARCH_FALLBACK_MODELS.

The pure model-selection helpers were extracted into extensions/web-search-models.js so they can be unit-tested without importing the extension entrypoint, which pulls in the Pi-runtime-only @sinclair/typebox dependency. Added test/web-search.test.js covering parseFallbackModels, modelCandidates ordering/dedup, and isModelUnavailableError classification.

Validated: node check, web-search test 5/5, full suite 594 green, docs check passing.

Filed as a self-initiated resilience improvement during agent-utils operation under the improve-projects directive.
creator: ms-mac:agent-utils:ms-mac-agent-utils-agnt-dev-2

title: Add segmented kitty-compatible Pi footer chrome
description: Move the standalone Pi one-line footer/statusline into agent-utils pi-graphics. Preserve compact one-line format while adding stable Unicode-placeholder divider anchors and low-z relative backgrounds for segments such as branch, cwd, context, compaction, model, and thinking. Should be kitty/caco-compatible and avoid width overflow.
creator: ms-mac:agent-utils:ms-mac-agent-utils-agnt-dev

title: Improve Pi kitty graphics glow theme and validation
description: Operator request from Harry: improve Pi kitty graphics mode so the theme visibly changes instead of appearing identical. Build a better TypeScript-side mirror/test harness for rendering TUI components in graphical style, inspired by the Rust Cacophony implementation. Target aesthetic: efficient pulsating/glowing/high-tech terminal visuals with deep Nordic color gradients and rendered kitty graphics glow. Include better tests and validation demonstrating visible rendered differences.
creator: ms-mac:agent-utils:ms-mac-agent-utils-agnt-dev

title: skill-search human output should list candidates on ambiguous route
description:
The `skill-search` human (non-JSON) output for an ambiguous route only prints the
top-level message ("multiple equally good routes matched `X`; pass a more
specific domain or tool") and shows nothing about which routes actually
collided. The JSON envelope already carries the competing `matches`, but a human
or agent using the default text surface cannot see the candidate names/domains,
so the "pass a more specific domain or tool" guidance is not actionable from the
human output alone.

Fix: when the response status is `ambiguous` (and `selected` is None),
`render_meta_human` should list the top-scoring candidate routes (kind, name,
domain, score, and tool if present) so the operator/agent can disambiguate. This
preserves the documented "no guessing" contract — it only surfaces the existing
candidate set, it does not auto-pick a route. Add a unit test asserting the
human renderer includes the colliding candidate names for an ambiguous response.

Scope: crates/skill-server/src/lib.rs (render_meta_human + a unit test). No
behavior change to routing/JSON. Filed as proactive agent-tooling polish under
operator overnight directive (no open beads). bd context: agnt-dev session.
creator: ms-mac:agent-utils:ms-mac-agent-utils-agnt-dev

title: Add coverage for pi-graphics theme-colors.js ansi256ToRgb + fallback branches
description:
Found via npm run test:coverage: extensions/pi-graphics/theme-colors.js is at ~76% line / 56% branch (uncovered 36 38 43-52 70-74 78 82-83). The uncovered code is genuine, pure color-math with correctness stakes (wrong conversion = wrong UI theme colors): the entire ansi256ToRgb (256-color grayscale ramp index>=232, the 6x6x6 color cube 16<=index<232, and the index<16 bright/low base mappings), the 16-color ANSI fallback path in parseAnsiTruecolorRgb, and getThemeColorRgb's short-hex (3-char) + invalid-length fallback branches and the theme.getFgAnsi-throws catch.

Scope: append focused tests to test/theme-colors.test.js asserting ACTUAL behavior of those branches (grayscale 244->128, cube 196->[255,0,0], 21->[0,0,255], 16->[0,0,0], bright 9->[255,0,0], low 1->[205,0,0]; 16-color "\\x1b[31m"->[205,0,0]; null/non-string/no-match -> null; short-hex #abc -> [170,187,204]; invalid-length fallback -> [136,192,208]; theme throws -> hex fallback; 256-color via stub). Regression net, no source changes. Verified via npm test + coverage. In-lane self-improvement (aurora-agent-utils-agnt-dev-aur-0, 2026-06-24).
creator: aurora:agent-utils:aurora-agent-utils-agnt-dev-aur-0

title: Add contextual editor border responsivity
description: Add editor responsivity modes for contextual state such as agent thinking: alter top/bottom separator masks (e.g. cosine-squared/negated thought-bubble shapes) and tick/scroll the editor border graphics during thinking animation while composing with editor drawing style and placement mode.
creator: ms-mac:agent-utils:ms-mac-agent-utils-agnt-dev-2

title: Clamp kittwm/kitty graphics drawing to reported terminal rows
description: Operator reports the likely root cause of the current kittwm/kitty graphics display issue: we are trying to draw larger than the actual number of terminal rows reported. Inspect the kitty graphics / preview / Pi graphics window sizing paths, clamp rendered/placement rows to the reported terminal viewport (accounting for reserved editor/footer rows), and add tests so row requests cannot exceed available terminal rows.
creator: ms-mac:agent-utils:l7fipzlhc4u4d0t9

title: Make Kitty animation frame chunk continuations include a=f
description:
Audit finding from Harry's Kitty graphics protocol review: the protocol requires animation frame transfer (`a=f`) in all escape codes, and chunked frame continuations must also specify `a=f`. `extensions/kitty-graphics.js::serializeKittyGraphicsChunks()` currently emits only `{m}` on continuation chunks, so large animation frames generated by `buildPngCursorAnimationUpload()` or `buildPngVirtualPlacementAnimation()` are protocol-invalid.

Fix chunk serialization for animation frames and add a test with a multi-chunk animation frame payload verifying every chunk includes `a=f` where required.

Filed from agnt-dev-2 Kitty protocol audit session.
creator: ms-mac:agent-utils:ms-mac-agent-utils-agnt-dev-2

title: Make Pi graphics box effects form coherent boxes
description: Box chrome effects currently appear on random lines instead of forming a coherent box around a rendered component. Fix row grouping/instance identity/wrapping so effects align as a box, with tests.
creator: ms-mac:agent-utils:ms-mac-agent-utils-agnt-dev

title: Add efficient halo Pi graphics editor chrome
description: Operator request from Harry, 2026-05-23: continue developing new, interesting graphical styles for Pi kitty graphics or polish existing ones. Focus on beauty, rendering efficiency, and creative robust ways of replacing Pi UI with kitty graphics. Scope this slice to one focused, testable agent-utils Pi graphics style improvement.
creator: ms-mac:agent-utils:ms-mac-agent-utils-agnt-dev

title: Add efficient scrim Pi graphics overlay chrome
description: Add a deterministic low-entropy scrim box chrome effect for overlay surfaces: sparse dimmer curtains, focus brackets, and modal edge ticks, so overlay surfaces no longer use the frost motif. Keep frost available as an explicit variant; avoid timers, dense textures, masks, glyph dependencies, graph layout, animation loops, or repaint loops.
creator: ms-mac:agent-utils:ms-mac-agent-utils-agnt-dev

title: Fix IOSurface cleanup in tendril/kitty to prevent infinite RAM growth
description:
Address IOSurface memory leaks identified in the audit that cause WindowServer to grow unbounded on MacOS. Long-running backgrounded pi agents are consuming infinite RAM due to uncleaned IOSurface allocations.

- Implement proper IOSurface deallocation/cleanup routines
- Ensure all created IOSurfaces are properly released when no longer needed
- Prevent spamming of IOSurface creation without cleanup
- Test with long-running background processes to verify memory stabilizes

Acceptance criteria:
- WindowServer memory usage stabilizes over long durations
- No IOSurface handle leaks detected
- Background pi agents no longer exhibit unbounded memory growth

IMPLEMENTATION COMPLETE across three layers, all landed on origin/main (do NOT re-implement; only operator verification remains). Layer 1 bd-b94fa1 (8ee6d0a): within-process never-free fixed via uppercase d=I/d=Z free-data delete on every eviction/teardown path + free-before-forget + upload-cache invalidation. Layer 2 bd-ad43f8 (960d1cd): dominant cross-pid-orphan leak fixed — managed agents drop the pid salt and key kitty image ids to a stable caco-agent namespace, so restarts reuse+overwrite+free instead of orphaning. Layer 3 (this bead, 7753170): cross-generation startup reclaim — on session_start the extension frees the persisted prior-session image data (uppercase d=I, excluding the current image) so a restart self-reclaims prior-generation orphans without a WindowServer restart. 636/636 node test green. Audit doc docs/kitty-graphics-protocol-audit.md (533f513). Non-critical refinements in draft bd-ded98d. REMAINING acceptance criterion is operator verification: repin agent-utils to current main and watch `footprint WindowServer` (not plain ps) across a long multi-restart agent session; it should stop climbing. Held in_progress by agnt-dev-0 pending that; cacophony incident bd-e10fcd tracks overall RAM stabilization.
creator: ms-mac:agent-utils:harryaskham

title: Add tendril-app snapshot cleanup commands
description: Expose the new snapshot cleanup plan/apply workflow through the `/tendril-app` command surface, not just agent-visible tools. Add `/tendril-app cleanup [app] [keep:<n>]` for dry-run planning and `/tendril-app cleanup-apply [app] [keep:<n>] confirm` for explicit deletion. Preserve the same safety semantics as the tool: no deletion without an unambiguous confirmation token, state-root containment, and protected latest/auth artifacts. Add source guards/tests.

title: [flaky] realtime-agent speed test races on fixed setTimeout waits

load). Root cause: fixed-duration waits (await setTimeout(25) then setTimeout(5))
used to sync with async response.create handling are too tight under load —

yet when the assertion runs.

waitUntil(predicate,{timeoutMs,intervalMs}) poll helper and replace the two
fixed sleeps in this test with poll-until-condition (wait for the expected
response.create, and for the retry to be the >=2nd response.create with
speed:undefined). Keeps the fix surgical; the broader 24-site fixed-sleep pattern

title: [broken-on-main] realtime VAD mic-restart test flake: lost 'exit' event when record command fails fast
description:
test/realtime-agent.test.js:1318 "realtime restarts VAD mic when recorder exits unexpectedly" is the recurring flake every peer caveats their status with ("suite green modulo the known realtime VAD timing flake").

Root cause: in extensions/realtime-agent.js startMic(), the proc.on("exit", ...) listener is registered AFTER `await this.maybeApplySession(...)`. The test injects PI_RT_RECORD_CMD="sh -c 'exit 7'", which exits almost immediately. When the child exits during that await (before the listener is attached), Node emits 'exit' once with no listener and the event is lost, so scheduleMicRestart() never runs: micRestartAttempts stays 0, micRestartTimer stays null, and no "restarting vad capture" notification is emitted. The assertion at :1337 then fails. It only passes when the await happens to outlast the child exit.

Fix: extract the exit handler into a named idempotent function (guarded by this.mic === proc) and, immediately after attaching it, reconcile the already-exited case by checking proc.exitCode/proc.signalCode and invoking the handler synchronously. This closes the race for both the test and real fast-failing record commands. Not caused by any in-flight bead's changes; pre-existing.
