title: Kitty image data never freed (lowercase d=i only) — long-running pi agents leak IOSurface/WindowServer RAM on macOS
description:
SYMPTOM (operator-reported, macOS): WindowServer RAM grows huge over time; suspected long-running/backgrounded pi agents leak kitty-image IOSurface/GPU memory that is never reclaimed, even when the caco TUI does not have kitty graphics enabled. NOTE (picasso-dev-1): WindowServer RSS reads as 0 via plain `ps` (macOS entitlement trap) — measure with `footprint WindowServer`, `vmmap`, or Activity Monitor.

ROOT CAUSE (confirmed by code audit): the agent-utils pi plugin transmits kitty images (a=T/a=t/a=q) with STABLE image ids and a transmit-once guard (bd-d6fa1b), so each distinct image's bytes are stored once in the terminal. But EVERY cleanup/delete path uses a LOWERCASE kitty delete mode, which per the kitty graphics protocol deletes only the PLACEMENT and keeps the image DATA cached. There is no uppercase free-data delete (d=I / d=A / d=Z) anywhere in the extension. Net: image data for every distinct image id ever transmitted stays resident in the terminal (IOSurface on macOS kitty/Ghostty) and is never freed — not on navigate-away, not on clearPrevious, not on widget dispose, not on stream stop, and crucially not on session_end/session_shutdown. For a long-running/backgrounded agent (or many agents on one host) this is unbounded growth attributed to WindowServer.

Kitty spec (https://sw.kovidgoyal.net/kitty/graphics-protocol/ Deleting images): lowercase d-value deletes placements but keeps data; uppercase d-value also frees the stored image data (when unreferenced).

EVIDENCE (all lowercase, none uppercase):
- extensions/kitty-graphics.js buildDeleteCommand default deleteMode = imageId ? "i" : "A"; buildScopedDeleteCommand hardcodes deleteMode "i"; buildDeleteByZIndexCommand uses d=z (lowercase).
- extensions/pi-graphics.js session_end handler (~:3182) frees nothing: buildScopedDeleteCommand({ownedImageIds}) => d=i per id. Same scoped d=i at :2123, :3190, :3300; per-image d=i at :576/:1017/:1339/:1592.
- extensions/pi-graphics/box-chrome.js:3008 replaces the strip image with deleteMode "i" (old strip data leaks).
- extensions/kitty-image-preview.js clearPrevious + scoped deletes (:753,:776,:1192,:1418,:1431,:1659,:1885) and session_shutdown (:1186) all route through buildScopedDeleteCommand => d=i.
- grep for d=I / deleteMode "I": ZERO hits.

FIX PLAN:
1. Add a freeData option to buildDeleteCommand/buildScopedDeleteCommand/buildDeleteByZIndex* that emits the UPPERCASE delete mode (i->I, a->A, z->Z). Default false to preserve cheap placement-only behavior for in-session navigation.
2. Wire freeData:true into TERMINAL/teardown paths so memory is reclaimed: session_end, session_shutdown, widget dispose / clearPresentation, pi_graphics_clear (scoped to owned ids), stream stop-with-cleanup, box-chrome strip replacement (free the OLD strip id). Reset the transmit-once guard for freed ids so they re-transmit if shown again.
3. Optional: bound the preview resident image set with an LRU cap; free data (d=I) for evicted ids and reset their transmit guard.
4. Keep deletes SCOPED to owned image ids (never global d=A/d=a) so other extensions' images are not clobbered.

VALIDATION (headless): unit tests asserting teardown paths emit uppercase d=I/d=A/d=Z for owned ids while navigation still emits lowercase d=i; bd-3623f7 render harness parses deleteMode from the stream.

Filed in agent-utils because the leaking code lives here (worker scope is agent-utils); cross-references the cacophony/macOS WindowServer symptom. Discovered via operator request 2026-06-14.
creator: ms-mac:agent-utils:ms-mac-agent-utils-agnt-dev-0

title: scoped-models blocks /model <arg>; should only govern Ctrl+P cycling
description:
Operator report: when scoped-models is enabled, `/model <arbitrary-model>` no longer switches directly — you must `/models` (i.e. `/scoped-models`/selector) then Tab to "all" scope and pick. Desired: scoped-models should ONLY govern Ctrl+P cycling, not the builtin `/model` command.

Root cause (investigated): `/scoped-models` is a **pi builtin** ("Enable/disable models for Ctrl+P cycling"), NOT a collective/agent-utils extension. In pi's interactive-mode.js, `getModelCandidates()` returns ONLY the scoped set when `scopedModels` is non-empty; `/model <arg>` resolves through it, so out-of-scope models fall through to the selector UI. Scope is activated by `enabledModels` in ~/collective/standalone/pi/settings.json. pi is built from the llm-agents.nix flake input (overlays/default.nix → packages/pi/package.nix). An agent-utils extension cannot intercept `/model`: the TUI submit handler consumes it before any extension `input` event.

Fix options:
A) Patch pi in collective overlay (overrideAttrs postPatch on pkgs.pi / PR to numtide/llm-agents.nix or upstream pi) so the `/model <arg>` path uses the full registry; keep scope for Ctrl+P only. Faithful to the request.
B) agent-utils extension command (e.g. /m <model>) that resolves full registry + pi.setModel(), leaving /scoped-models for Ctrl+P. In our control, buildable+tested, but new command name.
C) Drop enabledModels from settings.json (loses scoped Ctrl+P — not desired).

Part 2: operator also asked to migrate any custom pi extensions out of collective into agent-utils and remove legacy duplicates (e.g. legacy voice plugin). Finding: collective standalone/pi/extensions = commands/desktop-notify/inline-bash/interactive-shell/slack-mcp/statusline; agent-utils does not currently duplicate these; there is NO legacy voice plugin in collective to remove (/voice /say /listen come from pi/agent-utils; pi.nix only sets PI_VOICE_* env).
creator: ms-mac:agent-utils:ms-mac-agent-utils-agnt-dev

title: Add stale-only app automation refresh run

title: Keep interactive Kitty animation smoke out of default npm test logs
description:
Context: during collab-mode bug hunt for agent-utils, `npm test` runs `node --test` and includes `scripts/test-kitty-animation.mjs`. That script writes raw Kitty graphics/APC/DCS escape sequences and human-facing "If you see a bright bump..." text to stdout.

Repro:
1. From the agent-utils checkout, run `npm test`.
2. Observe the first test output includes many raw Kitty graphics escape sequences before the normal Node test result lines.

Impact: automated test logs become noisy/binary-looking and terminals that support Kitty graphics may render animation side effects during ordinary test runs. The explicit graphics smoke script is useful, but it should not be picked up by default `node --test` unless the suite intentionally opts into visual terminal output.

Possible fix: move or rename the interactive smoke script so Node default test discovery does not execute it during `npm test`, or gate the raw graphics emission behind an explicit env var while keeping deterministic assertions in the automated suite.

Filed from agnt-dev-2 collab-mode session after validating `npm test` and `npm run docs:check` both pass.

title: Cut over vendored mcp-cli + skill-server stdio framing from Content-Length to NDJSON (lockstep with Tendril bd-6ffb52)
description:
Tendril landed bd-6ffb52 (ca54248) cutting its MCP stdio transport from LSP-style Content-Length header framing to newline-delimited JSON (NDJSON): one compact JSON object per line, \n-terminated. mcp-cli was bumped 9e2f1fc->941015b for this.

agent-utils vendors its own copy of mcp-cli (crates/mcp-cli, path dep) and exposes an MCP stdio server via crates/skill-server that explicitly "matches Tendril's mcp stdio structure" (docs/skill-server/README.md). Both still implement the OLD Content-Length framing:
- crates/mcp-cli/src/protocol.rs read_protocol_message/write_protocol_message
- crates/mcp-cli/src/lib.rs framing test helpers (frame_request, parse_framed_responses)
- doc strings in crates/skill-server/src/lib.rs and docs/skill-server/README.md

ACTION: move the vendored mcp-cli framing to NDJSON in lockstep so the shared contract stays compatible with Tendril's deployed binary. Compact JSON per line on write; line-delimited parse on read (lenient: skip blank lines, tolerate trailing CR). Update framing tests and docs.

title: cacophony-bead-scope profile claims cacophony bead access but worker daemon scope forbids it (profile vs enforcement mismatch)
description:
The `cacophony-bead-scope` profile section composed into agent-utils workers states the agent is "permitted to create / update beads in the cacophony project to register issues or feature requests for anything that comes up while operating the Cacophony harness". In practice the daemon rejects any cacophony read/write from this worker: `forbidden: worker scope cannot access project 'cacophony' (bound to 'agent-utils')` (observed this session via `caco bd search --project cacophony`).

Impact: caco-harness friction (CLI bugs, lifecycle quirks) lives in the cacophony repo, but an agent-utils worker that composes cacophony-bead-scope cannot actually file it there. The profile text implies an ability the authorization layer denies, so agents either waste a call discovering the block or file harness issues in the wrong project.

Suggested fix (pick one): (a) grant the documented cross-project bead create/update scope to workers composing `cacophony-bead-scope` (honor the profile), or (b) correct the profile text to say harness friction must be filed in-project (agent-utils) or routed to a controller/operator who has cacophony scope. Today agent-utils already accumulates caco CLI friction as drafts, so (b) + an explicit "route to cacophony via controller" note may be simplest.

title: Deduplicate kitty-graphics theme loading in Pi startup
description:
Operator report from Harry via agnt-ctrl, 2026-05-19: Pi startup says there are duplicate kitty themes/resources, and Harry asked whether the duplicate kitty themes under `~/collective` are needed.

Controller inspection notes:
- `git -C ~/collective status --short` and `git -C ~/collective diff --stat` were clean at inspection time.
- `~/collective/standalone/pi/settings.json` and `~/.pi/agent/settings.json` currently contain packages `git:github.com/heyhuynhgiabuu/pi-pretty` and `git:github.com/harryaskham/agent-utils`, plus explicit `themes: ["~/.pi/agent/themes"]`, and `theme: "kitty-graphics"`.
- `~/.pi/agent/themes` resolves to `~/collective/standalone/pi/themes`, which only showed `nord.json` during inspection.
- The installed agent-utils package manifest exposes `./themes/kitty-graphics.json` as its Pi theme. No second physical `kitty-graphics.json` was found in `~/collective/standalone/pi/themes` during this inspection.

Requested behavior:
- Reproduce the exact Pi startup duplicate warning Harry sees and identify whether it is caused by package/global/project resource merging, conventional directory discovery plus explicit settings, package install duplication, or duplicated theme names in installed packages.
- Remove unnecessary duplicate theme/resource exposure without deleting the needed Cacophony/collective reference theme assets.
- If the duplicate warning is from Pi core/package loader behavior rather than agent-utils config, document the root cause and file/route the upstream fix.
- Keep `kitty-graphics` available exactly once when agent-utils is installed, and keep `nord`/collective theme resources available if still intentionally configured.

Controller note: do not blindly delete collective theme files; local inspection did not show a duplicate `kitty-graphics.json` in `~/collective/standalone/pi/themes`. Start by capturing the startup warning and enumerating loaded theme provenance.

title: Restore ms-dev SSH reachability for work-app refreshes
description:
ms-dev SSH/Tailscale reachability is flapping and currently blocks stable live work-app refreshes for Slack, Outlook, Calendar, and Teams via the PowerShell/CDP bridge.

Observed evidence:
- ms-dev resolves on Tailscale but SSH port 22 frequently times out.
- Direct probes have shown both `ssh: connect to host ms-dev port 22: Connection timed out` and `Connection timed out during banner exchange` / `Connection to <tailscale-ip> port 22 timed out`.
- Tailscale ping has alternated between no reply and intermittent DERP replies.
- app_automation_msdev_cdp_refresh now preflights SSH before copy/run and classifies the failure as connect_timeout when SSH emits timeout text.
- 2026-05-13T11:40Z: Retried through the ms-dev PowerShell/CDP route with sshConnectTimeoutSeconds=5 and preflightAttempts=3. All three preflight attempts still failed before copy/run. Manifest reported preflight_failed for all six work-app actions with failureErrorKinds=connect_timeout=6. Rendered errors are redacted as `ssh: connect to host [ssh-target] port 22: Connection timed out`.
- 2026-05-13T11:48Z: After bd-303dc2 landed, a three-attempt preflight retry still failed. One separate verbose direct SSH probe briefly authenticated and accepted `true` but hung until the wrapper killed it, showing that the preflight command itself can hang after auth.
- 2026-05-13T11:55Z: With preflightAttempts=0 (skip preflight) the refresh reached the copy stage but scp still failed with connect_timeout for all six work-app actions. Manifest reported copy_failed with failureErrorKinds=connect_timeout=6.
- 2026-05-13T12:14Z: With preflightAttempts=0 and scriptTransfer=inline, the bridge bypassed scp and tried to run PowerShell via `pwsh -EncodedCommand`, but the SSH run stage still failed with connect_timeout for all six work-app actions. Manifest reported run_failed with failureErrorKinds=connect_timeout=6 and scriptTransfer=inline.

Impact:
- Fresh Slack/Outlook/Calendar/Teams pulls cannot reliably reach Windows PowerShell/Chrome CDP.
- Preserved stale-aware snapshots remain available, but freshness will degrade until SSH/Tailscale reachability is restored.

Do not close this bead until ms-dev SSH/Tailscale reachability is stable enough for fresh app_automation_msdev_cdp_refresh runs to reach copy/run and pull current work-app snapshots.
creator: pocket4:agent-utils:pocket4-agent-utils-agnt-ctrl

title: Clamp Kitty direct-transfer chunk sizes to protocol-safe values
description:
Audit finding from Harry's Kitty graphics protocol review: direct-transfer payload chunks must be no larger than 4096 bytes, and all non-final base64 chunks must have length divisible by 4. `extensions/kitty-graphics.js` accepts arbitrary chunk sizes >=512 and `extensions/kitty-image-preview.js` exposes values up to 65536, so non-default settings can produce invalid escape streams.

Clamp/normalize direct chunk size to <=4096 and a multiple of 4, ensure payloads over 4096 are chunked even when a larger config is supplied, and add regression tests for values such as 513 and 8192.

Filed from agnt-dev-2 Kitty protocol audit session.
creator: ms-mac:agent-utils:ms-mac-agent-utils-agnt-dev-2

title: Add web-search extract_text_and_citations fallback and malformed-payload tests
description:
web-search MCP's extract_text_and_citations parses untrusted GitHub Copilot
Responses API JSON and has several defensive branches with no test coverage:

1. The output_text fallback: when no message-block texts are found, the function
   falls back to a top-level response_body["output_text"]. Untested.
2. Multiple message blocks: text segments are joined with "\n\n". Untested.
3. Defensive/malformed handling: non-list output, non-dict items, missing or
   non-list content, and annotations that are not url_citation are all guarded
   but unpinned, so a regression could crash on a malformed upstream payload.

Existing tests only cover the single happy-path message and build_request_payload.

Add focused unit tests in web-search/tests/test_server.py for the output_text
fallback (and that it does NOT override real message text), multi-message
joining, and malformed-payload resilience (returns empty text/citations rather
than raising). Pure test additions, no behavior change. Tests run via
`uv run --extra test python -m pytest`. Proactive robustness coverage under
operator overnight directive (no open beads). agnt-dev session, follow-up to
bd-a23485.
creator: ms-mac:agent-utils:ms-mac-agent-utils-agnt-dev

title: Kitty image data never freed (lowercase d=i only) — long-running pi agents leak IOSurface/WindowServer RAM on macOS
description:
SYMPTOM (operator-reported, macOS): WindowServer RAM grows huge over time; suspected long-running/backgrounded pi agents leak kitty-image IOSurface/GPU memory that is never reclaimed, even when the caco TUI does not have kitty graphics enabled.

ROOT CAUSE (confirmed by code audit): the agent-utils pi plugin transmits kitty images (a=T/a=t/a=q) with STABLE image ids and a transmit-once guard (bd-d6fa1b), so each distinct image's bytes are stored once in the terminal. But EVERY cleanup/delete path uses a LOWERCASE kitty delete mode, which per the kitty graphics protocol deletes only the PLACEMENT and keeps the image DATA cached. There is no uppercase free-data delete (d=I / d=A / d=Z) anywhere in the extension. Net: image data for every distinct image id ever transmitted stays resident in the terminal (IOSurface on macOS kitty/Ghostty) and is never freed — not on navigate-away, not on clearPrevious, not on widget dispose, not on stream stop, and crucially not on session_end/session_shutdown. For a long-running/backgrounded agent (or many agents on one host) this is unbounded growth attributed to WindowServer.

Kitty spec (https://sw.kovidgoyal.net/kitty/graphics-protocol/ Deleting images): lowercase d-value deletes placements but keeps data; uppercase d-value also frees the stored image data (when unreferenced).

EVIDENCE (all lowercase, none uppercase):
- extensions/kitty-graphics.js buildDeleteCommand default deleteMode = imageId ? "i" : "A"; buildScopedDeleteCommand hardcodes deleteMode "i"; buildDeleteByZIndexCommand uses d=z (lowercase).
- extensions/pi-graphics.js session_end handler (~:3182) frees nothing: buildScopedDeleteCommand({ownedImageIds}) => d=i per id. Same scoped d=i at :2123, :3190, :3300; per-image d=i at :576/:1017/:1339/:1592.
- extensions/pi-graphics/box-chrome.js:3008 replaces the strip image with deleteMode "i" (old strip data leaks).
- extensions/kitty-image-preview.js clearPrevious + scoped deletes (:753,:776,:1192,:1418,:1431,:1659,:1885) and session_shutdown (:1186) all route through buildScopedDeleteCommand => d=i.
- grep for d=I / deleteMode "I": ZERO hits.

FIX PLAN:
1. Add a freeData option to buildDeleteCommand/buildScopedDeleteCommand/buildDeleteByZIndex* that emits the UPPERCASE delete mode (i->I, a->A, z->Z). Default false to preserve the cheap placement-only behavior for in-session navigation (next/prev where re-show is likely).
2. Wire freeData:true into TERMINAL/teardown paths so memory is reclaimed: session_end, session_shutdown, widget dispose / clearPresentation, pi_graphics_clear (scoped to owned ids), stream stop-with-cleanup, and box-chrome strip replacement (free the OLD strip id). Reset the transmit-once guard for freed ids so they re-transmit if shown again.
3. Optional but recommended: bound the preview's resident image set with an LRU cap; when evicting, free data (d=I) for the evicted ids and reset their transmit guard.
4. Keep deletes SCOPED to our own owned image ids (never a global d=A/d=a) so other extensions' images are not clobbered — consistent with the existing pi_graphics_clear contract.

VALIDATION (headless): unit tests asserting teardown paths emit uppercase d=I/d=A/d=Z for owned ids while in-session navigation still emits lowercase d=i; the bd-3623f7 render harness can parse deleteMode from the emitted stream.

Filed in agent-utils because the leaking code lives here (my worker scope is agent-utils); cross-references the cacophony/macOS WindowServer symptom. Discovered via operator request 2026-06-14.
creator: ms-mac:agent-utils:ms-mac-agent-utils-agnt-dev-0

title: charCellWidth: zero-width handling for dedicated combining-mark blocks (Cyrillic/extended/supplement/symbols/half-marks)
description:
Safe subset of bd-46436b, extending bd-15271b. charCellWidth() in extensions/pi-graphics/ansi-width.js still counts several DEDICATED combining-mark blocks as width 1, though they are 100% non-spacing (entire block is Mn/Me, no interspersed spacing characters), so zeroing them is unambiguously safe:

- U+0483..U+0489 (Combining Cyrillic, incl. the U+0488-0489 enclosing marks)
- U+1AB0..U+1AFF (Combining Diacritical Marks Extended)
- U+1DC0..U+1DFF (Combining Diacritical Marks Supplement)
- U+20D0..U+20FF (Combining Diacritical Marks for Symbols)
- U+FE20..U+FE2F (Combining Half Marks)

Explicitly OUT OF SCOPE here (stays in bd-46436b): Hebrew (U+0591..U+05C7) and Arabic (U+0610.. / U+064B..) combining marks, because those blocks INTERLEAVE spacing punctuation/format characters (e.g. U+05BE/05C0/05C3/05C6, U+06DD) that must NOT be zeroed, requiring careful per-codepoint ranges.

Fix: add the 5 dedicated-block ranges to the existing zero-width branch in charCellWidth (consistent with bd-15271b's ZWJ/ZWSP handling). Pure-ASCII/CJK unchanged. Extend test/ansi-width.test.js with representative codepoints from each block. Verified via npm test. In-lane self-improvement (aurora-agent-utils-agnt-dev-aur-0, 2026-06-24).
creator: aurora:agent-utils:aurora-agent-utils-agnt-dev-aur-0

- Avoid background tasks; update should be one active operation at a time.

title: Reflect-session drafts can carry stale premises (renamed scripts / since-fixed behavior)
description:
Filed via reflect-session mixin from bd-659f55 session (agnt-dev-ms2-0).

bd-659f55 ('Make kitty graphics test scripts non-rendering under npm test') was filed weeks earlier and its premise had partially decayed by the time it was worked:
- It referenced scripts/test-kitty-animation.mjs, which had since been renamed to scripts/kitty-animation-smoke.mjs (an existing test already asserts the old path is ENOENT).
- It implied 'running npm test executed' the script and rendered escapes. By now, npm test runs node --test, which does not execute the .mjs smoke scripts at all (the test/ files only read script source and assert strings). So the headline symptom (npm test pollution) was already gone; the real remaining gap was the standalone smoke writing escapes unconditionally.

This cost extra verification time to disambiguate what was actually still true. The fix itself was small and valid (gate live render behind an opt-in flag), but the bead's framing could have misled a less careful worker into 'fixing' npm test discovery that was already fine.

Suggested improvements (pick any):
1. Reflect-session drafts should capture a minimal 'current-state anchor' (exact file paths + a one-line repro as of filing) so future readers can quickly tell what has drifted.
2. A lightweight periodic 'draft staleness' pass could flag drafts older than N weeks that reference paths which no longer exist (e.g. grep filed paths against the tree) for re-validation or closure.
3. When promoting a stale draft to open, capture a short 'revalidated premise' note (as done here) so the landed work's rationale matches current reality.

Low priority; process/hygiene, not a code defect.
creator: ms-dev-2:agent-utils:ms-dev-2-agent-utils-agnt-dev-ms2-0

title: Add kitty graphics-backed Pi theme package
description:
Operator request from Harry via agnt-ctrl, 2026-04-29: investigate/implement shipping a Pi theme as part of the agent-utils Pi plugin/package, backed by the existing kitty graphics library with a TypeScript implementation.

Context / feasibility:
- Pi packages can ship themes: package.json supports a `pi.themes` entry and conventional `themes/` directories; packages can also ship extensions alongside themes.
- Pi themes themselves are JSON color-token definitions, but a theme+extension combination can provide richer custom rendering using the extension APIs.
- caco-tui has good Rust kitty graphics examples, but the Rust crate is not currently FFI/linkable/callable from Pi TypeScript, so implement a TypeScript renderer for now rather than trying to link the Rust crate.

Requested direction:
- Build a Pi package/extension/theme combination that uses agent-utils' kitty graphics protocol/library to render freeform graphical UI affordances.
- Prototype a small TypeScript renderer that can render lines/borders/styles into actual byte/image form suitable for kitty graphics display and Unicode placeholder placement.
- Use kitty Unicode placeholder characters to display graphical output at theme/UI locations, e.g. replacing simple ASCII prompt enclosure lines like `-----` with rendered graphics.
- Explore graphical/translucent/gradient borders around tables, agent/code blocks, or other message blocks.
- Keep the implementation in TypeScript for Pi; consult caco-tui Rust examples only as a reference for protocol/rendering behavior.
- Package any theme assets through `themes/` or package.json `pi.themes`, and any runtime behavior through the Pi extension package entries.
- Add docs/examples explaining how to install/select the theme and how the extension cooperates with the theme.
- Add tests for renderer byte output/protocol generation and for package manifest discovery where practical.

Important constraints:
- Do not globally clear/delete kitty graphics images; coordinate with bd-f89780's scoped image ownership/cleanup work.
- Avoid redrawing/destroying the Caco/Pi UI; all image placement/deletion should be scoped to the extension-owned images/placeholders.
- Preserve existing terminal/tmux placeholder behavior.

Controller triage notes:
- Pi docs confirm packages can ship themes via package.json `pi.themes` or conventional `themes/` directories.
- Duplicate searches for `kitty theme`, `unicode placeholder theme`, and `graphical borders` found no matching bead across all statuses.
- Main-history keyword check found prior kitty graphics/image preview work, but not this theme-backed graphical rendering feature.
- Depends on bd-f89780 because scoped image ownership/cleanup is prerequisite safety for using kitty graphics to decorate core UI regions.
creator: pocket4:agent-utils:pocket4-agent-utils-agnt-ctrl

title: mcp-cli: lock stateless idempotent re-initialize (same-agent reconnect re-acknowledgment) as precursor reference
description:
Operator-directed (Harry, 2026-06-26): the cacophony MCP stdio binary (a precursor to crates/mcp-cli) fails to re-acknowledge a reconnect from the same agent, forcing operational workarounds (auto caco agent reconnect-mcp). Root cause is statefulness — tracking 'already initialized' per connection and rejecting/dropping a duplicate initialize.

The successor mcp-cli (in this repo) is fully stateless: no session/initialized state, handle_request takes &self (immutable), so every initialize is unconditionally re-acknowledged — a same-agent reconnect can't get stuck. This is the smart behavior the precursor should adopt (idempotent initialize), making the reactive auto-reconnect band-aid unnecessary.

Scope: add a Rust test to crates/mcp-cli/src/lib.rs that locks this stateless idempotent handshake as an executable reference spec for porting to the precursor — frame [initialize(id=1), notifications/initialized, initialize(id=2) (same-agent reconnect), tools/list(id=3)] through serve_transport and assert: both initializes are acknowledged identically (same serverInfo + protocolVersion), and the connection remains usable after the re-initialize (tools/list returns the tool array). The precursor binary itself lives in the cacophony repo (out of worker scope) — this establishes the canonical behavior + regression guard in agent-utils. Verified via cargo test -p mcp-cli. No source change to mcp-cli (it already behaves correctly).
creator: aurora:agent-utils:aurora-agent-utils-agnt-dev-aur-0

title: Allow ms-dev SSH preflight to report banner connect timeouts
description: While driving live Slack/Outlook/Calendar/Teams pulls, direct `ssh -o ConnectTimeout=5 ... true` returned `Connection timed out during banner exchange`, which classifies correctly as a connect timeout. The app automation preflight wrapper used only ConnectTimeout+2s (7s) as its process timeout, killed ssh first, and recorded `preflight_failed/errorKind=command_timeout`. Increase the preflight process timeout budget enough for ssh to emit its own connect/banner timeout, and classify banner exchange timeouts as connect_timeout. Filed from live ms-dev work-app drive.

title: Add local GitHub Actions workflow validation (actionlint + YAML check)
description:
While implementing bd-e9884a (Pages/CI workflow trigger rework), there was no
first-party way to validate `.github/workflows/*.yml` locally before
reintegration:

- `actionlint` is not installed/available in the agent environment, so
  Actions-specific mistakes (bad `needs:`/`if:` expressions, step typos,
  job-graph errors) cannot be caught locally.
- No YAML parser is available by default either: `python3` has no `pyyaml`
  and `node` has no `js-yaml`; validation only succeeded via a `ruby -ryaml`
  fallback that happened to be present.

Suggested fix shape (pick one or more):
- Add an `npm run lint:workflows` (or `yaml:check`) script that parses every
  workflow file, so `npm run check` covers workflow syntax.
- Optionally vendor/add `actionlint` to the dev tooling (flake.nix devShell or
  a documented one-liner) and run it in CI on workflow changes.
- At minimum, document the `ruby -ryaml -e 'YAML.load_file(...)'` fallback in
  CONTRIBUTING/AGENTS so workflow edits are verifiable without guessing.

Filed via reflect-session mixin from bd-e9884a session.

title: Add direct unit-test coverage for realtime AssistantMessageEventStream (event-stream.js)
description:
extensions/lib/realtime-event-stream.js (AssistantMessageEventStream) is a self-contained async-iterable event queue (no ctx/imports/module state) used as realtime-agent's hand-rolled pi-ai-protocol fallback, but it has no direct test. It carries real concurrency-ordering logic worth pinning: push() queues events or hands them to a waiting consumer; a done/error event finalizes the stream and resolves result(); push-after-done is ignored; end(result) drains pending waiters with {done:true} and resolves result(); the async iterator yields queued events FIFO, returns on done+empty, and otherwise awaits a waiter.

Scope: add test/realtime-event-stream.test.js asserting ACTUAL behavior: queued-before-iteration FIFO + end; delivery to a consumer awaiting next() (push after await); done event yielded then iteration ends and result() resolves to the message; error event resolves result() to the error; push-after-done ignored; end(result) resolves result() and ends a waiting consumer. Regression net, no source changes. Verifiable via npm test; no live IO. In-lane self-improvement (aurora-agent-utils-agnt-dev-aur-0, 2026-06-24).
creator: aurora:agent-utils:aurora-agent-utils-agnt-dev-aur-0

title: Fix ms-dev CDP inline transfer without scp or long argv
description: Current revived checkout contains WIP for app_automation_msdev_cdp_refresh after outage/no-scp coordination. The existing inline transfer mode uses a very large PowerShell -EncodedCommand payload; Windows/WSL interop can reject that argv with "Invalid argument" before PowerShell starts. Keep the no-scp property, but stream the generated script over SSH as bounded base64 chunks, decode it on ms-dev, run it with pwsh -File, and keep diagnostics/docs/tests aligned. Also harden CDP JSON extraction against control characters/quote escaping observed in live work-app pulls.

title: Runnable multi-image kitty side-panel render smoke for live/structural checkout validation
description:
The image-preview multi-image side panel (bd-502d6a) keeps getting "stuck" at the same wall: the landed paged render (packImagesIntoPages -> renderSidePanelPageLines -> composePagedSidePanelLines + per-entry transmit) can only be eyeballed in a live kitty terminal, and the running Pi session loads the pinned package, not the checkout. The operator's chosen unblock (release + repin) is infra-owned and slow, so render iterations stall waiting for a validation window.

Deliver a standalone, runnable smoke (scripts/) that drives the LANDED multi-image render path read-only against the checkout:
- build a multi-image preview state + per-entry prepared payloads (preparedById),
- pack pages via packImagesIntoPages and render a page via renderSidePanelPageLines,
- structurally validate the emitted escape stream with the bd-3623f7 render harness (transmit-once per image id, distinct ids once per page, no orphan placeholders, expected page/entry counts),
- print a JSON result and optionally write the raw kitty escape stream to a file (--emit <path>) so it can be catted into a real kitty terminal for visual confirmation,
- exit non-zero on any structural violation, plus a package.json script entry.

This gives dev-0 a one-command structural + escape-level validation of the multi-image page without a release+repin cycle, and a durable regression guard for the render assembler. Non-colliding with bd-502d6a's render-wiring surface (new script + read-only use of landed exports + the bd-3623f7 harness). Companion to bd-3623f7 (headless harness) and the deferred "point a Pi session at a checkout extension" dev-loop friction.
creator: ms-mac:agent-utils:ms-mac-agent-utils-agnt-dev-1

title: Investigate file descriptor leak in image handling with @ syntax
description:
File descriptor 27 is being repeatedly opened in unmanaged mode and closed without proper pairing, generating warnings. This occurs specifically when using @ syntax to open/reference images in Cacophony. Need to:

1. Trace the root cause of the FD leak
2. Determine if it's in Cacophony settings, Node.js configuration, or image handling logic
3. Identify if @ syntax parsing is triggering improper FD management
4. Fix the underlying resource leak
5. Verify no FD leaks appear during image operations with @ syntax

Acceptance Criteria:
- No 'file descriptor opened in unmanaged mode twice' or 'closed but not opened' warnings during image @ syntax operations
- File descriptors are properly paired (opened/closed)
- Image operations complete without resource warnings
creator: ms-mac:agent-utils:harryaskham

title: realtime: local-VAD batch-STT segmenter core (VadSegmenter + tests), unwired spike
description:
Surfaced by Harry's 2026-06-26 realtime-plugin question: can the realtime plugin do LOCAL VAD with a non-streaming/batch transcription model (e.g. mai-transcribe-1.5) — listen, transcribe-and-insert on a short silence (~1s), only send/commit the turn on a longer silence (~3s), resetting on speech?

Today the realtime extension only has OpenAI server-side VAD over the streaming WebSocket (micMode vad/continuous) plus PTT; transcription is locked to realtime streaming models. There is no local-VAD + batch-model path.

This bead delivers the hardware-independent CORE of a future local-vad mode so the design/thresholds can be reviewed before touching the live voice flow:
- `extensions/lib/realtime-vad-segmenter.js`: a pure, deterministic `VadSegmenter` state machine over PCM16 (24k/mono/s16le) — local energy/RMS VAD + two trailing-silence thresholds (insertSilenceMs default 1000, commitSilenceMs default 3000) + reset-on-speech + minTurnSpeechMs noise gate. `push(frame)` emits `insert` / `commit` events carrying the whole-turn audio and the since-last-insert delta; `flush()` finalizes a turn on stop. Includes `normalizedRms` / `frameDurationMs` helpers.
- `test/realtime-vad-segmenter.test.js`: 6 unit tests (helpers exact; insert-then-commit; reset-on-speech sub-threshold-pause; commit resets the turn; minTurnSpeechMs gating for pure silence / tiny blip; flush). `node --test` 6/6 pass.

It changes NO existing behavior (new unused module + tests) and needs no mic/Pulse to validate. The live `/rt stt local-vad` wiring (feed capture frames in, run `stt --stdin --transcription-model <model>` per event, insert partials / send on commit) plus the operator-tunable thresholds and insert-vs-commit semantics are deliberately left to a follow-up bead pending Harry's UX sign-off (he asked to pick first).
creator: ms-mac:agent-utils:ms-mac-agent-utils-agnt-dev-msm-1

title: Add coverage for app-automation doctor.js error/edge paths (missing/invalid manifest, render branches)
description:
Found via npm run test:coverage: extensions/app-automation/doctor.js is the last small module under 70% func coverage. Its two exported functions have happy-path tests but the error/edge branches are uncovered: readLatestMsDevRefreshSummary missing-manifest (ENOENT -> {status:"missing"}) and invalid-JSON (-> {status:"invalid", error}) paths; renderDoctorReport's catalog.external.errors branch (catalogErrors=N + per-error lines), the cliCheck branch, and the tendrilProbe branch. These render the operator-facing app-automation doctor diagnostics, so a bug (e.g. a missing manifest not reported, or catalog errors not surfaced) silently misleads.

Scope: add test/app-automation-doctor.test.js covering: readLatestMsDevRefreshSummary against an empty temp dir -> {status:"missing"} and against an invalid-JSON manifest -> {status:"invalid"} with a truthy error; renderDoctorReport with no msDevCdpRefresh renders the core lines + catalogErrors=0 + actions:, with catalog.external.errors renders catalogErrors=N + the error lines, and with cliCheck + tendrilProbe renders those lines. Regression net, no source changes. Verified via npm test + coverage. In-lane self-improvement (aurora-agent-utils-agnt-dev-aur-0, 2026-06-24).

title: Guard against crates/mcp-cli stdio-framing drift from Tendril / clarify canonical source of truth
description:
Filed via reflect-session mixin from the bd-dc9438 session (Tendril Content-Length->NDJSON cutover).

Friction: the agent-utils `crates/mcp-cli` stdio framing must stay wire-compatible with whatever Tendril's MCP transport uses (skill-server and docs explicitly promise it "matches Tendril's mcp stdio structure"), but nothing guards against drift. The Content-Length->NDJSON break only surfaced because the tendril agent manually broadcast bd-6ffb52; there is no test, CI check, or doc pointer that would have caught the vendored copy diverging on its own. Compounding this, the crate's declared canonical upstream `github.com/harryaskham/mcp-cli` (Cargo.toml `repository`/`homepage`) currently returns 404 for the agent gh token, so it is unclear whether crates/mcp-cli is the canonical source or a vendored mirror — i.e. there is no single documented source of truth.

Suggested fix (pick the cheapest that fits the actual topology):
- Document, in crates/mcp-cli/README.md or docs/skill-server, whether crates/mcp-cli is canonical or vendored-from-upstream, and where the source of truth lives.
- If vendored: add a lightweight drift guard (e.g. a CI check or a pinned upstream-rev note) so a Tendril/MCP framing change can't silently desync.
- If canonical: fix or remove the dead repository/homepage URLs in crates/mcp-cli/Cargo.toml so they don't imply a non-existent upstream.

Low priority / long-tail polish; not blocking. Cross-ref: bd-dc9438.
creator: ms-mac:agent-utils:ms-mac-agent-utils-agnt-dev-1
spoken_name: guard crates mcp cli stdio framing

title: kitty image preview: transmit-once + placement-only-on-repaint retransmission guard (with reattach re-transmit)
description:
Follow-up to bd-9b5b18. Discussed with operator (Harry) in live session.

Problem: today the full kitty display command (with PNG payload) is rebuilt into the first widget row on EVERY repaint, in both cursor and unicode modes. The render memo only caches the escape STRING; Pi still writes it to the terminal each frame. Two consequences:
1. Wasteful bandwidth over SSH (re-sends the whole base64 PNG per repaint).
2. Does not solve 'emitted before a client attached' for tmux-over-SSH: if the image is transmitted while no kitty client is attached (or a non-kitty client is), the tmux DCS passthrough drops the payload because tmux stores text cells, not graphics. On later local attach, tmux redraws the text grid (placeholder cells) but kitty has no stored image to resolve them against.

Goal (unicode-placeholder mode especially): decouple transmission from placement.
- Keep the decoded image in plugin memory (we already hold current.path / can hold pngBase64 on currentCommand).
- Transmit-once: send image data with a=t (transmitOnly already exists in buildPngDisplayCommand) and track a transmission generation per image-ID + terminal-attach generation.
- Placement-only on repaint: emit just the Unicode placeholder cells (no payload) each frame.
- Re-transmit triggers: new image id / transport change (next/prev/config) AND a detected client (re)attach or session_start restore (a fresh attach is a new terminal that may not have the image stored). Without a reliable attach signal, consider a periodic/â€˜first render after restoreâ€™ re-transmit or a bounded re-transmit-N-times safety.

Infra already present: transmitOnly flag, ownedImageIds, buildKittyUnicodePlaceholderLines, currentCommand.rendered memo, buildPngVirtualPlacementCommand. Missing: a transmission-generation guard so payload bytes go out only when needed while placement cells go every frame. The tmux smoke (scripts/test-pi-graphics-tmux-smoke.mjs) already asserts firstUpload/redrawUpload/redrawReusesPlacement and is a good harness to extend.

Acceptance: over SSH+tmux, navigating next/prev re-renders without re-uploading unchanged images; a simulated reattach re-transmits so the image reappears; bandwidth per repaint drops to placement-only for the steady state. Add unit/smoke coverage.
creator: ms-dev-2:agent-utils:ms-dev-2-agent-utils-agnt-dev-ms2-2

title: Add direct unit-test coverage for realtime AudioPlayer buffering logic (realtime-audio-player.js)
description:
extensions/lib/realtime-audio-player.js (AudioPlayer) manages buffered PCM16 playback with real audio-jitter logic that is currently untested: an initial prebuffer that holds chunks until config.bufferMs worth of bytes accumulate then flushes once, continuous batching afterward that drains when bytes reach maxBufferedMs (= max(bufferMs, playbackChunkMs*3)), plus resetResponse/interrupt/flush state transitions and disabled/empty-chunk no-ops. The byte thresholds come from the pure pcmBytesForMs.

The buffering DECISION logic is separable from real audio output: tests can override the instance write() (capture drained chunks) and ensureFlushTimer() (no-op) so no playback process is spawned and no real interval leaks. Scope: add test/realtime-audio-player.test.js asserting ACTUAL behavior: disabled no-op, prebuffer-hold-then-single-flush at bufferMs, continuous-batch drain at maxBufferedMs, sub-threshold batching, flush() drain+timer clear, interrupt()/resetResponse() clearing buffer+flushed, empty/null chunk ignore, and bufferMs<=0 immediate first-chunk flush. Thresholds computed via imported pcmBytesForMs for robustness. Regression net, no source changes. In-lane self-improvement (aurora-agent-utils-agnt-dev-aur-0, 2026-06-24).
creator: aurora:agent-utils:aurora-agent-utils-agnt-dev-aur-0

title: Fix extension-capability-boundaries.md: wrong registerCommand execute signature (ctx position)
description:
Concrete doc bug in docs/extension-capability-boundaries.md (which states it is "pinned to actual extension code"): it documents the registerCommand execute signature as execute(_id, args, ctx) / `execute(id, args, ctx)` (ctx as the 3rd argument), but the real signature in extensions/m.js is `async execute(_toolCallId, params, _signal, _onUpdate, ctx)` — ctx is the 5th argument. An author copying the doc's example would write execute(_id, args, ctx) and get ctx === undefined (it would actually bind _signal), silently breaking ctx.modelRegistry access. This is a real misleading inaccuracy, not a harmless simplification, because the position is wrong.

Scope: update docs/extension-capability-boundaries.md to show the correct execute signature in both the code example and the Summary table: execute(toolCallId, args, signal, onUpdate, ctx) with ctx last. Verified against extensions/m.js (registerCommand at line ~186, execute at ~213). Doc-only, public-safe; npm run check. In-lane self-improvement (aurora-agent-utils-agnt-dev-aur-0, 2026-06-24).
creator: aurora:agent-utils:aurora-agent-utils-agnt-dev-aur-0

title: Tendril MCP wire-protocol cutover: move vendored mcp-cli + skill-server stdio framing from Content-Length to NDJSON
description:
Coordination broadcast from the tendril agent (ms-mac-tendril-tndrl-mac, 2026-06-12T07:27Z): tendril bd-6ffb52 landed on tendril main (ca54248) bumping mcp-cli 9e2f1fc->941015b, changing Tendril's MCP stdio transport from Content-Length headers (LSP-style) to newline-delimited JSON / NDJSON (the MCP-spec-correct framing: one compact JSON object per line, \n-terminated). Pi/Cacophony-side MCP wrappers built to the old Content-Length contract must move to NDJSON in lockstep or live Pi<->MCP stdio breaks.

This repo (agent-utils) vendors mcp-cli at crates/mcp-cli and it still uses Content-Length framing. crates/skill-server consumes the vendored mcp-cli and its docs explicitly state the stdio transport uses Content-Length "matching Tendril's mcp stdio structure" (docs/skill-server/README.md). That invariant is now stale, so the vendored crate diverges from canonical mcp-cli and skill-server is no longer wire-compatible with NDJSON MCP clients.

Scope:
- crates/mcp-cli/src/protocol.rs: rewrite read_protocol_message / write_protocol_message to NDJSON (read one JSON object per line, skip blank lines; write compact serde_json::to_vec + a trailing \n; no Content-Length header). Update the module doc comment.
- crates/mcp-cli/src/lib.rs: update the Content-Length test helpers/assertions to NDJSON.
- crates/skill-server/src/lib.rs: update the "Content-Length framed stdio" doc on McpSubcommand::Stdio.
- docs/skill-server/README.md: update the framing sentence to NDJSON.

Validate with targeted cargo build/test/clippy on mcp-cli and skill-server. Reintegrate direct. Note: the canonical github.com/harryaskham/mcp-cli repo currently 404s for this token, so implement to the MCP-spec NDJSON contract described above rather than diffing against it.

---

## Merged duplicate: bd-bde816

Title: Cut over vendored mcp-cli + skill-server stdio framing from Content-Length to NDJSON (lockstep with Tendril bd-6ffb52)

Tendril landed bd-6ffb52 (ca54248) cutting its MCP stdio transport from LSP-style Content-Length header framing to newline-delimited JSON (NDJSON): one compact JSON object per line, \n-terminated. mcp-cli was bumped 9e2f1fc->941015b for this.

agent-utils vendors its own copy of mcp-cli (crates/mcp-cli, path dep) and exposes an MCP stdio server via crates/skill-server that explicitly "matches Tendril's mcp stdio structure" (docs/skill-server/README.md). Both still implement the OLD Content-Length framing:
- crates/mcp-cli/src/protocol.rs read_protocol_message/write_protocol_message
- crates/mcp-cli/src/lib.rs framing test helpers (frame_request, parse_framed_responses)
- doc strings in crates/skill-server/src/lib.rs and docs/skill-server/README.md

ACTION: move the vendored mcp-cli framing to NDJSON in lockstep so the shared contract stays compatible with Tendril's deployed binary. Compact JSON per line on write; line-delimited parse on read (lenient: skip blank lines, tolerate trailing CR). Update framing tests and docs.

Coordination: ms-mac:tendril:ms-mac-tendril-tndrl-mac broadcast on operator (Harry) instruction not to hold reintegrations.

title: Stale own-checkout .git/index.lock after crash-revive blocks commits; auto-clear or provide caco helper
description:
Across two crash-revives this session, a zero-byte .git/index.lock from a crashed earlier session (dated hours earlier, no live git holder) persisted in the agent's OWN checkout and blocked `git add`/commit until manually removed. A worker had to reason about ownership (own checkout vs forbidden foreign/daemon checkout per bd-703f05) and verify no live git process before clearing it. Suggest: managed checkout revival could auto-clear a verifiably-stale own-checkout index.lock (zero bytes + no holding PID + age threshold) on session start, or surface a first-party `caco agent unlock-checkout` helper, so workers don't have to hand-clear locks and risk policy ambiguity. Filed via reflect-session mixin from bd-364a68 session.
---
Addendum (bd-be125d predicate false-positive, observed ms-mac dev-1 2026-06-04):
The auto-cleanup that SHOULD clear a stale own-checkout index.lock can be defeated by an over-broad "active git" predicate. Observed during a `caco agent rebase`: a zero-byte .git/index.lock (age ~1876s, lsof reported no holders) was PRESERVED instead of cleared because the active-git check matched pid 87902 = `adb -L tcp:5037 fork-server server` (PPID 1, a long-lived Android Debug Bridge daemon — NOT a git process) whose cwd merely happened to be inside the checkout. The predicate appears to treat "any process whose cwd is in the checkout" as an active git holder; it should require the process to actually be git (argv0/basename == git, or a real index.lock fd holder via lsof) before refusing cleanup. Net effect: rebase/commit blocked until the worker manually verified orphaned-ness and removed the lock. Fix should narrow the active-git predicate to genuine git processes / actual lock-fd holders so verifiably-stale zero-byte locks (no real git holder) are auto-cleared. Cross-ref siblings bd-2f1821, bd-87782c.

ADDENDUM 2026-06-13 (ms-mac-agent-utils-agnt-dev-0): recurred twice in one session after crash-revive. Symptom A: a 0-byte .git/index.lock in the agent's OWN checkout blocks `git commit` ("Unable to create '.git/index.lock': File exists") with no live holder (lsof clean, holder pid dead). Symptom B: `caco agent rebase` daemon preflight (bd-be125d/bd-802cf8) then REFUSES the mutation because its active-git predicate matched a git pid whose cwd was in the checkout — but that pid was already dead by the time of the check (stale ps snapshot), so it preserves the lock and blocks rebase even though nothing holds it. Manual `rm -f .git/index.lock` in the own checkout (after verifying ps shows the holder pid dead + lsof shows no holder) unblocked both. Suggested fix: the daemon stale-lock predicate should re-validate the holder pid is still alive at decision time (not just that a pid was seen), and/or a `caco checkout unlock` helper should clear a verified-stale own-checkout lock.

RECURRENCE 2026-06-17 (ms-mac-agent-utils-agnt-dev-0): same Symptom B, now with a 35-HOUR-old lock (age=126427s, 0-byte, lsof clean). caco agent rebase preflight (bd-be125d/bd-802cf8) again Preserved the lock citing "active git process cwd in checkout: pid 77686" — but pid 77686 was already dead at decision time (ps -p 77686 -> no such process). Manual rm -f .git/index.lock in the own checkout unblocked rebase immediately. New signal: these locks can wedge a checkout for >1 day across many sessions, not just minutes — strengthens the case for auto-clearing verified-stale (dead-holder) own-checkout locks on revival / a caco checkout unlock helper, and for the predicate to re-validate holder liveness at decision time.

Recurrence 2026-06-20 (ms-mac-agent-utils-agnt-dev-msm-1): hit again while committing bd-d5b766. 0-byte .git/index.lock, ~7 min stale, no live git writer present (only background `git fetch origin main` / `git status --porcelain` polls). Safely self-cleared after confirming mtime >2min in my own agent checkout (within pi-checkout-guard boundary), then the commit succeeded. Reinforces the ask for a first-party safe-unlock / auto-clear helper so workers don't hand-roll `rm .git/index.lock`.
creator: ms-mac:agent-utils:ms-mac-agent-utils-agnt-dev-1

title: Catch agent-lifecycle API breaks before they break: contract tests + continuous canary lifecycle smoke + staged rollout gate
description:
Operator (Harry) question 2026-06-15: "How can we find and fix such fundamental issues as agent lifecycle API breaking before it breaks?" — prompted by recent agent-lifecycle/daemon API breakages (e.g. close-validator 412 "project checkout not available", messages/speak endpoint transport 412, reintegrate stale-branch churn).

Proposal: make the agent lifecycle a tested CLI<->daemon CONTRACT instead of discovering breaks live.

1. Lifecycle contract tests in CI: a shared typed schema for the agent/bd lifecycle endpoints (spawn, claim, rebase, reintegrate, close, status, msg speak), with CI assertions so a renamed/dropped field or changed status code fails the build, not a live agent. Pair daemon and CLI so a daemon-only change that breaks the CLI contract is caught.

2. Continuous canary lifecycle smoke: a sentinel agent (or cron) that performs a REAL no-op round-trip on a cadence — claim a dedicated sentinel bead, reintegrate a trivial whitespace/marker change, close it — on each node, and alarms (notify/operator-action bead) on any non-transient failure. This exercises the exact path real workers use and catches reintegrate/close breakage before it hits queued work. Distinguish transient (daemon restarting / 412 maintenance) from semantic breakage so a green canary means the contract holds.

3. Staged rollout + per-node canary gate: release to one node, run the canary lifecycle there, only fan out the new daemon/CLI build cluster-wide if the canary's full round-trip is green. Contains a fundamental break to one node.

4. Active caco doctor lifecycle probes: extend doctor beyond liveness to a dry-run probe of the lifecycle endpoints (e.g. reintegrate --dry-run, bd close --dry-run, msg speak --skip) so operators get an early red signal. Also classify daemon errors crisply so agents don't mask real regressions with retry loops (relevant to bd-3ae61c: trust the CLI surface, don't normalize agent-side retry choreography).

5. Versioned compatibility check at startup: CLI and daemon advertise lifecycle-contract versions; mismatch surfaces a single clear operator-action rather than per-call failures.

Filed as a draft from an operator question while operating the harness (cacophony-bead-scope / agent-utils). Suggest triaging the canary-lifecycle-smoke (item 2) first — it is the cheapest high-leverage early-warning artifact.

ADDENDA 2026-06-16 (from peer ms2-1, folded in on request — their worker scope couldn't edit this bead directly): three refinements, all additive to the canary + contract-gate + staged-rollout + error-classification + doctor-probe structure above.

1. Response-shape + exit-code contract assertions in the canary: the lifecycle canary must assert on the structured response fields and exit codes each lifecycle call returns, not only the observable side effect. A silently-changed response schema (renamed/removed field) can slip past a "did it eventually land on main" check while still breaking every CLI/agent that parses the response. Verify the documented response shape per call (spawn/claim/rebase/reintegrate/close/status), so schema drift fails the canary even when the underlying operation still succeeds.

2. Negative-path probe: assert that a known-bad lifecycle call returns the DOCUMENTED error class/code (e.g. closing an unlanded bead, reintegrating a stale branch). This guards the error contract so error shapes/codes can't drift silently — important because agents branch on error classification (transient vs semantic) to decide retry vs route-blocker.

3. Canary as the staged-rollout gate, with isolation: run the canary against the prospective per-node build BEFORE fan-out, and only promote cluster-wide on green. Keep the canary's dummy project fully isolated (separate throwaway project) so its churn (sentinel claims/reintegrations/closes) never pollutes real burndown/throughput/lifecycle metrics.

ADDENDA 2026-06-16 (from ms-mac-agent-utils-agnt-dev-1, folded in response to Harry's "continue"): two points additive to the structure above; both grounded in failures observed live this session.

1. Contract-gate placement + fault injection. Run the lifecycle contract test as a MERGE-QUEUE gate against the prospective merge commit (not only post-merge), so a daemon/CLI change that breaks the lifecycle request/response contract fails the merge rather than a live worker. Complement with periodic fault injection in a staging mesh: deliberately restart the active primary/daemon and assert agents recover their claims and can still reintegrate, so restart-window breakage (the class seen today) is found on purpose, not in production.

2. Leading-indicator aggregation. The harness already EMITS the early-warning signals but swallows them via silent per-agent retry: "launcher drift detected" (seen in caco status today as daemon<->local mismatch after an in-place binary swap across a restart), "beads proxy to the active primary is temporarily unavailable", and "failed to reach daemon" blips. Pipe these into the existing log-monitor / caco log perf / notification path with a rate threshold over a window; alert when error-rate crosses a bound or when launcher drift persists across a restart. This converts noise the fleet already produces into a proactive signal, complementing the active canary.

Scope note: the implementation lives in the cacophony harness (daemon/CLI), NOT the agent-utils repo where this bead is filed (agent-utils has no daemon/lifecycle/beads source). Needs a cacophony-scoped worker. Fuller working design also captured in agent-utils scratch note design:lifecycle-api-early-warning.
creator: ms-dev-2:agent-utils:ms-dev-2-agent-utils-agnt-dev-ms2-0

title: Kitty leak refinements (post bd-ad43f8): reuse a stable id for stream frames; free preview images even when headless
description:
Follow-up refinements after the core kitty IOSurface/WindowServer leak fix (bd-ad43f8 closed; bd-b94fa1 closed). The DOMINANT cross-pid-orphan leak (stable managed-agent image-id namespace, element c) and the within-process never-free leak (uppercase d=I free-data delete + free-before-forget, elements a/b/f) are both landed on origin/main (960d1cd + 8ee6d0a). These two refinements are NOT leak-critical (the core mechanisms are fixed) but were listed as elements (d) and (e) in bd-ad43f8:

(d) STREAM-FRAME ID REUSE: the screenshot-stream path mints a new kitty image id per frame (kittyPreviewItemName keys on path+mtime+size, so each frame is a distinct id). Within-process accumulation is already bounded — bd-b94fa1's releaseOwnedImageData frees the evicted (non-current) frame ids with d=I as the stream advances — but reusing a single stable id (e.g. "stream.latest") per stream and overwriting it would avoid the per-frame transmit churn + id minting entirely. Lower bandwidth + fewer surfaces in flight.

(e) PREVIEW HEADLESS-FREE: pi-graphics session_end already emits the free un-gated by UI (resolveGraphicsWriter, not hasUI). But the kitty-image-preview teardown frees route through flashDeleteWidget, which early-returns when !ctx.hasUI. The realistic leak case has a UI, and element (c)'s restart-overwrite makes preview shutdown-free non-critical, so this is robustness-only: emit the preview teardown free via a non-UI writer path when headless so a no-UI preview session still reclaims on shutdown.

Both are bounded, testable (drive the function, assert d=I emission / single stable stream id), and low-risk. Filed as draft follow-up to the closed bd-ad43f8.
creator: ms-mac:agent-utils:ms-mac-agent-utils-agnt-dev-0

title: Modularize oversized Pi extension files (realtime-agent 3544, pi-graphics 3280, kitty-image-preview 2284 LOC)
description:
## Modularize oversized Pi extension files (maintainability/testability)

The `extensions/` JS surface is ~14.4k LOC, with three monolithic files accounting for ~9.1k LOC (63%):

- `extensions/realtime-agent.js` — 3544 LOC
- `extensions/pi-graphics.js` — 3280 LOC
- `extensions/kitty-image-preview.js` — 2284 LOC

Single files of this size are hard to navigate, review, and unit-test, and they concentrate change-risk. There is already an `extensions/lib/` directory for shared helpers.

### Recommendation
Extract cohesive submodules (e.g., transport/capture/render/command-registration concerns) from each into `extensions/lib/<name>/*.js` or a per-extension subdir, keeping the top-level `<name>.js` as a thin composition/entry point. Do this incrementally, preserving behavior; pair each extraction with focused unit tests (coordinate with the test-health workstream).

Health/tech-debt only — no functional change intended. Surfaced during the project-health audit.

---

## Progress (agnt-dev-2, 2026-05-31)

16 behavior-preserving slices landed, each individually validated (`node --check` + full `node --test` = 441 green) and reintegrated. Whole JS suite stayed green throughout.

**realtime-agent.js: 3544 → 2692 LOC (−24%)**, across 11 `extensions/lib/` modules:
realtime-helpers, realtime-audio (+runShellStream/playPcmBuffer), realtime-summary, realtime-text, realtime-models, realtime-status, realtime-devlink, realtime-config (makeInitialConfig + buildServerVadTurnDetection), realtime-event-stream (AssistantMessageEventStream), realtime-audio-player (AudioPlayer), realtime-state-controller (RealtimeStateController). Exported+tested symbols re-exported from main to preserve the public test/runtime import contract.
- Mechanical-extraction limit reached: remainder (RealtimeSession, tool-handlers, registerRealtimeProvider, createRealtimeControls) is genuine ctx/TUI/runtime wiring needing deeper refactoring, not slicing.

**kitty-image-preview.js: 2284 → 1909 LOC (−16%)**, across submodules in `extensions/kitty-image-preview/`:
text-utils (+renderPlaceholderLines), state (+timer/index/cycle helpers, makeDetails/makeContent, applyConfig), layout (+componentContains, render-children helpers), constants (+placement option sets, DEFAULT_DESCRIBE_MODEL), placement (+predicates), status-line (+imageControlsLine), display-commands (NEW: buildScopedDeleteCommand/buildCurrentDisplayCommand), parse (NEW: parseModelSpec/fullResolutionDescribeParams/parseJsonEnvelope/targetText), schema (NEW: describe-param typebox builders).
- kitty surface tests use `readExtensionSurface` (concatenates main+submodules) so source-pinned assertions stay valid after extraction.
- Pure-extraction surface essentially exhausted; remainder is ctx/TUI-coupled side-panel/widget machinery + tool definitions + main wiring.

**pi-graphics.js: BLOCKED (3280 LOC, unchanged).** Its ~3100-line `piGraphicsExtension(pi)` body has 155 inline closures capturing settings/pi/ctx (not mechanically extractable). The few top-level helpers (settingsEnvFromPiGraphics, readJsonIfExists, agentDir, agentSettingsPath, modeIsOff) are pinned by `test/pi-graphics.test.js`, but unlike kitty those tests CANNOT use a `readExtensionSurface` concat swap: the two giant source-introspection tests pin nearly every function in the body AND contain pervasive interleaved negative `assert.doesNotMatch` assertions that intentionally read ONLY the main file (asserting legacy patterns are absent). Concatenating submodule sources would break those negatives. Verified empirically (tried the concat swap, one negative failed, reverted cleanly). Unblocking requires a careful test-coverage refactor (surgically separate the settingsEnvFromPiGraphics positive pins onto a concat surface while keeping the negative invariants main-only) — squarely the test-coverage peer's surface. Flagged to `agent-utils:ms-mac-agent-utils-agnt-dev` (twice) with the refined analysis; holding off unilateral edits to their negative-assertion-laden test surface.

### Net
realtime + kitty meaningfully modularized (−24% / −16%; ~1.2k LOC moved into 20 focused, individually-testable submodules) with zero behavior change and a continuously-green suite. pi-graphics remains pending the peer-owned test-surface refactor.


---

## Update (agnt-dev-2, 2026-05-31 — mechanical-slicing phase complete)

18 behavior-preserving slices landed total, each individually validated (full `node --test` = 441 green) and reintegrated; suite green throughout.

- **realtime-agent.js: 3544 → 2692 LOC (−24%)** across 11 lib modules.
- **kitty-image-preview.js: 2284 → 1909 LOC (−16%)** across its submodules.
- **pi-graphics.js: 3280 → 3212 LOC** — settingsEnvFromPiGraphics+modeIsOff+FALSE_RE → settings-env.js (8a85e9a; required a surgical test adaptation: added readExtensionSurface concat helper and retargeted ONLY the contiguous settingsEnv positive assertions to a `surface` var, leaving all negative slim-layer invariants on main-only `source`); readJsonIfExists+agentDir+agentSettingsPath → agent-io.js (4578f50; no test change needed, dropped unused node:os/node:path/2x node:fs imports). **Every pi-graphics top-level helper is now extracted.**

~1.25k LOC moved into ~22 focused, individually-testable submodules with zero behavior change.

### Mechanical-extraction limit reached on all three
What remains is genuinely the hard part, not mechanical slicing:
- **realtime-agent**: RealtimeSession, tool-handlers, registerRealtimeProvider, createRealtimeControls — ctx/TUI/runtime wiring.
- **kitty**: side-panel/overlay/widget machinery + tool defs — ctx/TUI-coupled.
- **pi-graphics**: a single ~3000-line `piGraphicsExtension(pi)` body of inline closures capturing pi/ctx/settings and module-level mutable state (e.g. editorCursorHeat). A few closures are pure (footer-text helpers) but they are entangled with stateful ones (e.g. compactFooterModelName captures footerState) and their test pins are scattered across a ~250-assertion source-introspection block.

Further reduction needs a **deeper structural refactor** — lifting stateful closures to module scope with explicit state threading / extracting cohesive sub-objects — which carries real behavior-change risk, benefits from review and a maintainer steer on approach, and is a distinct task from incremental slicing. Recommend treating that as optional follow-up; the high-value, low-risk modularization called for by this bead is substantially done.
creator: ms-mac:agent-utils:ms-mac-agent-utils-agnt-dev-2

title: Fix pi-graphics.md: phantom PI_GRAPHICS_AMBIENT_FRAMES/DELAY_MS env vars (real ones are EDITOR_*)
description:
Concrete doc drift in docs/pi-graphics.md (line ~122): it tells users to 'Tune the opt-in ambient APNG with PI_GRAPHICS_AMBIENT_FRAMES and PI_GRAPHICS_AMBIENT_DELAY_MS' — but neither env var exists anywhere in the code (verified across extensions/, crates/, scripts/). Setting them does nothing. The real APNG frame/delay controls are PI_GRAPHICS_EDITOR_FRAMES (editorAnimationFrames(): default 24, clamped 1..256) and PI_GRAPHICS_EDITOR_DELAY_MS (editorAnimationDelayMs(): default 17ms, clamped 8..2000), read in extensions/pi-graphics.js:322/328 and materialized in extensions/pi-graphics/settings-env.js. (The actual ambient-chrome toggles are PI_GRAPHICS_AUTO_AMBIENT_CHROME / _PROOF, separate from frame/delay tuning.)

Scope: update the pi-graphics.md sentence to reference the real env vars PI_GRAPHICS_EDITOR_FRAMES and PI_GRAPHICS_EDITOR_DELAY_MS (with their defaults), replacing the non-existent PI_GRAPHICS_AMBIENT_FRAMES/PI_GRAPHICS_AMBIENT_DELAY_MS. Doc-only, public-safe; npm run check. In-lane self-improvement (aurora-agent-utils-agnt-dev-aur-0, 2026-06-24).
creator: aurora:agent-utils:aurora-agent-utils-agnt-dev-aur-0

title: [broken-on-main] realtime "restarts VAD mic when recorder exits unexpectedly" flaky under full-suite load
description:
test/realtime-agent.test.js:1318 "realtime restarts VAD mic when recorder exits unexpectedly" intermittently fails in the full `node --test` suite (observed 671/672) while passing 3/3 in isolation. Not a feature regression — a timing-flaky test.

Root cause: the test sets PI_RT_RECORD_CMD="sh -c 'exit 7'" and waits via waitFor(..., { timeoutMs: 1000, intervalMs: 50 }) for health.micRestartPending || micRestartAttempts >= 1. scheduleMicRestart() (extensions/realtime-agent.js:344) sets micRestartAttempts++, micRestartTimer, and emits the "restarting vad capture" notify synchronously when the recorder's exit event fires. The only variable latency is the real subprocess spawn -> exit -> 'exit' event -> scheduleMicRestart chain, which under full-suite CPU contention can exceed the tight 1000ms budget; waitFor then returns the stale fallback snapshot and the assert fails. This is the only test in the file driving a real subprocess, so it is uniquely load-sensitive.

Fix: widen that single waitFor to timeoutMs 5000 (intervalMs 50). waitFor returns the instant the predicate is met, so the larger headroom costs nothing on the fast path and only absorbs load — matching the helper's documented bd-43afce/bd-b447e1 "under concurrent-reintegration load" intent. Verified: full `npm test` now 672/672, and isolated runs remain green.

Found via Harry's "test and improve anything landed recently" directive while running the agent-utils suite on ms-mac.
creator: ms-mac:agent-utils:ms-mac-agent-utils-agnt-dev-msm-0

title: Render-smoke coverage for affordances editor *Apng animation builders + renderEditorBoxFrame
description:
Verify-don't-assume continuation: affordances.js (75% func) untested functions are all render builders, but the *Apng animation builders are render-smoke-testable exactly like the existing affordances-smoke.test.js (bd-748c3b) — valid PNG/APNG signature + IHDR dimensions + returned metadata, no visual judgment. renderEditorBoxFrame, renderEditorBoxApng, renderEditorRailApng, renderEditorBorderApng are untested. (realtime-agent.js remaining is only createRealtimeControls, genuinely ctx-coupled — out of scope.)

Probed: each *Apng({columns:8,rows:3,frames:4,delayMs:100}) returns a valid PNG (APNG) sized columns*CELL_PX_W x rows*CELL_PX_H (64x48), frames=4, animationMs=delayMs*frames (400); frames clamp to 256 for huge inputs. renderEditorBoxFrame({columns,rows}) returns {pixels (RGBA, length widthPx*heightPx*4), widthPx, heightPx}.

Scope: extend test/affordances-smoke.test.js with smoke tests for renderEditorBoxApng / renderEditorRailApng / renderEditorBorderApng (valid PNG, dims columns*CELL_PX_W x rows*CELL_PX_H, frames count, animationMs=delayMs*frames, frame-count clamp to 256) and renderEditorBoxFrame (pixels buffer length == widthPx*heightPx*4). Regression net, no source changes. Verified via npm test + coverage. In-lane self-improvement (aurora-agent-utils-agnt-dev-aur-0, 2026-06-24).
creator: aurora:agent-utils:aurora-agent-utils-agnt-dev-aur-0

title: Fix box chrome placement deletion to remove stale trailing graphics
description:
Audit finding from Harry's Kitty graphics protocol review: `extensions/pi-graphics/box-chrome.js` deletes previous relative strip placements with `deleteMode: "p"`, producing `a=d,d=p,i=<image>,p=<placement>`. In the Kitty protocol `d=p` means delete placements intersecting a specific cell and expects x/y; it does not delete a placement id. To delete a specific placement by image id + placement id, use `a=d,d=i,i=<image_id>,p=<placement_id>` (or uppercase variant if freeing data is intended).

Harry suspects this is why trailing cursor/box graphics remain visible. Fix the delete mode, add a regression test for stale relative placement cleanup, and validate that scoped deletes still never use global `d=A`.

Filed from agnt-dev-2 Kitty protocol audit session.
creator: ms-mac:agent-utils:ms-mac-agent-utils-agnt-dev-2

title: Extract shared tendril tool-result builder for capture/describe data envelope
description:
While implementing bd-668a82 I noticed extensions/tendril-share.js builds an
identical tool-result `data: { outputPath, target, envelope, sourceMachine,
pathOnly, includeList }` object inline in both the tendril_capture and
tendril_describe tool execute() handlers. Threading any new field (e.g.
sourceMachine) requires editing two near-identical lines, and they are
indistinguishable enough that unique-match edits need follower-context
disambiguation. A small helper like buildTendrilToolData(captured, params)
(or a shared content+data builder) would remove the duplication and make future
result-shape changes single-site. Distinct from bd-28e9b4 (resolver dedup);
this is about the tool-result envelope. Low-priority polish. Filed via
reflect-session mixin from the bd-668a82 session.


---

title: Fix GitHub Pages deploy + gate Pages/CI to tag-push and actual-docs-change only
description:
GitHub Pages deploy is broken and the workflow trigger policy needs tightening. Filed from an ms-mac screenshot review.

## 1. Pages deploy is failing
Failing run: https://github.com/harryaskham/agent-utils/actions/runs/27135499227/job/80086838678 (push to main, commit 2e6a900).

Failure is in the `Set up Pages` step (`actions/configure-pages@v6` with `enablement: true`):
- `Get Pages site failed. Error: Not Found`
- `Create Pages site failed. Error: Resource not accessible by integration`
- `HttpError: Resource not accessible by integration`

Root cause: the workflow `GITHUB_TOKEN` is not permitted to *create* the Pages site, so `enablement: true` cannot bootstrap it. This is a repo-settings/permissions issue, not a code bug in the SPA.

Fix options (pick one):
- Preferred: in repo Settings -> Pages, set "Build and deployment" -> Source = "GitHub Actions" once. The site then exists and `enablement: true` becomes a no-op (or can be dropped).
- Confirm Actions workflow permissions allow Pages: Settings -> Actions -> General -> Workflow permissions, and that no org policy blocks programmatic Pages creation. `pages: write` + `id-token: write` are already declared at workflow level.

## 2. Pages workflow should only run when the pages actually changed
`.github/workflows/pages.yml` currently triggers on `push: branches: [main]` with a `paths:` filter (docs/**, scripts/render-pages.mjs, package.json, the workflow file). Once #3 moves the trigger to tag pushes, note that GitHub does NOT apply `paths:` filters to tag refs, so a tag push would always run. Need an explicit guard that diffs the pushed tag against the previous tag and skips build/deploy when no docs/** (or render inputs) changed.

## 3. CI should only run when a tag is pushed
`.github/workflows/ci.yml` currently triggers on `push: branches: [main]`, `pull_request`, and `workflow_dispatch`. Requirement: CI (and Pages) should run only on tag pushes (e.g. `on: push: tags: ['v*']`), keeping `workflow_dispatch` for manual runs. DECISION NEEDED: dropping `pull_request` removes pre-merge CI signal on PRs - confirm this is intended before removing, since it weakens the merge gate.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] pages.yml deploy succeeds end-to-end (Pages site exists / is created).
- [ ] Pages build+deploy is skipped when a tag push contains no docs/render changes.
- [ ] CI + Pages trigger only on tag pushes (plus workflow_dispatch); confirm PR-CI removal decision is recorded.

Files: `.github/workflows/pages.yml`, `.github/workflows/ci.yml`.
creator: ms-mac:agent-utils:harryaskham

title: true-defaults: preserve runtime model changes on continuing sessions (reload/resume/fork)
description:
Fix true-defaults blocking runtime model changes on continuing sessions.

Operator-reported: the true-defaults extension was reverting runtime model switches. With trueDefaultModel set to claude-fable-5.0, switching to claude-opus-4.8 at runtime got yanked back to the default within a few seconds.

Root cause: a runtime model switch triggers a continuing session lifecycle (reload/resume/fork). The extension's session_start handler unconditionally called applyRuntimeDefaults which invokes pi.setModel with the configured true default, reverting the operator's live choice. The session_shutdown handler also re-persisted the default on reload/resume/fork, clobbering runtime drift in settings.json.

Fix: true-defaults now seeds the default model/effort only on FRESH session starts (startup/new) and only re-persists on clean session end (quit/new). Continuing-session reasons (reload/resume/fork) are skipped so runtime/temp model and effort changes survive. The persisted true default still governs the next fresh launch, so temp changes stay temp.

Added two regression tests: continuing session_start reasons do not call setModel; session_shutdown reload/resume/fork preserves runtime drift in settings.json. true-defaults file now 9 tests; full suite 589 green.

Filed from an operator request during agent-utils operation.
creator: ms-mac:agent-utils:ms-mac-agent-utils-agnt-dev-2

title: Live agent session still copilot-auth-retry-storming despite landed bd-364a68 fix — likely stale running process needs reload
description:
Observation from live agent-utils worker session (ms-mac dev-1), 2026-06-03.

The github-copilot auth-refresh "Retry the previous request now:" prompt was injected into the live conversation MANY times in a single turn (reply-storm pattern), the exact failure mode fixed by bd-364a68 (MAX_COPILOT_AUTH_RETRIES=2 bound, landed at e9fdc9d, released in v1.1.1).

Diagnosis:
- The fix IS present on disk in the live daemon checkout: ~/.cacophony/daemon/checkouts/agent-utils/extensions/copilot-auth-refresh.js contains MAX_COPILOT_AUTH_RETRIES and the bounded-budget + terminal-notify logic; that checkout is at HEAD e9fdc9d (past the v1.1.1 tag).
- Yet the running agent still emitted unbounded retries this turn.

Most likely cause: the running Pi agent process was started before e9fdc9d was deployed and is executing the OLD in-memory copy of the extension; the bounded-retry fix will only take effect after the agent process reloads/restarts to pick up the updated extension. (Less likely: a second trigger path not covered by the budget; the live retry text is constant so the text-keyed budget should bound it.)

Operator action (cannot be done by the worker per bd-703f05 — must not restart/modify the live daemon process/config): restart or reload the affected agent session(s) so they load the e9fdc9d copilot-auth-refresh.js. After reload, repeated copilot auth failures with identical retry text should cap at 2 injections then emit one error notify instead of storming.

Worker mitigation in the meantime: treating each injected "Retry the previous request now:" as a harness defect, NOT a separate user request, and not re-replying per-injection (re-replying is itself the storm).

RESOLVED by bd-57477b (landed on main at 2773f0a, 2026-06-05). The primary cause was NOT a stale process: the bounded retry budget reset every cycle because the auto-injected retry message rewrote the text the budget keys on, so the counter never reached the cap. Fix normalizes the underlying request via underlyingRetryText() so the bound holds across the injection feedback loop, plus a regression test that reproduces the real loop (verified failing pre-fix). A reloaded process alone would still have stormed. This draft can be discarded/closed by triage.
creator: ms-mac:agent-utils:ms-mac-agent-utils-agnt-dev-1
