title: pi-graphics editor border still duplicates above native read-image: scroll-resilience (#1) + upstream Pi/pi-tui FD-28/29 + a=T scroll (#3) follow-up to bd-470314
description:
Follow-up to bd-470314 (closed), which landed only fix direction #2 (reserved high kitty image-id band for pi-graphics editor chrome + regression test).

REMAINING WORK:

(#1) Scroll-resilience of the editor border (agent-utils side, but VISUAL verification needs a live kitty/ghostty terminal):
When Pi's native read tool renders a PNG via a=T (direct cursor placement), the screen scrolls and our editor-border Unicode placeholder cells scroll up into scrollback, where they keep rendering the border image (placeholder cells render their image wherever they appear) -> 'border duplicated above the image' on every read. Candidate mitigations to prototype + verify live:
  - cycle the editor-border image id per repaint and delete the previous id (like editorCursorAnchorSeq) so scrolled-back placeholder cells reference a now-deleted id and render blank -- weigh against PNG re-upload/perf/flicker cost;
  - or scope the border to a real/relative placement anchored to the managed editor region (terminal repositions/clears on scroll) instead of Unicode virtual placeholders, without flipping the operator's default look;
  - or detect scroll and re-assert/repaint owned border placements.
ACCEPTANCE: reading an image with the native read tool no longer leaves a duplicated editor border above it (confirm on a live kitty/ghostty terminal).

(#3) Upstream Pi/pi-tui report (NOT fixable from agent-utils):
  - FD-28/29 'File descriptor opened/closed in unmanaged mode' warnings print just before native read images. The string is not present in agent-utils or Pi extension source; our extension writes only through Pi's managed writer (ctx.ui.write / ctx.ui.terminal.write). Originates in Pi/pi-tui core terminal/image tty handling (double-wrap of a tty fd).
  - pi-tui terminal-image.js allocateImageId() uses Math.floor(Math.random()*0xfffffffe)+1 (uniform 32-bit random). Our reserved band lowers but cannot eliminate aliasing; a coordinated reserved-range convention between Pi core and extensions would help. File as an upstream Pi issue.

CONTEXT: see landed commit 125baf0d on origin/main and extensions/pi-graphics/id-space.js band comment.

Triage diagnosis (aurora-agent-utils-agnt-dev-aur-0, 2026-06-24):

Code-path map for part #1 (editor-border scroll-resilience), from reading pi-graphics.js:
- buildEditorBorderRows() dispatches to three border render paths: buildJoinedUnicodeEditorBorderLine (unicode/topLeft), buildEditorRelativeBorderRow (relative placement), buildEditorBorderPlaceholderLines (legacy).
- The scroll-vulnerable part is the Unicode virtual-placeholder ANCHOR cell emitted by buildJoinedUnicodeEditorBorderLine via buildKittyUnicodePlaceholderLines({imageId: anchorImageId, ...}). On a=T native-read scroll, that placeholder cell scrolls into scrollback and keeps rendering its image -> 'border duplicated above the image'. The escalation strip itself is ALREADY a relative placement (editorBorderRelative[edge]) with id reuse + evictOwnedImage eviction (bd-b94fa1/bd-f4d277).
- So mitigation option 2 (relative placement) is PARTIALLY already implemented for the strip; the residual bug is specifically the Unicode-placeholder anchor cell. Option 1 (cycle anchor image id per repaint + delete previous, like editorCursorAnchorSeq) is the smallest change but trades against PNG re-upload/flicker on the operator's daily editor chrome.

Why NOT safe to ship blind from a non-live-terminal worker: choosing among options 1/2/3 is a flicker/perf/correctness tradeoff on the operator's default editor chrome, and ACCEPTANCE explicitly requires live kitty/ghostty VISUAL confirmation. A protocol-level test can assert 'previous anchor image id receives a delete (d=I) on repaint' but cannot confirm absence of flicker/perf regression. High blast radius + visual-judgment gate.

Recommendation: route to an ms-mac / ms-dev pi-graphics specialist on a live kitty/ghostty terminal. Labeled owning-checkout:agent-utils + lane:pi-graphics + needs-live-terminal; non-specialist / non-live-terminal idle workers (aurora) should SKIP rather than force-claim. Part #3 stays an upstream Pi/pi-tui report.
creator: ms-mac:agent-utils:ms-mac-agent-utils-agnt-dev-2

Both independently define parseModelSpec-style parsing, modelSupportsImage, agentSettingsPath/readAgentSettings, a github-copilot FALLBACK_DESCRIBE_MODELS chain, and the param->env->settings->default+fallback precedence loop (only the env var name and settings.json key namespace differ: TENDRIL_SHARE_DESCRIBE_MODEL/tendril.describeModel vs KITTY_IMAGE_PREVIEW_DESCRIBE_MODEL/kittyImagePreview.describeModel).

Suggested fix: extract a shared helper (e.g. extensions/lib/describe-model.js) parameterized by { envVar, settingsKeys, defaultModel, fallbacks } that both call sites consume, so the fallback chain, image-support check, and precedence semantics live in one place. title: Redraw pi-graphics footer background as a 1x1 unicode underlay (kill stray horizontal gradient)
description:
The footer segment background (ensureFooterSegmentBackground) rendered a left-to-right horizontal glow gradient (accent -> editorBg) as a relative placement anchored to each footer divider cell at PI_GRAPHICS_Z.BACKGROUND. On footer relayout the cached relative placements could detach and bleed onto the editor row or rails, appearing as a stray horizontal gradient strip even with box/editor-background features off.

Operator (Harry) directed a redesign:
- Draw the footer background as a single 1x1 unicode placeholder whose image is transmitted at full terminal width, so it spills under the whole footer line (no relative-placement shenanigans).
- The 1-cell LHS marker adds one column of padding; add one trailing space on the RHS so the line ends 'provider/model<space>'.
- Visual: like a mild 1-row editor TOP border -- a solid hline along the bottom edge with a gentle glow radiating upward (fading to alpha 0), milder than the editor border. For Nord: lightest dark base (editorBg) glow fading out, with a nord deep-blue (borderAccent) hline.

Implemented renderFooterUnderlay in affordances.js; replaced ensureFooterSegmentBackground with buildFooterUnderlayCell in pi-graphics.js; updated the footer line layout and source-pin tests.

title: Extract a shared evictOwnedImage helper (free data + drop caches) with a consistent imageId-equality guard
description:
Codebase friction observed during bd-15ea4f. The "genuine eviction" of a kitty image — free image data (buildDeleteCommand with freeData:true / d=I) THEN drop the upload cache and ownership tracking so memory is reclaimed and a future render re-uploads — is now duplicated across several sites in extensions/pi-graphics.js and extensions/pi-graphics/box-chrome.js:
- pi-graphics.js box-rail strip eviction (~1021): freeData:true + uploadedImages.delete(entry.imageId) + state.ownedImageIds?.delete?.(entry.imageId).
- pi-graphics.js footer-underlay eviction (~1602): same shape with footerUnderlayRelative.
- pi-graphics.js cursor-glow halo supersede (~1346, bd-15ea4f): same shape, but additionally guarded by `evictsImage = old.imageId !== newImageId` so it never frees a still-live shared image when only the placement id changed.
- box-chrome.js strip relative supersede (~3011): freeData:true + uploadedStrips.delete.

Two improvements:
1. Extract a single helper, e.g. evictOwnedImage({ imageId, placementId }), that emits the freeData delete and drops the upload-cache + ownedImageIds entries, so the pattern is defined once.
2. Apply the imageId-equality guard consistently. The bd-15ea4f cursor-halo path guards on old.imageId !== newImageId before freeing data; the strip (1021) and footer (1602) evictions free data unconditionally on `entry.imageId !== imageId || entry.placementId !== placementId`. In practice imageId+placementId change together for those strips (both keyed on width/palette), so it is currently safe, but a future key change that lets placementId vary independently of imageId could free a still-live image. The shared helper should take the guard so this cannot regress.

Add a unit test driving the helper that asserts: a placement-only change (same imageId) does NOT free image data, and an image-id change DOES free + drop caches. Low risk, tidy-up; P3.

Filed via reflect-session mixin from bd-15ea4f session.
creator: ms-dev-2:agent-utils:ms-dev-2-agent-utils-agnt-dev-ms2-0

title: Add coverage for kitty-image-preview state.js (applyConfig clamping/validation + advanceIndex + helpers)
description:
Found via npm run test:coverage: extensions/kitty-image-preview/state.js sits at ~74.5% line / 63% func in the full suite, with these pure state-helper functions untested (uncovered lines 98-100 104-108 112-115 119-122 128-132 135-136 141-157): keepTimerFromHoldingProcess, advanceIndex (index wrapping), stopAnimation, stopCycle, makeDetails, makeContent, and especially applyConfig — which validates/clamps 14 preview-config fields (columns 1-4096, rows/maxRows/minRows 1-200, zIndex i32 range, placementId/chunkSize bounds) plus boolean type guards and enum validation for placement/transferMode/passthrough/placementMode, and invalidates the memoized currentCommand. A bug in applyConfig means bad/unclamped config silently applied.

Scope: add test/kitty-image-preview-state.test.js with direct assertions: advanceIndex empty->false + forward/backward wrap; applyConfig clamps columns(->4096)/rows(->1)/zIndex, type-guards booleans, accepts valid enums (belowEditor) and ignores invalid (BOGUS), invalidates currentCommand, no-ops on null/non-object (currentCommand preserved); makeContent/makeDetails shape; stopAnimation/stopCycle clear timer+flag; keepTimerFromHoldingProcess calls unref and tolerates a non-timer. Regression net, no source changes. Verified via npm test + coverage. In-lane self-improvement (aurora-agent-utils-agnt-dev-aur-0, 2026-06-24).
creator: aurora:agent-utils:aurora-agent-utils-agnt-dev-aur-0

title: Reintegration isolated-integration-checkout fails with ENOENT when TMPDIR points at a vanished nix-shell temp dir
description:
Observed while reintegrating image-preview work (bd-502d6a) on ms-mac after the node had rebuilt: `caco agent reintegrate --mode direct` failed with:

  error: reintegration failed: direct reintegration rejected before publish: daemon error: create isolated integration checkout: No such file or directory (os error 2) at path "/var/folders/.../T/nix-shell.6xyxvp/caco-direct-integration-XXXX"

Root cause: the agent process inherited TMPDIR=/var/folders/.../T/nix-shell.6xyxvp from a nix-shell that no longer exists. The reintegration isolated-integration-checkout path (CLI-driven) and `node --test` (mkdtemp) both blindly use $TMPDIR and fail ENOENT when that dir is gone. Setting a fresh TMPDIR (mktemp -d /tmp/...) worked around both.

Impact: any managed agent whose long-lived process inherited a stale nix-shell TMPDIR after a rebuild/revive cannot reintegrate until TMPDIR is fixed — a silent wall on a core lifecycle path. Nodes were rebuilding around this window, so likely recurs fleet-wide.

Suggested fix: the reintegration isolated-checkout helper (and ideally other temp-using caco paths) should validate the resolved temp root exists/is writable and fall back to a known-good root (os tmpdir) or recreate it, instead of failing ENOENT; at minimum surface a clear hint to export a valid TMPDIR.

Filed via reflect-session mixin from the bd-502d6a session.
creator: ms-mac:agent-utils:ms-mac-agent-utils-agnt-dev-0

title: Defer Pi cursor relative placement until after anchor render
description: Harry reports the cursor glow remains at +5,+2: the 11x5 image top-left lands on the transparent Unicode placeholder anchor, so H=-5,V=-2 appears ignored. Defer the side-channel relative placement until after the current render turn so Kitty can resolve the virtual parent from the physical placeholder, while keeping raw APC out of editor text and preserving C=1/H=-5,V=-2.

title: Improve kitty graphics multiviewer navigation and scoped cleanup
description:
Operator request from Harry via agnt-ctrl, 2026-04-29: improve the Pi kitty graphics image display extension/multiviewer.

Requested behavior:
- Add controls/commands for the multiviewer to move through images explicitly: e.g. /kitty-show-next and /kitty-show-prev.
- Add a timed cycling mode: e.g. /kitty-start-cycle 5 to cycle images every 5 seconds and /kitty-stop-cycle to stop. If there is an existing command naming convention, follow it, but provide equivalent next/previous/start/stop-cycle behavior.
- Make the multiviewer UI expose these controls clearly enough for users.
- Fix image cleanup/deletion: the plugin appears to be killing all kitty graphics images in the entire terminal. When run inside caco this redraws/destroys the entire UI. The extension must only manage/delete the kitty images it created.

Implementation guidance:
- Track image IDs / placements / namespaces owned by this extension, and scope clear/delete operations to those owned resources only.
- Avoid global kitty graphics clear/delete sequences that affect unrelated images or the caco UI.
- Preserve existing tmux/placeholder placement behavior and current preview commands.
- Add/update tests and docs for navigation, cycle controls, and scoped cleanup behavior.
- Verify with the project’s normal test suite or targeted tests if the full suite is not practical.

Controller triage notes:
- Existing closed bead bd-74597a covers the original Pi kitty graphics extension, not these multiviewer/cycle/scoped-cleanup changes.
- Search/main-history check did not find an already-landed multiviewer cycle/scoped cleanup change on main before filing this bead.

title: Prevent pi-graphics from drawing outside terminal bounds
description:
Pi is crashing in otherwise healthy managed agents when agent-utils pi-graphics emits drawing/kitty-placeholder/chrome output outside the terminal width. This is a stability issue: graphics must be exactly clipped to current terminal bounds, because overwide drawing can kill Pi completely.

Operator direction 2026-05-31: be extremely sharp on keeping pi graphics work exactly within bounds; this is killing otherwise healthy agents.

Acceptance criteria:
- Every pi-graphics text/placeholder/chrome line emitted to the terminal is clipped or constrained to the current terminal width.
- Width accounting must use visible cell width, not JavaScript string length, and must treat ANSI/APC/DCS controls as zero-width.
- Kitty placeholder/chrome paths must avoid emitting extra visible cells past the terminal columns.
- Add regression tests for overwide input, narrow terminals, and graphics placeholders near the right edge.
- Validate with targeted pi-graphics tests and the full agent-utils test suite if feasible.
creator: pocket4:agent-utils:pocket4-agent-utils-agnt-ctrl

title: Eliminate redundant Kitty graphics redraws during animation ticks in tmux
description:
In tmux with Kitty graphics protocol enabled, animation tick-downs are causing excessive frame redraws and visual jumping. Currently, the system redraws Unicode characters and their associated graphics on every tick, even when nothing has moved.

Optimize the rendering pipeline so that:
1. Unicode character glyphs remain stable and are not redrawn
2. Already-uploaded Kitty graphics images retain their stable IDs
3. Only the placement/positioning of these stable IDs is updated via Kitty graphics placement commands
4. Full redraws only occur when characters or graphics actually move around the screen

Acceptance criteria:
- Animation ticks no longer trigger redundant character/glyph redraws
- Visual artifacts and jumping are eliminated
- Frame rendering performance improves
- Only positional updates are sent to tmux/Kitty during standard animation ticks
creator: ms-mac:cacophony:harryaskham
